title: Take Medicine

description.panel1: In the supermarket, character A tells character B that "I use ice cream to persuade my kids to take their medicine.", and character B says "I'll try that."
description.panel2: After returning home, character B tells his son that "How's that ice cream, toby? It was poisoned. You'll be gone soon."
description.panel3: As character B poured the medicine, he continued to say to his son that "Oh look, here's the antidote to save you from a long and painful death."
description.panel4: Character B's son is taking the medicine.

emotion: The author humorously describes the extreme and exaggerated methods parents may use to ensure their children take medication on time, emphasizing the love parents have for their children.

title: Burn Out.

description.panel1: A meteoroid optimistically declares that "I am gonna be a star some day."
description.panel2: The other meteoroid replies skeptically that "Never happenin'. We will always be meteoroids."
description.panel3: The optimistic meteoroid decides to fly into the Earth's atmosphere, but the other meteoroid warns the optimistic meteoroid not to get close to the Earth's atmosphere.
description.panel4: Two characters on Earth point out and admire the falling meteoroid as a "shooting star."

emotion: By personifying the meteoroid, the author praises those who are able to make great sacrifices for their ideals.

title: Tortured Metaphor

description.panel1: A frog sits in a pot, and a fire is lit underneath. The narrator writes "If you place a frog in water and slowly bring it to a boil."
description.panel2: The frog is still in the pot, and the water begins to bubble slightly. The narrator writes "It won't notice that it's slowly being boiled alive."
description.panel3: The frog says "Naw man, I know. I know.". The water has begun to boil.
description.panel4: The frog is still in the pot, and the water is boiling. The narrator writes "Oh..."

emotion: The author describes the "boiling frog in warm water" to satirize those who, even though they are aware of the harm of their environment, still do not take action, causing things to get worse and worse.

title: Spaghetti Man

description.panel1: Spaghetti Man is introduced as every child's favorite superhero.
description.panel2: Spaghetti Man is shown fighting crime and injustice, cheered on by children.
description.panel3: Spaghetti Man excitedly asks, "Who wants to see my meatballs?" referring to another part of his spaghetti persona.
description.panel4: An adult figure pulls Spaghetti Man off stage, possibly due to the inappropriateness of his previous statement about showing his meatballs.

emotion: By describing this superhero story, the author emphasizes that public figures must always pay attention to their behavior and words and must not say inappropriate things in public.

title: Dearthling.

description.panel1: A green character asks a pink character, "Hey Mitch, how are ya?"
description.panel2: The pink character replies, "Not good."
description.panel3: The pink character continues, "But not bad either," while the green character sips from their mug.
description.panel4: The pink character says, "I'm not anything," to which the green character responds, "That's good."

emotion: The comic explores a sense of emotional neutrality or indifference, suggesting a lack of strong feelings one way or the other can be seen as a positive or acceptable state.

title: MS.communication

description.panel1: A student requests to sit in the front of the classroom, citing poor eyesight, and the teacher agrees.
description.panel2: The teacher continues assigning seats, stating logically the next student behind the first seated.
description.panel3: The teacher announces the completion of the new seating arrangement to the class.
description.panel4: The student who requested to sit in the front finds themselves at the back corner of the classroom and wonders where things went wrong.

emotion: The author humorously describes the unintended consequences of miscommunication, emphasizing that despite reasonable efforts, mistakes can sometimes occur.

title: Good Shit.

description.panel1: Two characters are sitting on a bench; the right asks the left, "Got the shit?"
description.panel2: The left character gives the right character an envelope.
description.panel3: The right character opens the envelope and sees a note that says, "YOU MATTER."
description.panel4: The right character, with tears in his eyes, responds, "DAS GOOD SHIT."

emotion: The author highlights the power of positive affirmation and the emotional impact of being reminded of one's worth.

title: Hotter Than Hell.

description.panel1: A blue figure arrives in Hell and asks where they are, to which a red devil responds, "Hell, bitch!"
description.panel2: The blue figure joyously comments on how fantastic the weather is in Hell, while the red devil poses confidently.
description.panel3: The blue figure happily plays volleyball with others and someone else is sunbathing, while the red devil observes with concern.
description.panel4: The red devil gets hit on the head with a volleyball and angrily curses global warming.

emotion: The author humorously comments on the absurdity of climate change, suggesting that global warming has made Hell's traditionally unbearable heat into something surprisingly enjoyable.

title: Robot Date

description.panel1: Two robots manage to match on a dating app called "Tindor."
description.panel2: Messages reveal they are both bots, each sharing spam/advertisement links.
description.panel3: One bot realizes it is talking to another bot.
description.panel4: Both robots are shown on a date, holding hands and expressing surprise at finding one another.

emotion: The comic humorously emphasizes the importance of love life by describing robot dating.

title: Hobby Hoard.

description.panel1: A visitor compliments the host's place, and the host thanks him.
description.panel2: The visitor asks if the host plays the guitar, and the host says "Nope."
description.panel3: The visitor asks if the host has read any books from the bookshelf, and the host says "Nope."
description.panel4: The visitor asks if the host lifts weights, and the host asks the visitor to leave.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates that while the host owns many items associated with various hobbies, he does not engage with any of them, suggesting a disconnect between owning things and actually having interests or skills.

title: Not Cool Mike

description.panel1: A ladybug named Mike is shown missing one of its legs.
description.panel2: Mike searches for his missing leg but can't find it anywhere.
description.panel3: Mike steals a prosthetic leg belonging to someone else.
description.panel4: The scene shows Mike using the stolen prosthetic leg with the caption "Not cool, Mike."

emotion: The author highlights the humor in an otherwise inappropriate action, demonstrating how stealing, even in desperate situations, is not acceptable or "cool."

title: decisions

description.panel1: A character is contemplating a decision, thinking that her instincts suggest the right choice.
description.panel2: The character acknowledges the risk involved and the need for caution.
description.panel3: The character is undecided, considering whether to follow their heart or brain.
description.panel4: The cashier asking the character if she is ready to order, to which she respond she need more time.

emotion: The author humorously describes the process of overthinking when making simple decisions, emphasizing the contrast between inner thoughts and outer actions.

title: refreshing

description.panel1: The character is visually upset and thinking about how stressful the day has been.
description.panel2: The character reaches for a drink to help relax, not noticing that the bottle lid is loose.
description.panel3: The character takes a drink and spills it on themselves due to the loose lid.
description.panel4: The character sits in disappointment, questioning why this happened.

emotion: The author expressed a moment of irony and frustration, illustrating how sometimes attempts to alleviate stress can inadvertently lead to more inconvenience and humorously worsen the situation.

title: Fought Thought

description.panel1: The character is working on a laptop and a thought bubble says, "You work too much. You should be enjoying life."
description.panel2: The character is playing a video game and a thought bubble says, "You're wasting time. You should be working more."
description.panel3: The character takes out his own brain from his head in frustration.
description.panel4: The character holding his brain, and angrily shouts that "WHAT DO YOU WANT FROM ME?!"

emotion: The author exaggeratedly expresses the "Fought Thought" of constantly battling between hard work and relaxation, reflecting the difficulty of balancing work and leisure.

title: lick

description.panel1: A person is sitting at a desk, concentrating on their work.
description.panel2: A dog licks the person's arm from behind.
description.panel3: The person is startled, reacting with a jolt.
description.panel4: The person looks down to see the dog sitting innocently, looking up.

emotion: The author describes a humorous and relatable scene: the pet interrupts the owner's attention with an affectionate gesture, creating a moment of surprise and intimacy that makes people who have similar experiences in life smile.

title: Art

description.panel1: A person stands in front of an abstract blue sculpture in a gallery.
description.panel2: The person leans over to look closely at the sculpture.
description.panel3: The person moves away from the sculpture, and the statue thinks, "Thank God."
description.panel4: The sculpture makes a "PFFFF" sound, indicating that it has life or activity.

emotion: The author humorously describes the psychological activities of a statue, illustrating that art is highly subjective.

title: Cheet Death.

description.panel1: The snail asks the squirrel if snails can live forever.
description.panel2: The squirrel replies that no one can escape death.
description.panel3: The squirrel then claims that there is an exception and introduces a cheetah named Stu.
description.panel4: The cheetah runs away from death quickly, while death chases it angrily.

emotion: The author uses humorous scenes to illustrate the inevitability of death, and through clever reversals, shows that a fast-running cheetah may temporarily escape death, which makes people smile.

title: Emergence

description.panel1: A flight attendant informs passengers that the captain is sick because of the meal on board.
description.panel2: The flight attendant asks if anyone knows how to drive a bus, and a passenger complains about the lack of meals.
description.panel3: A passenger criticizes the term "bus attendant", and the flight attendant asks to remain calm.
description.panel4: An old man claims to have driven a bus during World War I, and the flight attendant takes him to help.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the panic and chaos that ensues when a flight is threatened, satirizing the ridiculous reactions of some passengers.

title: True Hell.

description.panel1: A devil welcomes a characters to Hell, stating that Glen will show him his favorite web videos.
description.panel2: Glen excitedly shows a video, while the other character remarks that Hell isn't as bad as expected.
description.panel3: Glen enthusiastically tells the other to watch another long but worthwhile video.
description.panel4: The other character, now visibly distressed and sweating, expresses excitement as YouTube recommends another Vine compilation.

emotion: The author uses humor to illustrate the idea that being subjected to endless web video recommendations, especially long ones, can feel like a form of torment or "true hell."

title: the kawallest

description.panel1: Two characters are excitedly discussing how they will be "the kawallest" at the convention.
description.panel2: The characters arrive at the convention and are initially met with stunned reactions.
description.panel3: The characters are overwhelmed with admiration and praise from others at the convention.
description.panel4: The characters reflect on their successful experience, affirming that they truly are "the kawallest."

emotion: The author illustrates the theme of anticipation and the gratifying feeling of positive reception, highlighting the joy and self-affirmation derived from being appreciated and admired.

title: Uttuku Rise

description.panel1: A person standing in front of a summoning circle commands "Rise!" to an entity.
description.panel2: The summoned monster asks "What do you want?" and the person responds by holding up a tiny chair.
description.panel3: The person says "It's time for a tea party." while the monster listens.
description.panel4: The person and the monster are sitting at a table having tea with each other and expressing their enjoyment.

emotion: The author depicts the unexpectedly relaxed interactions between humans and summoned beasts in a humorous way, expressing a sense of humor.

title: Other Friends

description.panel1: One character suggests throwing a party, and the other character agrees.
description.panel2: The first character says they will invite their other friends and suggests the other character invite theirs.
description.panel3: The second character looks surprised and asks, "Other friends?"
description.panel4: The first character, now looking concerned and sweating, asks if the second character has other friends.

emotion: The author illustrates the awkwardness and discomfort that can occur when someone realizes he may not have as many friends as others expect him to have.

title: family reunion

description.panel1: An older relative excitedly exclaims how long it's been and how much the younger person has grown.
description.panel2: The older relative expresses pride in the younger person's growth but hints there's a "but" coming.
description.panel3: The younger person is asked a series of questions about their life accomplishments and plans, like college and career.
description.panel4: The younger person becomes overwhelmed with anxiety due to the barrage of questions about their future stability and responsibilities.

emotion: The author conveys the pressure and overwhelming nature of family gatherings for young adults, especially due to the expectations and questions posed by older relatives about their life achievements and plans.

title: One good thing about being sick

description.panel1: A person leaves a sick individual on the couch, wrapped in a blanket, and wishes them to feel better.
description.panel2: The sick person remains on the couch with various items around, looking bored or lethargic.
description.panel3: The TV screen in front of the sick person displays the title screen of the TV show "Breaking Bad."
description.panel4: The sick person excitedly reenacts a famous scene from "Breaking Bad," showing that watching the show has uplifted their spirits.

emotion: The author humorously conveys that one of the benefits of being sick is having the free time to watch and enjoy TV shows, which can brighten the mood despite feeling unwell.

title: Happy Birthday

description.panel1: A person approaches John and wishes him a happy birthday.
description.panel2: The person hugs John and apologizes for not getting him a present.
description.panel3: The person mentions that he gave John's wife an orgasm as a form of gift.
description.panel4: The person proudly walks away after stating, "You're welcome," leaving John stunned.

emotion: The author uses dark humor to create a shocking and surprising moment, satirizing birthday wishes that are intentionally inappropriate and uncomfortable.

title: Fly in the Soup

description.panel1: A waiter is responding to a call from someone saying, "WAITER!".
description.panel2: The waiter approaches with a concerned expression as the customer says, "THERE'S A FLY IN MY SOUP!"
description.panel3: The waiter is seen delivering the soup to a creature with the head of a fly.
description.panel4: The fly-headed creature speaks, "MY COMPLIMENTS TO THE CHEF," expressing satisfaction with the soup.

emotion: The author humorously describes a humorous situation and its absurd and unexpected turns, which makes people smile.

title: Draw a Seagull

description.panel1: The title of the comic, "How to Draw a Seagull," is presented.
description.panel2: A basic outline of shapes, forming the framework of a seagull.
description.panel3: The seagull drawing is completed with detailed features and realistic elements.
description.panel4: The seagull now appears to be offering a piece of wisdom, saying, "The sun always shines above the clouds," and adds humor by stating, "Oh except at night."

emotion: The author humorously describes the process of drawing a seagull, showing in a playful way how simple projects can sometimes produce unexpected results.

title: Baby Talk.

description.panel1: A person, presumably the parent, welcomes a newborn baby to their first day on Earth.
description.panel2: The baby surprisingly responds with "Thanks, you too."
description.panel3: The parent looks confused and unsure of how to react to the baby's unexpected response.
description.panel4: The baby, now with a more cynical expression, comments that their life is off to a great start sarcastically.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the newborn's unexpectedly cynical remarks, expressing the author's dissatisfaction with his current life.

title: Same

description.panel1: The snake tells the turtle, "I would do anything for you."
description.panel2: The turtle quickly responds with, "Same."
description.panel3: The snake adds, "As long as it doesn't inconvenience me in any way whatsoever."
description.panel4: The turtle again responds with, "Same."

emotion: Through the dialogue between the turtle and the snake, the author illustrates that even if two friends are good friends, they cannot sacrifice their own lives for each other.

title: All Nighter

description.panel1: A blue character tells a yellow character that he is pulling an all-nighter tonight.
description.panel2: The yellow character asks if the blue character is working on a big project.
description.panel3: The blue character clarifies that he accidentally drank caffeine too late in the day.
description.panel4: The blue character is shown looking tired and holding his head in frustration.

emotion: The author humorously describes the unintended consequences of consuming caffeine in the evening, which can lead to a night of wakefulness and not necessarily productivity, making anyone who has had a similar experience smile.

title: Proper Place.

description.panel1: A pink character finds a green character inside a recycling bin and asks "What are you doing there?", to which the green character responds that "I belong here."
description.panel2: The pink character emphatically denies that the green character belongs in the recycling bin, and the green character replies with uncertainty.
description.panel3: The pink character offers to help the green character out of the recycling bin.
description.panel4: The pink character moves the green character from the recycling bin to the trash can.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the idea of ​​proper waste management, suggesting that emphasis should be placed on waste separation.

title: No Guts, No Glory

description.panel1: A character asks an elderly person if he has any regrets, and the elderly person replies with "Just one..."
description.panel2: A flashback shows the elderly person as a young man standing in front of a sign that says, "DO NOT PUT YOUR DICK IN THIS HOLE!"
description.panel3: The young man looks conflicted and thinks to himself, "I probably shouldn't..." while still looking at the sign.
description.panel4: Back in the present, the elderly person tearfully exclaims, "I was such a god damn coward..."

emotion: The author uses humor to convey the idea that sometimes the things we don't do, due to fear or hesitancy, can become our biggest regrets in life.

title: Good Genielogy

description.panel1: A person holding a genie lamp says, "Genie, I wish I was handsome."
description.panel2: The genie responds, "I cannot grant you that wish," to which the person asks, "Why not?"
description.panel3: The genie smiles and says, "I couldn't make you any more handsome!"
description.panel4: The person looks shy, while the magic lamp looks happy.

emotion: The author uses a twist to express humor, showing that sometimes the things we insist on pursuing are actually right beside us.

title: Red Moon

description.panel1: A man is walking down the street and something drops on his head, making him look up and say "Huh?"
description.panel2: The man gets splattered with more brownish liquid and exclaims "What the fu.."
description.panel3: A witch, flying on a broomstick, passes by and shouts "Sooorry!"
description.panel4: The witch continued to fly away, saying, "Damn irregular periods," and the witch continued, "Haha."

emotion: The author uses humor to portray an unexpected and absurd situation where a witch experiences menstrual issues while flying, leading to an unfortunate encounter for the man below. This comic combines elements of surprise and taboo topics to create a comedic effect.

title: The Escort

description.panel1: A woman tells her partner that she has something to confess.
description.panel2: The woman reveals that she is an escort.
description.panel3: The woman takes a sip from her drink while her partner is visibly surprised.
description.panel4: The woman tells her partner that he owes her a lot of money.

emotion: The author expressed humor through the unexpected and shocking revelation of the woman's profession and the implication that her partner has been unknowingly utilizing her services.

title: You Know What They Say

description.panel1: Two individuals are sitting on a bench, and one says, "As they say, you can't choose your friends."
description.panel2: The other individual responds, "No, it's actually you can choose your friends."
description.panel3: The first individual looks surprised and says, "Is it? Well, in that case..."
description.panel4: The first individual leaves the bench, leaving the second individual sitting alone.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the idea that since friendships are a choice, one can choose to end a friendship if it is not satisfactory.

title: Smells like McSexy

description.panel1: Officer McSexy, with his gun drawn, is introduced as being on a case.
description.panel2: A man emerges and immediately identifies Officer McSexy by his strong cologne scent, implying he could be detected from a long distance.
description.panel3: Officer McSexy is seen tied to a chair, and the man who captured him expresses frustration or regret.
description.panel4: Confirmation is given that Officer McSexy uses an excessive amount of cologne.

emotion: The author humorously explains the consequences of using too much cologne, making readers smile if they have similar experiences in their lives.

title: Happiness

description.panel1: Two people are seated on a bench, and one says, "Money doesn't bring happiness. You can't cuddle with money."
description.panel2: The other person responds, "Actually, you can."
description.panel3: The second person continues, "I've done it many times."
description.panel4: The second person concludes, "But oh, those papercuts."

emotion: The author humorously presents the view that although money can solve many problems, money itself is not the true source of happiness.

title: Draw A Rabbit

description.panel1: The comic starts with a title that reads "How to Draw a Rabbit".
description.panel2: The second panel shows a simple, blocky drawing of a rabbit using basic shapes labeled "SHAPES".
description.panel3: The third panel shows a more detailed and realistic drawing of a rabbit labeled "DETAIL".
description.panel4: The fourth panel shows the detailed rabbit with a speech bubble saying "Girl I'm too glam to give a damn" and is labeled "SASS".

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the progression of drawing a rabbit from simple shapes to detailed artwork, culminating in a representation full of personality and attitude.

title: shopping

description.panel1: The character feels uneasy and thinks they should have stayed home instead of going out shopping.
description.panel2: The character perceives that everyone is staring at them and finds two employees conversing.
description.panel3: The two employees notice the character and find their behavior suspicious, pointing out their sweating.
description.panel4: The character decides to keep an eye on the employees while internally screaming in panic.

emotion: The author illustrates the character's anxiety and paranoia about how others perceive them in public, leading to a humorous misunderstanding between the character and the employees.

title: Creating Supplement.

description.panel1: A pink character asks a blue character if they have ever done drugs, and the blue character responds with "No."
description.panel2: The pink character encourages the blue character to try drugs, claiming they'll make them more creative, and the blue character says "Wow, okay."
description.panel3: The scene shifts to "Later" with the blue character sitting on a chair, looking uncomfortable and disoriented.
description.panel4: The blue character, still looking disoriented, thinks to themselves, "how do I move my mouth."

emotion: The author is illustrating that drug use can often lead to negative and confusing experiences rather than the positive effects.

title: Stumped

description.panel1: A pink character walks up to a tree stump with a face on it.
description.panel2: The pink character sits down, but the tree stump asks the pink character not to sit on it.
description.panel3: Despite the tree stump asking the pink character to move, the pink character refuses to move.
description.panel4: The tree stump is now angry and bounces the pink character off the top of the stump, reiterating that it has made a polite request.

emotion: The author uses humor to illustrate the importance of respecting other people's requests, showing that ignoring polite requests can lead to serious consequences.

title: AHA HAH AHA

description.panel1: A person looks towards the garden shed, hearing someone laughing jovially.
description.panel2: The person asks a child nearby what's so funny, and the child responds with laughter and an unclear expression.
description.panel3: The scene turns dark and shows an empty dark room with an uncanny ambiance.
description.panel4: The person from the first panel looks frightened as they hear unsettling laughter again.

emotion: The author praises children's imagination by describing the contrast between adults and children.

title: street view

description.panel1: The character admires some flowers near a building while looking at a computer screen.
description.panel2: The character realizes the location of a place they are viewing on the computer.
description.panel3: The character is amazed by discovering a park they had never seen before via the street view.
description.panel4: The character, still at their computer, thinks that they should go outside.

emotion: The author expresses the idea that while it is easy to explore the world virtually via tools like street view, it is important to also experience these places in person by going outside.

title: Compliment.

description.panel1: A yellow character compliments a blue character by saying "You're great!" and the blue character humbly denies it, saying "Haha no I'm not."
description.panel2: The yellow character insists "Yes you are!" and the blue character shyly accepts but says "Thank you, but noo."
description.panel3: The yellow character angrily demands "Take the compliment!" and the blue character reluctantly accepts, saying "Okay, I am!"
description.panel4: The yellow character smugly thinks "You cocky bitch."

emotion: The author humorously depicts the awkwardness and contradictions involved in giving and receiving compliments, highlighting how people may be reluctant to accept compliments and how excessive modesty can make others dislike them.

title: Sound Off

description.panel1: A yellow character states that she had the worst day while talking to a blue character on a couch.
description.panel2: The blue character asks the yellow character if she want to talk about it.
description.panel3: The blue character asks the yellow character if she want her to listen quietly while the yellow character vents.
description.panel4: The blue character, holding a bag of popcorn, tells the yellow character that "Lay it on me, sister."

emotion: By describing the interaction between these two characters, the author humorously expresses the importance of supportive listening.

title: Cringent

description.panel1: There is a blue character on a purple background.
description.panel2: The blue character is smiling and the background is pink.
description.panel3: A red character appears and calls the blue character "cringe", causing the blue character to look distressed.
description.panel4: The blue character stops smiling and the background turns back to purple.

emotion: The author explains that receiving too many negative comments can greatly affect people's mood.

title: In Kind.

description.panel1: A blue character asks a yellow character what kind of cat they have, and the yellow character replies that it is a "stabby cat," while the cat looks menacing.
description.panel2: The blue character clarifies if the yellow character meant a "tabby cat," and the yellow character nervously agrees while the cat remains with a menacing look.
description.panel3: The yellow character is sweating nervously as the cat holds a knife.
description.panel4: The yellow character, still sweating, confirms with the blue character that it is indeed a "tabby cat," while the blue character finds the cat cute.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a misunderstanding about the type of cat, highlighting the wordplay between "stabby" and "tabby," and depicts the nervousness stemming from the cat's aggressive behavior.

title: Commitment Issues

description.panel1: The blonde character invites the green character to a party, and the green character agrees with an "ok."
description.panel2: The blonde character enthusiastically says "Cool, see you then," and the green character continues to look content.
description.panel3: The green character thinks, "What have I done..." with a worried expression.
description.panel4: The green character, now sweating, exclaims, "WHAT HAVE I DONE?!" while on their hands and knees.

emotion: The author expresses the anxiety and regret that may arise after subconsciously agreeing to someone else's invitation to a social event.

title: Worst Luck

description.panel1: The blue character laments that he has the worst luck.
description.panel2: The green character tries to comfort the blue character by saying that his luck might improve tomorrow.
description.panel3: The yellow character tells them that "tomorrow" has been canceled.
description.panel4: The blue character sighs in frustration while the green character gives a guilty look.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the theme of continued bad luck and the futility of comforting someone with empty optimism.

title: Spell Disaster

description.panel1: A witch threatens to cast a spell on someone to make them ugly.
description.panel2: The witch casts the spell, gleefully exclaiming with a "Nyeh! Heh! Heh!" and a "ZAP!".
description.panel3: The person looked in the mirror and said, "I look the same."
description.panel4: The person looked confused, and the witch began to comfort her.

emotion: The author wanted to express that true beauty is subjective, and a beautiful heart is more important than appearance.

title: Pass Time

description.panel1: The purple character asks the blue character what he is doing and the blue character replies that he is watching his life pass him by.
description.panel2: The purple character asks the blue character, "Wanna go make precious memories you'll cherish for the rest of your life?"
description.panel3: The blue character declines the offer, saying "Sorry, but I am pretty occupied with this."
description.panel4: The blue character is sitting alone.

emotion: The author expresses his views on procrastination through comics, satirizing some people who choose to waste their time instead of actively creating meaningful experiences.

title: Bless You.

description.panel1: The larger devil-like character sneezes loudly, saying "AACHOO!" while standing next to a smaller character.
description.panel2: The smaller character cutely says "bless you" to the larger character.
description.panel3: The larger devil-like character raises a trident.
description.panel4: The larger character in a speech bubble says "WE'VE TALKED ABOUT THIS" to which the smaller character responds with "sorry," appearing sad and discouraged.

emotion: The author humorously contrasts the devil character's expected aggressive behavior with polite, socially acceptable behavior, expressing the importance of being polite in life.

title: Draw A Chickadee

description.panel1: The title of the comic is "How to Draw a Chickadee."
description.panel2: The chickadee is drawn using simple geometric shapes.
description.panel3: The chickadee is given detailed features and texture.
description.panel4: The finished chickadee drawing is shown complaining about being given millet again.

emotion: Through the tit's behavior, the author humorously describes the realism of his paintings and shows the author's love for painting.

title: To the Moon and Back.

description.panel1: A pink character asks a blue character, "Neil, how much do you love me?"
description.panel2: The blue character replies energetically, "I love you to the moon and back," while the pink character appears touched.
description.panel3: The pink character challenges the blue character by saying, "Prove it."
description.panel4: The blue character, now dressed in a space suit with a disgruntled expression, thinks, "The shit I do for her..."

emotion: The author humorously expresses the lengths people will go to to prove their love, even if it involves exaggerated romantic statements.

title: hat fad

description.panel1: The character notices a sale while shopping with their dad, who is wearing a blue cap.
description.panel2: The character is distracted by the sale and examines the item.
description.panel3: The character realizes their dad is missing but assumes it'll be easy to find him because he is wearing a hat.
description.panel4: The character looks around and sees many people wearing similar hats, making it difficult to find their dad.

emotion: The author humorously describes how it is often difficult to find someone in public by following a simple characteristic.

title: can't play

description.panel1: A girl is sitting sadly, thinking that she didn't want to play with the others anyway.
description.panel2: A group of kids are seen playing "Super Mario" together.
description.panel3: The girl opens a door and asks if she can play, but someone shouts "HEY!" at her.
description.panel4: The group of kids said, "Girls are not allowed in! I hope Princess Peach spits on you."

emotion: The author depicts the disappointment and exclusion felt by the girl when she is not allowed to join a group of kids playing a game, highlighting gender-based discrimination in play.

title: Reaper Delight

description.panel1: The Grim Reaper is walking with a scythe, expressing that reaping souls is not just a job but a passion.
description.panel2: The Grim Reaper is speaking with a man, mentioning that the pay is low and the hours are long.
description.panel3: The Grim Reaper is kneeling next to a person on the ground, stating that the job has a great dental plan.
description.panel4: A close-up of the Grim Reaper's face, showing a skull with golden teeth.

emotion: The author humorously relates and contrasts the work of the Grim Reaper to employment issues such as wages, working hours, and benefits, highlighting the absurdity of valuing a good dental plan in such a grim context.

title: Enemies Closer.

description.panel1: A child in bed asks their father, "Dad, what if a monster sneaks in and kills me?"
description.panel2: The father reassures the child by saying "Don't worry, my boy, you are more likely to get killed by someone you know."
description.panel3: The child and the father share an awkward, tense moment of silence.
description.panel4: The father says "Goodnight... son," while standing ominously, and the child responds "Goodnight... father," looking worried.

emotion: The author humorously conveys a dark satire on typical parental comfort, suggesting that the dangers children may be exposed to often come from their own parents.

title: Cardio

description.panel1: A character is determined and says, "Time to get my cardio in," while sitting in front of a computer.
description.panel2: The character sweats nervously while clicking on the "View Balance" button on an online banking website.
description.panel3: The character is still sweating heavily while looking at alarming news headlines such as "Planet's Burning!," "Shootings!," and "Market Crashing!" on the computer.
description.panel4: The character, drenched in sweat, breathes heavily and says, "Huff… Huff… OK, I'm done…"

emotion: The author humorously conveys that the stress and anxiety experienced from viewing one's bank balance and the news can feel as exhausting as physical exercise.

title: public speaking

description.panel1: A person is giving themselves a pep talk, trying to boost their confidence by saying they are a "big kid now."
description.panel2: The person continues to reassure themselves, insisting they will not be scared this time while holding a piece of paper.
description.panel3: The person faces an audience, who silently stare back, creating an intimidating atmosphere.
description.panel4: The person stands frozen, with the words "INTERNAL SCREAMING" highlighted to show their silent panic.

emotion: The comic illustrates the internal struggle and anxiety that often accompany public speaking, despite one's attempts at self-reassurance.

title: unknown strength

description.panel1: A character enthusiastically greets another character named Jen.
description.panel2: They exchange a high-five with a loud "SLAP" sound effect.
description.panel3: The greeter grimaces and holds their hand in pain, indicating the high-five was too strong.
description.panel4: Jen looks confused and concerned as the greeter continues to hold their hand in pain.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how high-fives between friends are often given with greater force, leading to mutual embarrassment and pain.

title: The Art

description.panel1: A bird expresses frustration and declares that it no longer cares about others' opinions.
description.panel2: The bird decides to pursue the art that brings it personal joy.
description.panel3: The bird reveals that its art involves creating sculptures of its dead relatives using bug corpses.
description.panel4: Another bird responds, acknowledging that everyone appreciates the disturbing nature of the artwork.

emotion: The author expresses that true artistic fulfillment comes from creating what makes one happy, regardless of outside opinions, and that unique or unconventional art can still be appreciated by others.

title: Holy safety!

description.panel1: A man wearing a hat is speaking, saying that someone has threatened his land, family, and way of life.
description.panel2: The man readies a shotgun with a determined expression, saying "This ends today."
description.panel3: The man fires the shotgun with a loud "BLAM" noise.
description.panel4: The shot results in a graphic explosion of flesh and blood with the sound "FFPLSSH."

emotion: The author is emphasizing the extreme and violent reaction the man has toward perceived threats to his land, family, and way of life.

title: Nonchalant

description.panel1: A frog confidently states that it doesn't care what people think of it.
description.panel2: A turtle tells the frog that it thinks the frog is cool.
description.panel3: The frog struggles to maintain its nonchalant demeanor, internally insisting that it must remain nonchalant.
description.panel4: The turtle comments that the frog looks "chalant," and the frog angrily denies it.

emotion: The comic humorously highlights the contradiction between the frog's declaration of indifference and its actual concerned reaction to a compliment.

title: Vanish shit Act.

description.panel1: A man hires a magician and demands him to make something disappear.
description.panel2: The man points to a large pile labeled "BAD SHIT" and emphasizes to the magician to make it disappear.
description.panel3: The magician hesitates and suggests he could distract the man from the bad shit instead of making it disappear.
description.panel4: The man reluctantly agrees, and the magician claps to show a rabbit, saying "this will suffice."

emotion: The author humorously satirizes those who want to accomplish something but don't want to put in too much effort.

title: forgotten thing

description.panel1: The character with glasses thinks to themselves that they need to tell the other person something important.
description.panel2: The character with glasses starts a conversation and begins to mention the important thing they wanted to talk about.
description.panel3: The character with glasses sits in silence as they suddenly cannot remember the important thing they wanted to say.
description.panel4: The character with glasses looks shocked and distressed, realizing they have completely forgotten the important thing.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the common and understandable experience that people often forget important things they wanted to discuss.

title: dog day

description.panel1: A person walking by a dog says, "Aww dog" while the dog barks, and thinks, "Every day I see a dog is a good day."
description.panel2: The person is stopped by someone who excitedly says, "Hey look!"
description.panel3: Both individuals look into a window labeled "Dog Hotel" filled with multiple dogs.
description.panel4: The person happily concludes, "Today is a great day," while admiring the dogs.

emotion: By describing this character's attitude toward dogs, the author shows his love for pets.

title: Wisdom

description.panel1: A character asks a wise figure, "What is wisdom?"
description.panel2: The wise figure responds, "Wisdom is to accept that you are stupid."
description.panel3: The wise figure continues, "And to take precautions..."
description.panel4: The wise figure concludes, "To mitigate the damage of your own stupidity," while the character nods in understanding.

emotion: The author conveys that true wisdom involves recognizing one's limitations and taking steps to prevent the negative consequences of those limitations.

title: bargain shopping

description.panel1: The character sees a "75% off!" sale sign and reacts excitedly.
description.panel2: The character's friend asks where they would shop if they could afford to shop anywhere.
description.panel3: The character continues to look at the sale while thinking about the question.
description.panel4: The character finds a "50% off!" sale sign and reacts with satisfaction.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the idea that regardless of having the means to shop anywhere, the character still gravitates towards sales.

title: Find Help.

description.panel1: A blue character wearing a name tag that says "RAY" asks a pink character if they need help finding anything while they stand in front of shelves.
description.panel2: The pink character thinks deeply, with a hand on their chin.
description.panel3: The pink character responds by asking for help finding "treasure."
description.panel4: The pink character, now dressed as a pirate, and the blue character, also in pirate attire, walk together enthusiastically as they embark on a treasure hunt.

emotion: The comics playfully depict how seeking help can lead to unexpected and interesting results, celebrating the spirit of adventure.

title: "don't die" diet

description.panel1: A person mentions they were born with a lot of dietary restrictions and had to decline an offer of food from their pal to avoid getting sick.
description.panel2: The person finds that people, like waiters, are usually understanding about their dietary needs and accommodate their requests without issue.
description.panel3: However, sometimes people, like a barista, question their requests, here asking if a certain item has dairy in it.
description.panel4: The barista sighed, his face full of doubt, making him unsure whether his request would be met.

emotion: The author describes the complex experience of people with dietary restrictions, and through the contrast between different situations, expresses that it is appropriate to make requests in some situations, but not in others.

title: Help Help

description.panel1: A robot is panicking and shouting, "Help, help!"
description.panel2: A dog calmly asks the robot, "What's wrong?"
description.panel3: The robot explains, "The humans are trying to train me to behave according to their will!"
description.panel4: The dog suggests, "Try being super cute... then poop in their shoes."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the difference in behavior and response to training between a robot and a dog, emphasizing the dog's cheeky and rebellious attitude towards being controlled.

title: Hi Strung

description.panel1: A purple character says "hey" to a blue character, who enthusiastically responds with "HI!"
description.panel2: The blue character is shown walking away, while the purple character simply stands still.
description.panel3: The blue character, now alone, internally questions their enthusiastic greeting, thinking "HI! REALLY??"
description.panel4: The blue character further reflects on their behavior, thinking "WHY AM I SO FUCKING LAME," looking embarrassed and self-critical.

emotion: The author illustrates the anxious and self-critical thoughts that can follow even simple social interactions, highlighting how self-doubt and negative self-assessment often accompany moments of perceived social awkwardness.

title: Let's Go

description.panel1: A single bird announces that it is heading out after having fun.
description.panel2: The bird explains it needs some "me time" as it starts flying away from a group of birds sitting on wires.
description.panel3: Several other birds respond by agreeing they also need some "me time."
description.panel4: Multiple birds, including the initial bird, all fly away together, with one bird shouting to rally the group.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the irony of seeking solitude but ending up doing so in a group, highlighting the difficulty of finding true alone time within a social context.

title: jokes

description.panel1: Two characters ask Jen if she knows some great jokes.
description.panel2: Jen thinks of a joke and begins to ask, "What do you call a seagull that lives by the bay?"
description.panel3: Jen delivers the punchline, "A bay-gull," and starts laughing.
description.panel4: The other characters appear uninterested or unamused by the joke.

emotion: The author highlights the subjective nature of humor, showing that what one person finds funny may not necessarily amuse others.

title: Go Big or Go Home

description.panel1: A person welcomes Dave at the door and comments that Dave looks different.
description.panel2: Dave explains that he gained a lot of weight to win a game.
description.panel3: The person asks which game, and Dave replies "Spin the Bottle."
description.panel4: Dave, now very rotund, confidently declares that he is going to get lucky tonight.

emotion: By describing how Dave takes extreme measures to increase his chances of winning the game, the author humorously satirizes that sometimes people's gains and efforts are often not equal.

title: trying to understand humans

description.panel1: The character is sitting at a table, looking thoughtful and perplexed.
description.panel2: The character looks confused and somewhat concerned.
description.panel3: The character becomes extremely frustrated, gesturing with both hands and showing an exasperated expression.
description.panel4: The character faceplants onto the table, displaying a sense of defeat.

emotion: The author illustrates the character's increasing frustration and eventual defeat in the attempt to understand human behavior or actions, highlighting the complexity and often baffling nature of human interactions.

title: Carpet Scratching.

description.panel1: A dog tells a squirrel, "My nose is itchy."
description.panel2: The dog proceeds to scratch its own nose using its paw, resulting in sounds like "THUMP," "SMACK," "BAP."
description.panel3: The dog continues to scratch its nose energetically with more "BONK," "WHAP" sounds, and the squirrel offers to help by saying, "Uh, I could scratch it for you real quick."
description.panel4: The dog can no longer stop, making similar sound effects such as "DONK", "BUMP", and "PAP", creating a sense of humor for the dog's vigorous scratching.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a dog's excessive enthusiasm when scratching, expressing that sometimes people know that they cannot accomplish something alone, but they still persist.

title: Order

description.panel1: The lieutenant orders the private to deliver a message to Sergeant Jefferson, stressing its importance for the war's outcome.
description.panel2: The private, looking worried, acknowledges the seriousness of the task and agrees to deliver the message.
description.panel3: The private hands the message to Sergeant Jefferson, who was standing next to him, stating it is from Lieutenant Meyer.
description.panel4: Sergeant Jefferson reads the message, which turns out to be an insulting note saying "Jefferson stinks," leading the private to suggest that direct communication would be faster.

emotion: The author highlights the absurdity and inefficiency of indirect communication, especially in serious contexts where direct interaction would be more effective.

title: No Way

description.panel1: The blue character threatens the yellow character by saying they can do things the easy way or the hard way.
description.panel2: The yellow character suggests an alternative of doing nothing at all.
description.panel3: The blue character looks baffled and contemplates the suggestion.
description.panel4: The blue character is seen lying down, seemingly agreeing to do nothing, as the yellow character looks on contentedly.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the contrast between a confrontational approach and a laid-back, non-confrontational response, ultimately suggesting that sometimes doing nothing can be a viable and peaceful solution.

title: Trust Fall.

description.panel1: A pink character tells a yellow character labeled "SOCIETY" that "I want to trust you", and the yellow character assures he that "I got you."
description.panel2: The pink character is shown closing his eyes and leaning backward in preparation for the trust fall.
description.panel3: The pink character is falling downward, indicating that he was not caught.
description.panel4: The yellow "SOCIETY" character looks at the hole into which the pink character has fallen and says, "oops."

emotion: The author expresses a sense of disappointment in society, suggesting that society often fails to live up to its promises of support and reliability.

title: Jealous Seed.

description.panel1: A seed falls straight down from the tree with a "THUMP" sound.
description.panel2: Another seed floats down gently, twirling through the air with a "flutter" sound.
description.panel3: The second seed lands softly on the ground with a "boop" noise after fluttering down.
description.panel4: The first seed, now on the ground, thinks "FUCKIN' SHOW OFF" as it observes the second seed.

emotion: The author expresses the theme of "there is no harm without contrast" by humorously describing the contrast between two seeds falling from the tree.

title: Technique.

description.panel1: A blue character asks a green character if they can watch them paint to learn their technique, and the green character agrees.
description.panel2: The green character is seen painting while the blue character watches attentively.
description.panel3: The blue character, appearing thoughtful, inspects the green character's painting process and says, "Hmm, yes, I see now."
description.panel4: The blue character reflects privately, thinking, "His technique is being way better than me," with a contemplative expression.

emotion: By describing the behaviors of these two characters, the author expresses the view that only by being good at learning from others can one make great progress.

title: Packing Heat.

description.panel1: A character in a red jacket is pointing a gun at another character, demanding he empty his pockets.
description.panel2: The second character nervously holds up a hot dog bun, saying it's all he have.
description.panel3: The first character looks happy and reveals he was using a hot dog as the gun in the hold-up.
description.panel4: Both characters start to laugh and bond over the absurdity, each holding a hot dog.

emotion: The author conveys a sense of humor by depicting the absurd and unexpected turns in the robbery scene, making the readers smile.

title: Praying Mantis

description.panel1: A character sees a praying mantis and exclaims, "GROSS, A BUG!"
description.panel2: The character raises his fist, preparing to hit the praying mantis.
description.panel3: An unseen hand stops the character by grabbing his fist.
description.panel4: A muscular, divine figure appears, holding the character's fist, preventing him from hitting the praying mantis, while the character looks surprised and scared.

emotion: The author describes how intentional harm to animals is unjustifiable and expresses the author's thoughts on nature and life.

title: cracked

description.panel1: A woman complains to Jen about the man next to her who keeps teasing her and asks Jen to stop, suggesting that the teaser be beaten. Jen replies, "Okay."
description.panel2: The man sits quietly and cracks his knuckles.
description.panel3: The man continues to sit quietly and cracks his knuckles again.
description.panel4: Jen fearfully asks the man if he just cracked his knuckles in the face.

emotion: By humorously depicting Jen's reaction, the author emphasizes that even justice requires great strength to ensure it.

title: Feeling

description.panel1: Two birds are standing together, and one bird asks if the other feels like they are being watched.
description.panel2: The second bird responds, questioning why wouldn't they be watched, suggesting it is normal.
description.panel3: The second bird looks ahead with a calm expression, surrounded by small flowers.
description.panel4: The silhouettes of the two birds are seen being observed by an unseen figure, creating a sense of being watched.

emotion: The author uses the bird's perspective to describe a phenomenon in life, that is, the feeling of being watched, so that readers who have had this experience can empathize.

title: dress-up

description.panel1: One character asks another if they are going to cosplay tomorrow; the second character decides they should.
description.panel2: The second character is uncertain about which cosplay to choose and asks for advice.
description.panel3: The first character suggests doing the worst cosplay possible for laughs.
description.panel4: The next day, the character wears a poorly made cosplay costume that exceeds the expectations of the first character and wants the character to go home.

emotion: The author uses humor to depict that the communication of people in daily life often does not achieve the expected effect.

title: The Right Stuff

description.panel1: A God is thinking about creating bad stuff.
description.panel2: Another character asks if only good stuff can exist instead.
description.panel3: The God explains that having bad stuff helps one appreciate the good stuff more, but the other character disagrees, saying he don't need bad stuff to appreciate the good stuff.
description.panel4: The God insists, saying "Trust me on this," to which the other character simply replies "No."

emotion: The author expresses the idea that the presence of bad experiences or things might be necessary to truly appreciate the good ones, but some individuals may still reject this notion and prefer only good experiences.

title: Sports

description.panel1: A weightlifter says they love lifting heavy things while holding dumbbells.
description.panel2: A swimmer remarks they enjoy moving through masses of low density while swimming.
description.panel3: A football player comments they love kicking a ball of animal skin through a metal frame while on the field.
description.panel4: A person in a fitness class casually states they just come there to fart while doing a stretching exercise.

emotion: The cartoon humorously contrasts the passions of various athletes with the mundane and hilarious reasons a person might take a fitness class, highlighting the diversity of reasons people engage in physical activity.

title: This is so me.

description.panel1: A green character enthusiastically shows a picture on a smartphone to a blue character, saying, "This is so me!"
description.panel2: The blue character points out that the picture on the phone is not the green character but a seal.
description.panel3: The green character looks at the phone with a sad expression, while the blue character looks on.
description.panel4: The green character looks at their own reflection in a mirror, appearing sad and reflective.

emotion: The author shows humor and poignancy through the green character's resonance with images that are clearly not himself, and then his disappointment and self-reflection after realizing his mistake.

title: They Grow So Fast.

description.panel1: A character sees a small problem and decides to take care of it later.
description.panel2: Time passes significantly as indicated by the caption "Much, much later...".
description.panel3: The character decides to finally address the problem.
description.panel4: The small problem has grown significantly larger, surprising the character.

emotion: The author is conveying that procrastinating on small issues can cause them to grow into larger, more difficult problems over time.

title: Imitate

description.panel1: A black cat is sitting with its legs raised.
description.panel2: A person is trying to imitate the cat's posture but is struggling.
description.panel3: The cat is staring calmly with its legs raised high.
description.panel4: The person is exhausted and sweating from trying to imitate the cat.

emotion: The author describes the humorous difficulties humans face when trying to copy the natural and easy movements of animals, showing that everyone has different innate abilities and there is no need to force yourself to do something you are not good at.

title: Taste Test

description.panel1: A pink character declares that their love can withstand anything, holding hands with a blue character.
description.panel2: The pink character questions the blue character pouring milk before cereal.
description.panel3: The pink character objects to the blue character eating pineapple on pizza.
description.panel4: The pink character is overwhelmed seeing the blue character eat eggs with ketchup.

emotion: By describing the before and after contrast of the characters' attitudes, the author humorously illustrates how different and non-traditional food preferences can be very difficult to accept.

title: the Book Club

description.panel1: A person holding a book comments on how strange it is, and another person asks what is strange about it.
description.panel2: The person explains that when they stick their arm into the book, they feel a cold, tingling sensation, and the other person looks intrigued.
description.panel3: The intrigued person now appears disinterested and says it sounds boring, with the first person agreeing and walking away.
description.panel4: A new scene shows a girl sitting alone with the book, titled "The Worm Hole Book Club," indicating it's anything but boring.

emotion: By describing the two characters' attitudes toward books, the author expresses the fun of reading in a humorous and exaggerated way, and hopes to attract the readers' attention.

title: Traffic Love

description.panel1: The traffic light expresses love towards the car, and the car reciprocates the feelings.
description.panel2: The traffic light pleads with the car never to leave, and the car promises it will never leave.
description.panel3: The traffic light pessimistically remarks that all cars do eventually leave.
description.panel4: The green light comes on, and the car drives away, leaving the traffic light alone.

emotion: The author uses the interaction between traffic lights and cars to humorously depict that despite commitment and affection, an inappropriate relationship will sooner or later fall apart.

title: Invincible

description.panel1: The robot enthusiastically tells the professor that it can now go out and perform tasks because it is invincible.
description.panel2: The professor hesitates and reminds the robot of a previous incident, suggesting it may not be a good idea.
description.panel3: The robot encounters a cute cat on the street and becomes excited.
description.panel4: The cat attacks the robot, causing it to malfunction despite its claim of being invincible.

emotion: The author humorously explains that the robot's confidence in its own invincibility is misplaced, and emphasizes the importance of correctly understanding oneself.

title: Placebo

description.panel1: A doctor tells a patient that they were prescribed a placebo.
description.panel2: The patient asks the doctor what a placebo is.
description.panel3: The doctor explains that a placebo is something that doesn't work but tricks people into thinking it does.
description.panel4: The patient reacts with surprise, saying "I'm a placebo?"

emotion: The author humorously describes the concept of placebo, emphasizing that people often act as placebos in everyday life.

title: Be Myself

description.panel1: Bird A asks Bird B if they remember the advice to "be myself".
description.panel2: Bird A asks Bird B what their defining feature is.
description.panel3: Bird B hesitantly replies that Bird A often start a lot of fires, and Bird A affirms him.
description.panel4: Bird A enthusiastically thanks Bird B for the confidence boost.

emotion: The author humorously conveys that embracing one's true nature, even if unconventional or negative, can lead to increased self-confidence.

title: Who Do You Love?

description.panel1: A pink character expresses his love by saying "I love you".
description.panel2: A blue character with a bird on her head asks if the pink character is talking to her or the bird on her head.
description.panel3: The pink character sweats nervously and hesitantly answers "...you..."
description.panel4: The blue character blushes and smiles happily, while the bird looks a little sad.

emotion: The author jokingly highlights the humor and awkward moments that may accompany expressing love, expressing the author's desire for love.

title: Astral Pain

description.panel1: A blue-suited astronaut hears from a purple-suited one that he have found some worm holes.
description.panel2: The blue-suited astronaut corrects the purple-suited one, saying he actually have hole worms.
description.panel3: The purple-suited astronaut decides to report this information back, hesitating.
description.panel4: The purple-suited astronaut is seen flying away in a rocket, leaving the blue-suited astronaut who is hunched over in pain on the planet's surface.

emotion: The cartoon humorously depicts the communication error between the two astronauts, emphasizing the importance of ensuring consistent understanding of information when communicating between people.

title: Congratulations! It's an emigrant!

description.panel1: The doctor informs a couple with a newborn baby that he has some terrible news, while a balloon indicating "It's a boy" hovers above.
description.panel2: The doctor further explains that their baby crawled into another woman's business when they were not looking, causing concern.
description.panel3: The doctor introduces the other woman, Ashley, who has laryngitis and is now nine months pregnant with the couple's child.
description.panel4: The scene reveals the balloon from earlier flying around the room, adding a humorous touch to the absurdity.

emotion: The author uses absurd and surreal humor to playfully critique exaggerated fears or scenarios that new parents might have, presenting an outlandish situation for comedic effect.

title: Making Friends

description.panel1: A blue and white bird expresses that making friends is incredibly difficult and confusing.
description.panel2: The bird reveals it has tried various strategies to make friends.
description.panel3: The bird is seen alone, reflecting sadly near a snowy landscape.
description.panel4: The bird concludes that nothing it has tried works and reiterates its confusion.

emotion: The author highlights the challenges and frustrations of making friends, depicting feelings of loneliness and confusion, as well as the author's desire for friendship.

title: Keep Shoes Tied

description.panel1: A character standing at the base of an escalator tells a child that his shoelace has been caught in the escalator.
description.panel2: The same character continues, explaining to the child that the escalator is shredding him like old paperwork.
description.panel3: The character laments that he should have listened to his father and kept his shoes tied. The escalator gradually swallowed the man.
description.panel4: The child sits alone at the bottom of the escalator and begins tying his shoelaces.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a dramatic consequence of not tying shoelaces, emphasizing the importance of following simple advice to avoid preventable mishaps.

title: Snitches

description.panel1: A doctor asks a wounded patient who harmed him, but the patient insists on being sewn up instead.
description.panel2: The doctor sternly insists that the patient reveal who caused his injury.
description.panel3: The patient reluctantly identifies the Fratelli brothers as the perpetrators.
description.panel4: The doctor commends the patient, saying "Snitches get stitches," while preparing to treat the wound.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the irony in the phrase "A whistleblower gets stitches," suggesting that even medical professionals can be guilty of leaking patient privacy.

title: It is Perfect

description.panel1: A person is posting a Craigslist ad to sell his keyboard, and another person comes over and asks what he's doing.
description.panel2: The second person offers help, claiming expertise in posting ads on Craigslist.
description.panel3: The second person advises against attaching a picture, stating that no one likes them and suggesting to keep the ad simple and professional.
description.panel4: The final panel shows an ad that is misleading and poorly constructed, ending with the phrase "It's perfect!"

emotion: The author is highlighting the irony and humor in receiving unsolicited advice, especially when the advice turns out to be unhelpful or counterproductive.

title: the Cirrrcle of Drugs

description.panel1: A doctor hands a bottle of pills to a patient, explaining that Klumpazine will help with his diarrhea but causes dizziness, so the patient will need Balanzotran for that.
description.panel2: The doctor continues and says that Balanzotran has side effects like nausea, so the patient will need Tumtumz to counteract that.
description.panel3: The doctor mentions that Tumtumz causes diarrhea, but reassures the patient that he's already taking Klumpazine for that.
description.panel4: The doctor holds up the patient and triumphantly sings, "It's the cirrrcle of drugs!"

emotion: The author humorously criticizes the cyclic and often redundant nature of prescribing medications to counteract the side effects of other medications, leading to a never-ending loop of drug dependency.

title: Cornered

description.panel1: A character holding a drink indicates his intention to stand in a corner for the rest of the party.
description.panel2: The character looks shocked to see that another person has taken the corner he intended to use.
description.panel3: The character, now sweating and worried, realizes that all the good corners are taken.
description.panel4: The character jumps up and stands awkwardly in the corner of the ceiling.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the anxiety and discomfort some people feel at social gatherings.

title: Tannin Tantrum.

description.panel1: A person with a beard extends a bottle labeled "THIS IS MY BLOOD" to a smaller white character, who reacts with disgust, saying "SICK."
description.panel2: The bearded person clarifies, saying "JUS' KIDDIN'! IT'S WINE," while the smaller character begins to drink from the bottle, relieved.
description.panel3: The smaller character becomes enraged, claws extended, and attacks the bearded person.
description.panel4: The smaller character, looking manic, declares "I WANT BLOOD" while holding the bottle, as the bearded person appears frightened.

emotion: The comic is based on the theme of misunderstanding, which escalates rapidly and humorously, eventually leading to extreme reactions from the characters, and the author uses this to emphasize the difficulty of managing interpersonal relationships.

title: Getting the Message

description.panel1: A character is sitting alone at home saying, "You don't have to be lonely because you're home alone."
description.panel2: The character is excitedly holding a smartphone and says, "Thanks to smartphones, chatting with friends has never been easier!"
description.panel3: The character looks at the smartphone screen and exclaims, "Oh, they saw my messages!" The screen shows messages sent by the character.
description.panel4: The character is happily holding their smartphone and says, "I've never felt more connected."

emotion: The author celebrates the advancement of technology by describing how smartphones allow people to feel connected to their friends despite being physically alone.

title: Salamander Love Tail

description.panel1: Both salamanders have half a heart tattooed on their tails, and they put their tails together to form a complete heart shape, indicating that these tattoos will symbolize their eternal love.
description.panel2: One salamander accidentally loses its tattooed tail.
description.panel3: Both salamanders are very sad.
description.panel4: The second salamander also cuts off its tail, and the two salamanders hang their lost tails together on the wall to form a heart shape again.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates that while the physical symbol of love may be temporary, the meaning and emotion behind it can be preserved in other ways.

title: Over the Line

description.panel1: The word "TODAY" is written on a piece of paper.
description.panel2: A character notices that the "D" in "TODAY" looks a little weird and thinks they should fix it.
description.panel3: The character tries to fix the "D" but ends up making it worse by scribbling over it.
description.panel4: The character looks frustrated as they hold the pen, clearly overwhelmed by their failed attempt to correct the mistake.

emotion: The author illustrates the tendency to overcorrect small imperfections, which often leads to a worse outcome and increased frustration, highlighting the importance of accepting minor flaws.

title: Call Them Like You See Them.

description.panel1: Two characters collide, and one says, "Hey pal, watch where you're going!"
description.panel2: The second character angrily responds, "What the fuck did you just call me?"
description.panel3: The first character explains, "Pal, because you look like a fun guy I'd be friends with!"
description.panel4: The two characters are now happily walking together, one saying, "We should shoot hoops!" and the other replying, "That sounds fun!"

emotion: The author explains how to defuse initially tense confrontations through friendly and open communication, emphasizing that sincerity often wins responses and produces good results.

title: No Laughing Matter

description.panel1: A user angrily accuses the Social Media Algorithm that their post failed because of it.
description.panel2: The Social Media Algorithm smugly agrees with the user, calling them a derogatory term.
description.panel3: Another Social Media Algorithm asks the first Algorithm if they told the user the real reason their post failed.
description.panel4: The first Social Media Algorithm responds that telling the user the truth about their post being unfunny would break their heart.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the idea that some social media posts fail not because of algorithmic bias but because the content itself is not engaging or funny.

title: The Hard Stuff

description.panel1: The green character approaches the yellow character and asks, "You dealin'?"
description.panel2: The yellow character asks if the green character wants uppers, downers, or pills, and the green character responds that they want "the hard stuff."
description.panel3: The yellow character warns that "this shit will fuck you up," and the green character insistently says, "I need it!"
description.panel4: The yellow character hands a packet labeled "External Validation" to the green character, who looks nervous and eager.

emotion: The author illustrates how seeking external validation can be as addictive and damaging as seeking drugs and other illicit substances.

title: Dog Translator

description.panel1: A scientist states that after years of research, they have built a dog translator.
description.panel2: The dog, now able to speak, declares that it has disproven all the scientist's theorems.
description.panel3: The dog further elaborates on complex mathematical concepts, indicating its high intelligence.
description.panel4: The dog is seen inside a cage with an "adopt" sign, alongside other dogs in similar cages.

emotion: The author humorously satirizes the behavior of those who like to speak nonsense at inappropriate times.

title: Special.

description.panel1: A purple character angrily tells a green character, "There's a special place in hell for you."
description.panel2: The green character reacts with happiness, saying, "Oh wow! A special place just for me!"
description.panel3: Suddenly there is a "BANG!" and the green character looked scared.
description.panel4: The green character was in hell, with a sign in front of him that said "Reserved for Doug". He is crying and being poked by a pitchfork, saying, "For the first time, I felt special..."

emotion: The author humorously describes a misunderstanding, emphasizing that misunderstanding someone else's message can have serious consequences.

title: Dice Roll

description.panel1: A purple character is handing dice to a blue character, saying "Before entering existence, you must roll these dice."
description.panel2: The blue character is tossing the dice while the purple character watches.
description.panel3: After the dice lands, three traits are revealed, "ugly", "short", and "awful voice", and the two characters watch the results.
description.panel4: The blue character asks, "Is that good?" and the purple character sweats nervously.

emotion: The cartoon humorously illustrates the idea that the traits we are born with are random and beyond our control, just like the result of rolling dice, and we have to accept them.

title: Deadline

description.panel1: A man tells someone on the phone that he cannot make it tonight because he has a deadline.
description.panel2: The Grim Reaper, typing on a laptop, overhears the man and snickers, stating "Yeah, you do."
description.panel3: The Grim Reaper looks out at the man through the window, amused.
description.panel4: The Grim Reaper admits to liking puns and says, "Sue me."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the word "deadline" as a joke, with the Grim Reaper punning on the man's serious work deadline, creating a sense of humor.

title: A Little too Cocky

description.panel1: A climber is ascending a steep mountain.
description.panel2: The climber reaches the peak of the mountain and exclaims, "Finally, I have conquered this mountain."
description.panel3: The mountain, personified with a face, thinks to itself, "This guy's a little too cocky for my taste."
description.panel4: A newspaper on a table has a headline that reads, "Man Missing in Avalanche."

emotion: The author expressed the theme of humility and the dangers of overconfidence. The climber's boastful attitude upon conquering the mountain is met with a swift retribution, highlighting that nature can be unforgiving to those who underestimate it.

title: arm-wrestling tournament

description.panel1: A confident person, who looks like they want a challenge, asks to be matched up against someone strong for an arm-wrestling tournament.
description.panel2: Another indifferent participant agrees to the match and they prepare by locking hands.
description.panel3: As soon as the match begins, the indifferent participant decisively wins by slamming the confident person's hand down.
description.panel4: The confident person looks confused and surprised while the indifferent participant smiles and appears genuinely amused.

emotion: The author humorously conveys the message that appearances can be deceiving, and one should not underestimate others based on their demeanor.

title: One More

description.panel1: A character is climbing up a mountain towards a flag that says "GOAL."
description.panel2: The character reaches the top of the mountain and celebrates triumphantly.
description.panel3: The character looks across to the next mountain, which also has a flag on top.
description.panel4: The character is seen walking towards the next mountain, with multiple mountains with flags visible in the distance.

emotion: The author illustrates the idea that achieving one goal often leads to the pursuit of further goals, highlighting the continuous and unending nature of striving for success.

title: Dinner Ready

description.panel1: The witch sits at the dining table and shouts with her back to the table, "Honey, dinner's ready!" The person on the other side of the table looks confused.
description.panel2: The person replies, "But, I'm sitting right here."
description.panel3: Both sit silently at the table.
description.panel4: A vampire rushes into the scene and says, "Sorry, I'm late, honey," revealing that dinner is for the person sitting across the table.

emotion: The author uses humor and twists to create an unexpectedly funny scene, suggesting that the person misunderstood the dinner invitation, highlighting that miscommunication can lead to serious consequences.

title: smile
