description.panel1: The character on the left is being stared at by the character on the right.
description.panel2: The character on the right smiles nervously and sweatdrops.
description.panel3: The character on the left continues to stare blankly.
description.panel4: The character on the left turned and walked away, ignoring the greeting from the character on the right.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts the frustration one might feel when their attempts to be friendly or approachable are met with unresponsiveness or indifference.

title: Which Game

description.panel1: One bird asks the other if he watched the "game" last night, and the second bird enthusiastically replies that he did.
description.panel2: The second bird then asks "Wait, Which game?"
description.panel3: The first bird looks visibly confused.
description.panel4: The first bird is speechless, as evidenced by the thought bubble with the word "shit".

emotion: The author humorously depicts the awkwardness and potential social embarrassment of trying to engage in a conversation about a hot topic without knowing the key specific details.

title: The Wardrobe Wizard

description.panel1: A person challenges a wizard, who readies his wand for attack.
description.panel2: The wizard curses the person with an irritating shirt tag.
description.panel3: The wizard continues by cursing the person with bunched up sleeves.
description.panel4: The wizard's final curse causes wet socks, leading the person to plead for him to stop.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how seemingly minor annoyances in daily life, like uncomfortable clothing issues, can accumulate to become incredibly bothersome.

title: Inner Value.

description.panel1: A fish expresses that it feels ugly.
description.panel2: A clam reassures the fish by saying "It's what's on the inside that counts."
description.panel3: The fish seems to be uplifted and wonders if the clam means qualities like a sharp wit or a kind heart.
description.panel4: The clam clarifies that it means literal precious gems inside it, leading the fish to react with a simple "oh."

emotion: The author humorously describes the misunderstanding between the fish and the clam, emphasizing the theme that a person's inner self is more important than their outer image.

title: Ooh Burn

description.panel1: The human tells the robot it will never be truly human because it does not fear death.
description.panel2: The robot retorts that it fears being bored to death by the human's whiny babbling.
description.panel3: There is a moment of silence as the human and robot stare at each other.
description.panel4: The robot raises its arm and exclaims, "Oh, BURN!"

emotion: The author expresses people's boredom with endless preaching by humorously describing the robot's behavior.

title: Freaking Disaster

description.panel1: A person is sitting on a toilet, looking anxious and thinking about how awkward it is to use the bathroom at a stranger's house.
description.panel2: The person notices there is no plunger or toilet brush, increasing their discomfort and concern.
description.panel3: The person reaches for a hand towel but can't find one, adding to their frustration.
description.panel4: The person, now standing, angrily exclaims about the lack of a hand towel and the overall chaotic state of the bathroom.

emotion: The author expresses the relatable and humorous anxiety of using the bathroom in an unfamiliar or unprepared environment, highlighting the minor yet significant inconveniences that can turn a simple task into a stressful experience.

title: Beautiful Nightmare

description.panel1: A black cat stand in the doorway of a bedroom, with the shadowy figure asking if the woman in bed is having a nightmare.
description.panel2: The shadowy figure calls the scene of the woman shaking in bed pathetically.
description.panel3: The woman wakes up, exclaiming she's had the best orgasm ever.
description.panel4: The woman falls back asleep with the shadowy figure leaving and the cat approaching the bed.

emotion: The comic humorously discusses people's expectations of nightmares, with a twist that reveals an unexpectedly humorous interpretation of the woman's experience.

title: Alpha Species

description.panel1: Two aliens look through a window and see a cat, with one saying, "That must be the alpha species of this planet."
description.panel2: One alien kneels before the cat, offering a symbol of peace and saying, "We come in peace."
description.panel3: The cat stares at the aliens and the offered item with indifference.
description.panel4: The cat swipes at the item, knocking it away.

emotion: The author humorously describes the cat's habit of pushing objects off the table in daily life, which makes readers who have similar experiences smile.

title: what if ...

description.panel1: The character starts doubting themselves, worrying if what she said was weird.
description.panel2: The character panics, fearing others now think she is creepy.
description.panel3: The character tries to reassure herself by thinking she might be overthinking the situation.
description.panel4: The character doubts herself again, wondering if she is truly overthinking it, and the cycle is repeated.

emotion: The author expresses the cyclical nature of self-doubt and overthinking, illustrating how an individual's insecurities can lead to continuous second-guessing and anxiety.

title: deliver happiness

description.panel1: The character is walking outside on a sunny day, feeling happy with the caption "Life is great!"
description.panel2: The character is suddenly attacked by dark figures labeled "negative feelings" and looks distressed with the caption "Life sucks."
description.panel3: The character gives a balloon to one of the dark figures, and they both look calmer.
description.panel4: The character walks away happily, now accompanied by the dark figures who seem friendlier.

emotion: The author expresses that while negative feelings can unexpectedly intrude on a happy life, dealing with them positively can diminish their power and allow life to return to a more balanced and content state.

title: air-hockey

description.panel1: One character expresses self-doubt by saying they are bad at air-hockey.
description.panel2: Their friend laughs and offers to help, confident that it will be humorous.
description.panel3: The friend scores impressively, flicking the puck into the goal with force.
description.panel4: The score is shown as 0-1, with a caption stating "the power of friendship," and both characters look content.

emotion: The author illustrates that with the support and help of friends, one can achieve unexpected success and highlights the motivational power of friendship.

title: Ever Sad

description.panel1: Max asks Gabe if he ever gets sad.
description.panel2: Gabe denies feeling sad.
description.panel3: Gabe reaffirms that he does not get sad.
description.panel4: Gabe, appearing to cry, claims that it is due to "excess happiness" when Max questions him.

emotion: The author uses humor to depict the paradox that people may have in their tendency to deny their sadness and hide their true emotions.

title: Cuite

description.panel1: A bird excitedly points out a new, attractive bird perched nearby.
description.panel2: The first bird claims it's their cousin, but the second bird expresses confusion.
description.panel3: The first bird asked the second bird if it was not its cousin.
description.panel4: Both birds were embarrassed.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the embarrassment and confusion about complex kinship relationships that may arise in social interactions, making readers who have similar experiences laugh.

title: Booster Seat

description.panel1: The character reads on their phone, "Rearranging furniture can improve your mood."
description.panel2: The character stands up and begins to move their yellow chair.
description.panel3: The character finishes repositioning the chair.
description.panel4: The character sits back down on the chair in its new position and says, "I feel better already."

emotion: The cartoon humorously illustrates how small things in life can have a positive impact on a person's mood.

title: An Apple a Day

description.panel1: An anthropomorphized apple states, "They say 'An apple a day keeps the doctor away."
description.panel2: The apple continues, expressing concern, "But there's this really cute doctor I don't want to keep away."
description.panel3: A banana character asks the apple, who is holding a syringe, "You trying to kill someone again?"
description.panel4: The apple, holding a syringe and standing next to a bottle with a skull and crossbones, angrily replies to the banana, "Shut up, Greg!"

emotion: The author humorously plays on the saying "an apple a day keeps the doctor away" to express the author's desire for friendship.

title: Wheel of Pain

description.panel1: The character, appearing tired or resigned, states "Another day in my 30's" while reaching out to spin a multi-colored wheel.
description.panel2: The character, with a weary expression, comments "Let's see what'll hurt today" as they spin the wheel.
description.panel3: The multi-colored wheel, labeled "Wheel of Pain," is shown spinning rapidly, suggesting anticipation of the result.
description.panel4: The wheel stops on the section labeled "Everything." The character stands with a resigned look, indicating that all parts will hurt.

emotion: The author humorously describes the common experience of physical discomfort or pain in one’s 30s, suggesting that people should take physical health seriously as they age.

title: bathtub adventures

description.panel1: A person is singing happily in the bathtub, saying "scrubba dub-dub, I'm in da tub."
description.panel2: A friendly-looking spider is hanging from a web, saying "lol me too."
description.panel3: The person reacts in panic and shock upon realizing the spider's presence.
description.panel4: The spider, now moving away, says "jk, later loser."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates an unexpected and amusing encounter between a person enjoying a bath and a spider, highlighting themes of surprise and fear.

title: Goals

description.panel1: A snail is shown with the caption "BEHOLD THE SNAIL."
description.panel2: The caption reads "WHILE SLOW, WITH PATIENCE AND ENDURANCE IT WILL SURELY REACH ITS GOAL," depicting the snail moving slowly.
description.panel3: The snail says, "I'M ON MY WAY TO GET TOTALLY SHITFACED."
description.panel4: The snail is seen moving towards a distant goal with the caption "GODSPEED."

emotion: The author humorously contrasts the motivational message of perseverance and patience with the snail's unexpected and humorous goal of getting drunk, showing a sense of humor.

title: locked out

description.panel1: A person expresses doubt about the functionality of a door lock and decides to test it.
description.panel2: The person goes outside and finds that the door does lock properly.
description.panel3: The person realizes that they are now locked outside with a concerned expression.
description.panel4: The person stands outside the house, looking frustrated at being locked out.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the consequences of testing something without considering potential outcomes, leading to an unintended predicament.

title: Just A Feeling

description.panel1: Two men are sitting on a bench, and one asks if the other ever thinks that pigeons are secretly planning to kill them.
description.panel2: The second man responds that the notion is ridiculous and asks where the first man gets such ideas.
description.panel3: The first man, looking unsure, says it's just a feeling.
description.panel4: The second man has turned into a skeleton, implying he has been attacked, while the first man reiterates, "Just a feeling."

emotion: The author is humorously addressing paranoid thoughts and irrational fears by exaggerating them to an extreme, illustrating that sometimes feelings, no matter how absurd, might hint at a bizarre reality.

title: Sun Glare

description.panel1: A character eagerly watches the sunrise with the exclamation, "Here comes the sunrise!"
description.panel2: The sun, with a wary expression, peeks over the horizon while the character continues to watch.
description.panel3: The sun nervously thinks to itself, "Oh God, that guy's watching me rise again... what a stalker."
description.panel4: The character, oblivious to the sun's discomfort, encourages the sunrise, saying, "Come on, Sun, let's see that rise."

emotion: The author humorously conveys the idea that being stared at in public is embarrassing.

title: productivity

description.panel1: The character says, "I should be productive today" while lying on the floor.
description.panel2: The character stays in the same position, now silent, with a speech bubble containing three dots indicating a pause or blank thought.
description.panel3: The character remains in the same position, still silent, with another speech bubble containing three dots.
description.panel4: The character stays lying on the floor and falls asleep, indicated by a speech bubble with a "Z."

emotion: The author humorously expresses the struggle of intending to be productive but instead feeling unmotivated and ultimately falling asleep.

title: Succulent

description.panel1: A yellow character says "Ouch!" after getting pricked by a cactus, and a blue character instructs them to stop messing with the cactus.
description.panel2: The yellow character sarcastically warns that the cactus now has a taste for blood, while the blue character says "it funny".
description.panel3: Later at night, the blue character is shown sleeping peacefully while the door to their room creeps open.
description.panel4: The open door reveals the silhouette of the cactus, humorously implying it is coming for the blue character.

emotion: By describing the behavior of the cactus that tasted blood, the author humorously points out that when raising pets, one should not stimulate their ferocity.

title: Eraser

description.panel1: The character confidently tells the eraser to "remove mistakes."
description.panel2: The eraser is used to remove a written mistake, but it smudges the text.
description.panel3: The character looks disappointed and says "Eraser..."
description.panel4: The character, now holding the eraser with a determined look, says "you know what to do."

emotion: The cartoon humorously shows the frustration of using an eraser to smudge text rather than cleanly remove mistakes, suggesting that not doing one's job well can lead to bad consequences.

title: sigh

description.panel1: The character proudly presents their completed artwork with excitement.
description.panel2: The character moves to save their work.
description.panel3: The computer displays a "PROGRAM NOT RESPONDING. RESTART?" message, indicating that the program has crashed.
description.panel4: The character is visibly frustrated and disappointed.

emotion: The author expressed the frustration and disappointment often felt by digital artists when they lose their unsaved work due to a computer crash or software malfunction.

title: Slutty Witch

description.panel1: A person in a skeleton costume asks a witch character what she is dressed as, suggesting it's a "slutty witch."
description.panel2: The witch character confidently states that she is indeed a "slutty witch."
description.panel3: The skeleton character mischievously asks the witch if she wants to perform some magic tricks on him.
description.panel4: The witch performs a magic trick, causing the skeleton character to be suspended upside-down in a pentagram, while she comfortably watches TV.

emotion: By describing the behavior of the characters in the cartoon, the author implies that being rude to strangers may lead to serious consequences.

title: Those Guys

description.panel1: Two bearded men are holding hands with a heart above their heads, indicating affection.
description.panel2: A person with a less impressive beard looks at the two bearded men walking and expresses frustration.
description.panel3: The frustrated person comments that the two bearded men don't need to flaunt something.
description.panel4: The frustrated person reveals that what he thinks the two bearded men are flaunting is their luscious beards.

emotion: The author humorously highlights jealousy and the fixation on physical attributes by showing the character's frustration is rooted in envy of the other bearded men's impressive facial hair.

title: THIS DOES NOT HAPPEN

description.panel1: A person comments on the unusual occurrence of snow in April, finding it amusing.
description.panel2: The person looks out the window with a neutral expression.
description.panel3: The person repeats the word "snow?!" with a surprised expression.
description.panel4: The person surprisedly questions "in April?!" with a very exaggerated expression.

emotion: The author highlights the absurdity or implausibility of unusual weather events that occur out of season, using exaggerated facial expressions to highlight a sense of humor.

title: It is Fine

description.panel1: Person A points out that Person B received the wrong burger and suggests they send it back, but Person B doesn't want to be troublesome.
description.panel2: Person A notes that the burger is also burnt, to which Person B replies they don't want to cause any trouble.
description.panel3: Person A observes a hair on the burger, and Person B dismisses it as probably their own hair.
description.panel4: Person A mentions there are shards of glass in the burger, but Person B insists that "It's fine" while clearly suffering.

emotion: The author satirizes that some people will take extreme measures, even at the expense of their own interests, in order to avoid conflict or trouble.

title: Must Be Fun

description.panel1: A yellow character compliments a pink character's haircut, calling them "Frankenstein," and the pink character responds sarcastically with "Very funny."
description.panel2: A green character corrects the yellow character, stating it's "Frankenstein's monster," and the pink character says, "Oh Snap, it's Dan!"
description.panel3: The pink character suggests they need to be careful with their words, and the green character pedantically corrects, "Technically, you listen."
description.panel4: The yellow character sarcastically comments, "Oh, Dan! You're so fun at parties!" while the green character appears pleased.

emotion: The author is highlighting the humor in overly pedantic corrections and how such behavior can be perceived as socially awkward or annoying in casual conversations.

title: On the Hook.

description.panel1: A character in a go-kart is driving off a cliff.
description.panel2: The character falls off the cliff along with the go-kart.
description.panel3: A cloud character hooks the falling character and go-kart with a fishing rod.
description.panel4: The character appears back on land, looking frustrated or disheartened, with a thought bubble saying "Just let me die."

emotion: The author humorously depicts the frustration of being saved from failure in a game when the player might prefer to start over, satirizing the perfectionism of the players.

title: Get Up in the Evening

description.panel1: A bird is perched on a branch with a purple sky in the background.
description.panel2: The bird is now on the same branch with a distant view of the sunset or sunrise.
description.panel3: Focus on the sky showing the sun setting or rising with an orange hue.
description.panel4: A close-up of the bird, who says, "Sometimes you just wake up sad."

emotion: The author is conveying a sentiment about how feeling sad can be an inexplicable part of life, sometimes occurring without any particular reason, similar to how the changing of the day occurs naturally.

title: Fear

description.panel1: The green character is frightened, exclaiming "Oh no! A hornet!" while the blue character appears calm.
description.panel2: The blue character reassures the green character, saying, "Relax. They're more afraid of you than you are of it."
description.panel3: The hornet states "I fear nothing."
description.panel4: Both the green and blue characters look alarmed, with the blue character advising "Run."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how quickly confidence can be overturned by a direct threat, emphasizing the unpredictable nature of fear.

title: dog day

description.panel1: A woman sees a dog on the street and thinks to herself that every day she sees a dog is a good day.
description.panel2: A friend calls her attention and points towards something.
description.panel3: They both stand in front of a "Dog Hotel" window filled with many dogs.
description.panel4: The woman, overwhelmed with joy, thinks that this is the greatest day.

emotion: The author expresses his love for dogs by describing the simple joy and happiness that dogs can bring to people.

title: Wing Tip.

description.panel1: A penguin asks a red bird, "What's the best way to get out of an awkward situation?"
description.panel2: The red bird responds, "You're a bird. Just fly away."
description.panel3: The penguin, looking confused, starts to say "BUT," while the red bird looks at it.
description.panel4: The red bird is gone, and there's a small black line in the sky indicating it has flown away, leaving the penguin standing alone.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the point that everyone has different advantages and strengths, and when dealing with problems one should not copy other people’s solutions, but instead find a method that suits one’s own.

title: Emotion Detector

description.panel1: A character asks another character if the dog is a seeing-eye dog.
description.panel2: The second character responds that the dog is an emotional support dog and the dog sniffs the first character.
description.panel3: The dog declares that the first character is depressed.
description.panel4: The first character, now sweating, internally acknowledges the dog's accuracy.

emotion: The author shows the importance the author places on emotions by depicting the dog’s accurate emotional assessment of the character.

title: 150 Million Years Age ...

description.panel1: A dinosaur expresses a desire for a significant change in its life.
description.panel2: The dinosaur sees a meteor shower approaching and gasps in surprise.
description.panel3: A view of meteors crashing into the ocean.
description.panel4: The dinosaur sighs, reminiscing about events that happened 150 million years ago.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the unexpected and drastic changes caused by the meteor events that led to the extinction of the dinosaurs, portraying the dinosaur's desire for change with ironic hindsight.

title: Somewhere Better

description.panel1: A child asks their mother where their father is while the mother is sitting in a chair reading.
description.panel2: The mother replies to the child, saying that the father has gone to a better place.
description.panel3: The scene shifts to a beach where the father is seen lounging on a chair while other people enjoy beach activities around him.
description.panel4: The father relaxes in a sun chair, thinking to himself, "This really IS better."

emotion: The author humorously describes the concept of a "Somewhere Better" as Dad being on vacation and enjoying a relaxing time on the beach, expressing the author's attitude of loving life and enjoying life.

title: All Beauty Must Die

description.panel1: A ghost admires a flower and says, "How beautiful."
description.panel2: The ghost plucks the flower and walks away.
description.panel3: The plucked flower lies on the ground.
description.panel4: A new ghost emerges from the withered flower and says, "Oh, damn."

emotion: The author conveys the message that admiring beauty sometimes leads to its destruction and that our actions towards beautiful things can have unexpected consequences.

title: Unfinished Business.

description.panel1: Death tells a man that it is time for him to die.
description.panel2: The man pleads with him to watch 1001 movies before he dies and shows Death a book called 1001 Movies Before You Die.
description.panel3: Death looks a little hesitant while the man holds the book with a determined look.
description.panel4: The man and Death sit on the couch and watch a movie together, and Death comments on how creepy Hannibal Lecter is, and the man agrees.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the man's reluctance to die before completing his personal goals and shows Death's unexpected willingness to fulfill the man's wish, conveying a sense of humor.

title: Thoughts and Prayers

description.panel1: Bobby shares online that he lost his job and is struggling, and Rory responds by sending "thoughts and prayers."
description.panel2: Later, Bobby calls Rory asking if he remembers sending thoughts.
description.panel3: Bobby explains that one of the thoughts sent by Rory was Bobby's bank login information by accident.
description.panel4: Bobby requests to get the bank login information back, but Rory, sitting on a pile of money, responds by only offering prayers.

emotion: The author uses humor to critique the often empty and unhelpful nature of "thoughts and prayers" as a response to serious problems, suggesting that tangible help would be more meaningful.

title: Snug Bug

description.panel1: One spider congratulates another spider for catching its first bug.
description.panel2: The first spider instructs the new spider to wrap up the bug quickly so it can't escape.
description.panel3: The second spider wove a piece of clothing for the bug.
description.panel4: The bug flew away happily wearing the clothing.

emotion: The author humorously depicted the process of the spider capturing and binding the bug, making the readers smile.

title: Big Decision

description.panel1: The Purple character enthusiastically tells the smaller Pink character, "Go big or go home!"
description.panel2: The Pink character suddenly becomes much bigger than the Purple character.
description.panel3: The Pink character, however, becomes frustrated and says, "Well, this isn't working. I guess I'll just go home..."
description.panel4: This motivates the Purple character, causing him to go home.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates how motivational phrases are often misleading, as the Purple character's advice to "go big or go home" leads to an unexpected and ironic outcome.

title: Concentrate

description.panel1: A bird screams "AAAGHH."
description.panel2: The bird wonders how it is supposed to concentrate with so many things to think about.
description.panel3: The bird questions if there have always been so many things.
description.panel4: The bird contemplates if it has been ignoring everything until now.

emotion: The author expresses the desire to try to focus amidst numerous distractions and realizes how difficult it is.

title: Dog Date

description.panel1: Two dogs are sitting at a table, one compliments the other's candle.
description.panel2: The other dog responds, mentioning that the candle is sausage-scented.
description.panel3: Both dogs continue sitting, focusing on the candle.
description.panel4: The first dog is now proposing marriage to the other dog.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the idea that small, thoughtful details, like the scent of a candle, can have a profound and unexpected impact, potentially leading to major decisions like a marriage proposal in a whimsical and lighthearted manner.

title: Look up in the Sky

description.panel1: A person excitedly points at the sky and exclaims, "IT'S A BIRD!"
description.panel2: Another person standing beside the first looks at the sky and corrects, "IT'S A PLANE."
description.panel3: They both just stand there quietly looking up.
description.panel4: The first person concedes, "OH, YES. A PLANE."

emotion: The author humorously depicts a common scenario of misidentification, expressing the excitement of the discovery and the embarrassment of being corrected.

title: Whiffed

description.panel1: A yellow character instructs a pink character to let a dog sniff their hand first, and the pink character agrees.
description.panel2: The dog sniffs the hand of the pink character.
description.panel3: The dog declares that the pink character's heart is impure.
description.panel4: The pink character is being flung away violently, while the dog remains calm.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a scene in which a dog judges a person's morality by smell and ultimately rejects those with "impure" intentions, expressing the author's love and trust for dogs.

title: Ugly Mug.

description.panel1: A person wearing mask points a gun at a scared victim, demanding their money.
description.panel2: The victim insults the masked person, saying they are so ugly they need to wear a ski mask.
description.panel3: The masked person starts to remove their mask, insisting they are not ugly, while the victim offers money for them to keep it on.
description.panel4: The masked person looks content, holding a stack of money.

emotion: The author uses humor to describe an unexpected situation, and the robbery turns into a negotiation about appearance, which ends with a comic ending, humorously expressing the importance that modern people attach to appearance.

title: wiggy

description.panel1: A person asks the main character if they cut and dyed their hair.
description.panel2: The main character replies that their hair is actually a wig.
description.panel3: The main character pulls off the wig to reveal their original long hair is still intact underneath.
description.panel4: The person is surprised and confused, questioning how this is possible.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a deceptive wig, highlighting the unexpected surprise when the wig is removed to reveal the original hair.

title: Cool Fate.

description.panel1: A character referred to as "Ghost of Christmas Future" is leading another character by hand, and the second character asks where they are being taken.
description.panel2: The second character sees a gravestone and questions if this is the future.
description.panel3: The second character, now in the future, is excited to see snow, fresh water, and clean air.
description.panel4: The joyful second character expresses their happiness that climate change has been beaten, while the ghost remains silent.

emotion: The cartoon uses imagery from "A Christmas Carol" to emphasize an extremely important message: the need to take good care of the environment.

title: Isolation

description.panel1: A person looks out the window and thinks that isolation is okay because their cats keep them company.
description.panel2: The cats read a newspaper and remark that it says the person can pass the virus on to them.
description.panel3: The cats look worried and concerned after reading the newspaper.
description.panel4: The two cats lie comfortably in the room, and the person disappears.

emotion: The author humorously expresses people’s concerns and anxieties about the spread of the virus, emphasizing that even pets react to the news that their owners may infect them.

title: A Head in Life

description.panel1: A character asks the "Wise Head of the Mountains" why they can't get ahead in life.
description.panel2: The "Wise Head of the Mountains" suggests working hard, but the character replies that they have already tried that.
description.panel3: The "Wise Head of the Mountains" suggests believing in oneself, but the character replies that they have also tried that.
description.panel4: The "Wise Head of the Mountains" suggests having rich parents, leaving the character speechless.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the challenges and sometimes unfair advantages of succeeding in life, showing that having wealthy parents can be an integral factor, but it's not something everyone can control.

title: lady life

description.panel1: A person is surprised by the sudden return of Mother Nature.
description.panel2: The person nervously attempts to reconcile with Mother Nature, acknowledging past issues.
description.panel3: Mother Nature kicks the person violently.
description.panel4: As the person lies on the ground, Mother Nature insults them.

emotion: The cartoon humorously depicts the tense relationship between humans and Mother Nature, emphasizing that humans should live in harmony with Mother Nature.

title: Going Out

description.panel1: A person sitting on a couch asks, "Bob, are you ready?"
description.panel2: Bob, an older man wearing a dress, stands up and replies, "I'm ready."
description.panel3: The seated person tells Bob, "You can't go out like that."
description.panel4: The seated person starts combing Bob's mustache and says, "Your moustache is unkempt."

emotion: The author humorously subverts expectations by having the criticism focus on Bob's mustache rather than his unconventional choice of attire, highlighting how societal norms can be seen differently depending on perspective.

title: Facebook Timelines.

description.panel1: A person stands alone as another person extends a baby toward them, shouting, "LOOK AT MY BABY!"
description.panel2: Another person appears, also thrusting a baby and exclaiming, "LOOK AT MY BABY!" while the initial person looks increasingly uncomfortable.
description.panel3: Multiple people aggressively push their babies toward the central figure, all shouting, "LOOK AT MY BABY!"
description.panel4: The central figure is overwhelmed and surrounded by a pile of babies, with a text bubble stating, "LOOK AT OUR FUCKING BABIES."

emotion: The author humorously criticizes some phenomena on social media, namely that users often display personal highlights, such as their children, to attract attention and recognition, which in turn causes anxiety among users.

title: Solution

description.panel1: Two anthropomorphic water droplets angrily confront each other, one yelling, "YOU WANNA PIECE OF ME, ASSHOLE?!"
description.panel2: The second water droplet responds angrily, saying, "BRING IT ON, BITCH!"
description.panel3: The first water droplet, still angry, declares "THAT'S IT!!" and charges towards the second water droplet.
description.panel4: The two droplets combine with a "BLOOP" sound, forming a single, larger water droplet with elements of both original droplets.

emotion: The author uses the merging of two drops of water as a symbol to humorously illustrate that the best solution to certain conflicts is reconciliation and unification.

title: I Got Your Back

description.panel1: A person says to his friend, "You know I got your back, Dave," while putting his arm around his friend's shoulder.
description.panel2: The person continues, "I'm always right behind you," with both now standing side by side.
description.panel3: The person leans in closer, implying he has something more to say.
description.panel4: The person finishes with, "Because your booty is so beautiful," revealing his true, humorous intention.

emotion: The author uses a play on words and visual humor to set up a situation where a seemingly supportive and heartfelt statement ends with an unexpected and funny twist, thereby making light of the situation.

title: Choices

description.panel1: A robot asks a professor if it is built to love or to destroy.
description.panel2: The professor responds by asking the robot if it wants to be good or evil.
description.panel3: The cat on the table pushed the vase on the table to the floor, causing the vase to break.
description.panel4: A black cat sitting on the table said that robots can also choose to be fabulous.

emotion: The author conveys the importance of choice and identity, emphasizing that options are not always binary, and people can choose other compromise paths.

title: Strength.

description.panel1: A character prays, asking for strength in difficult times.
description.panel2: The character becomes muscular, expressing surprise at gaining physical strength.
description.panel3: The character admires his new physique and calls for his son's attention.
description.panel4: The son, on his deathbed, mentions seeing a light, which the father overlooks while showing off his muscles.

emotion: The author humorously highlights a misunderstanding between wishing for strength and the different forms it can take, while also critiquing the parent's insensitivity to the child's critical condition.

title: Roommate Dog.

description.panel1: A green character is leaving and tells a yellow dog character, "Back in 20 minutes." The dog waves and responds with, "Later dude."
description.panel2: The dog stands alone in the room and thinks to himself, "Well... he's never ever coming back."
description.panel3: The dog stands next to a couch and says, "Only one thing left to do."
description.panel4: The dog holds a large axe and says, "Destroy everything."

emotion: The author humorously depicts the situation in which a dog is left alone in a room with no way to release its energy and can only destroy things everywhere, expressing the author's helplessness towards pet dogs.

title: Hello

description.panel1: A crow is enthusiastically greeting the sky and trees.
description.panel2: The crow is looking down at the ground.
description.panel3: The crow is observing small green plants sprouting from the ground.
description.panel4: The crow sarcastically greets the sprouting green plants as "nightmare grass."

emotion: The author humorously contrasts the crow's initial joy with its disdain for the grass sprouts, reflecting the phenomenon that the same person can have very different attitudes toward different things.

title: Silent Night Holy Night

description.panel1: A child sitting on the floor asks her daddy if Santa will burn himself when coming down the chimney.
description.panel2: The father agrees to put out the fire to prevent Santa from burning himself.
description.panel3: The child, now with an expression of determination, tells the father "No..."
description.panel4: Close-up of the child's face with glowing eyes saying "...leave it on."

emotion: The author uses the common concerns about safety when Santa Claus comes down the chimney and twists it into a humorous and dark scene, expressing a kind of black humor.

title: Info Dump.

description.panel1: A pink character is sitting on a toilet using their phone.
description.panel2: A blue blob-like character appears, saying "Look at you."
description.panel3: The blue blob-like character continues, "Staring at your phone instead of just living in the moment!"
description.panel4: The pink character continued to sit on the toilet and fell into deep thought.

emotion: The author humorously satirized the fact that modern people are often addicted to their mobile phones and do not care about the beauty of daily life.

title: "adult fiction"

description.panel1: A person with glasses is attentively reading a book with a cover that seems explicit or has romantic content.
description.panel2: The person closes the book, looking contemplative.
description.panel3: The person tosses the book away.
description.panel4: The person covers their face with their hands, indicating embarrassment or regret.

emotion: The author expresses the idea that reading adult or explicit fiction can lead to feelings of embarrassment or regret.

title: Beyond Belief

description.panel1: Character with long hair holds up two cups and says, "Check this shit out," to a pink character.
description.panel2: The long-haired character shows that he has changed the content of the cups.
description.panel3: The pink character, impressed, comments, "Wow, Jesus! You're unbelievable!"
description.panel4: The long-haired character, now angry, exclaims, "THE FUCK DID YOU SAY?!" while the pink character looks nervous.

emotion: The author uses humor to show a situation where a character does something surprising and is offended because it is misunderstood, which can lead to unexpected reactions.

title: art day

description.panel1: The character excitedly states they are going to draw all day.
description.panel2: The character begins to draw but is dissatisfied and says "ehhh... no."
description.panel3: The character repeatedly draws and erases, expressing frustration.
description.panel4: When asked what they have drawn, the character yells "NOTHING!! BLANK."

emotion: The author illustrates the frustration and struggle of the creative process, where despite dedicating time and effort, the results may still feel unsatisfactory or unproductive.

title: Cool Cat

description.panel1: A cat tells someone, who appears to be a witch, that it is rebranding itself while standing on a skateboard.
description.panel2: The cat smugly declares that it is now a "cool cat" as it stands confidently on the skateboard, with sunglasses and a cigarette.
description.panel3: The witch points out that the cat has kitty litter hanging off its backside.
description.panel4: The cat dismisses the witch by saying it can't hear her over the sound of its skateboarding skills as it rides away, continuing to leave a trail of kitty litter.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the cat trying to reinvent itself and adopt a "cool" image despite the ridiculous and obvious flaw of the cat litter sticking to it, which reflects the idea that simply appearing cool does not necessarily change the underlying problems and make one's image credible.

title: Funny Comic

description.panel1: A man asks his wife if she saw the comic he sent her.
description.panel2: The wife responds that it was okay.
description.panel3: The man looks disappointed and asks if it was just okay.
description.panel4: The man is seen dramatically leaving with a suitcase, signifying his exaggerated response to the feedback.

emotion: The author uses humor to highlight how people can sometimes overreact to simple feedback, especially when they were expecting more enthusiasm or appreciation.

title: Birthday Boy

description.panel1: Three characters sing "Happy Birthday to You" to a "birthday boy" at a table with a birthday cake.
description.panel2: They continue to sing "Happy Birthday to You".
description.panel3: The birthday boy looks distressed and a character says to him "You don't sing".
description.panel4: The three characters sing "Dear Adam, Happy Birthday..." to the birthday boy.

emotion: The author expresses a humorous scene when someone is embarrassed when everyone sings the birthday song to him on his birthday.

title: School of Card Knocks

description.panel1: The yellow character offers to go first to teach the pink character how to play the card game.
description.panel2: The yellow character plays the first card.
description.panel3: The yellow character explains that the card lets him draw six more cards and potentially cast any spell cards instantly.
description.panel4: The pink character asks if it's his turn; the yellow character responds that he have already lost.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the complexity and difficulty of learning a new card game, highlighting the overwhelming advantage a knowledgeable player can have over a beginner.

title: Living Space

description.panel1: A ghost approaches a person who is sitting comfortably in a chair.
description.panel2: The ghost greets the person by saying "Hey Jeff," causing the person to look startled.
description.panel3: The person, significantly irritated and pointing, tells the ghost that ghosts are not allowed in living rooms.
description.panel4: The ghost, now from the doorway, asks if it can have one of the person's Pop-Tarts.

emotion: The author humorously depicts ordinary interactions with a supernatural touch, highlighting the absurdity of everyday rules being applied to impossible situations, expressing a sense of humor.

title: Good Boy

description.panel1: A dog howls at the moon.
description.panel2: A witch asks the dog if it just howled at her.
description.panel3: The dog clarifies that it was howling at the moon and expresses respect for women's autonomy.
description.panel4: The witch acknowledges the response and leaves, to which the dog responds with "See ya."

emotion: The author uses humor to highlight the importance of respecting women's autonomy, even in casual or unexpected interactions.

title: Upon Reflection

description.panel1: A character mentions they feel unattractive but prompts their friend to observe something.
description.panel2: The character invites their friend to follow them.
description.panel3: The friend questions if they are looking at a bathroom mirror.
description.panel4: Upon looking in the mirror, the friend realizes and exclaims that they actually look attractive.

emotion: The author expounds on the idea that one can change one's confidence and self-perception by looking at oneself from a different perspective, and encourages readers to be good at discovering the beauty in themselves.

title: Wrong Answer

description.panel1: The interviewer is sitting at his desk, stating that the applicant has an excellent resume.
description.panel2: The interviewer asks the applicant a final question about her favorite animal.
description.panel3: The applicant confidently answers that she is a dog person.
description.panel4: The interviewer, surrounded by multiple cats in his office, tells the applicant that her answer is wrong.

emotion: The author uses humor to illustrate the subjectivity and potential bias in interview processes, showing that personal preferences of the interviewer can unfairly impact hiring decisions.

title: Stinky Kingdom

description.panel1: A father and son are standing on a beach, with the father stating that one day all of it will belong to the son.
description.panel2: The son questions whether that includes a nearby stinky, dead whale.
description.panel3: The father confirms, suggesting that the son is also stinky.
description.panel4: The whale suddenly regurgitates a massive pile of garbage.

emotion: The author uses humor and exaggeration to cause readers to think about the relationship between humans and nature, expressing a sense of humor while calling on everyone to love animals.

title: (in)dependent

description.panel1: The cashier tells a person that the cashier only accepts cash or checks, while she has a debit card in her hand.
description.panel2: The person approaches her dad and asks for the money.
description.panel3: The cashier again explains that she is 70 cents short.
description.panel4: The person approaches her dad again, still asking for the money.

emotion: The author describes the struggle of asking parents for financial help when money is tight, highlighting the relationship between seeking independence and relying on parents for support.

title: The Source of Promblems

description.panel1: A bird is standing on a rock, looking at something in the water.
description.panel2: The bird bends down to look at its reflection in the water, appearing surprised.
description.panel3: A close-up of the bird's face with wide eyes, saying, "Oh, it's just you."
description.panel4: A more distorted reflection of the bird in the water, with the text, "The source of all my problems."

emotion: The author is illustrating that individuals often see themselves as the root of their own problems, highlighting how self-perception can impact one's view of personal issues.

title: Fries on the Pier

description.panel1: Two seagulls are standing on a rocky shore.
description.panel2: One seagull asks the other where they think they are headed, the second seagull responds it's going to get some fries on the pier.
description.panel3: The first seagull elaborates, questioning the broader, existential meaning of their destination and the purpose of consciousness.
description.panel4: The second seagull maintains its focus on getting fries, suggesting it might be related to the philosophical query.

emotion: The author uses the contrast between the perspectives of the two seagulls to humorously juxtapose basic, straightforward desires with profound existential questions, demonstrating that even the most profound exploration cannot be separated from everyday life.

title: Majestic.

description.panel1: A yellow character asks a pink character why mountains are majestic while looking at a distant mountain.
description.panel2: The pink character explains that it is because mountains are very tall.
description.panel3: A bird, standing on very long stilts, asks the yellow and pink characters if it is also majestic.
description.panel4: The two characters enthusiastically agrees, saying, "Absolutely."

emotion: The author humorously satirizes the phenomenon of only focusing on appearance and not caring about substance.

title: Hermit Crab.

description.panel1: A hermit crab expresses frustration about being a hermit and decides to come out of its shell.
description.panel2: The hermit crab warmly greeted the other crab and said, "WADDUP, FELLOW CRAB!"
description.panel3: This crab directly told the hermit crab that "You are not a real crab, dude. You are living a lie."
description.panel4: The hermit crab returned to its shell dejectedly.

emotion: The author emphasizes that when people try to change their identity or behavior, it may bring great difficulties and potential rejection.

title: I am Alive

description.panel1: A bird says it has moments of clarity when it is alone.
description.panel2: The bird notes that it hears music in its head that perfectly matches its movements.
description.panel3: The bird reflects that even though it doesn't always have autonomy, it possesses individuality.
description.panel4: The bird acknowledges that it is alive.

emotion: The author suggests that lonely moments may bring a clear and profound sense of existence by describing the words of this bird.

title: Audience Laughter

description.panel1: A speech bubble saying "PSSST!" is the only thing visible.
description.panel2: A pilot leans over with a friendly expression and asks, "Hi there, do you know how to fly a plane?"
description.panel3: The passenger reacts in shock and confusion, exclaiming, "WHAT?! NO!! DON'T YOU?", while the pilot clarifies he was just making small talk.
description.panel4: The pilot looks exasperated and comments to another passenger, "Sheesh! And they say I lack basic social skills!"

emotion: The author presents a humorous scene where a pilot appears next to a passenger, causing panic and reminding readers to pay attention to their identity and take responsibility.

title: Put It There, Pal

description.panel1: Two characters, Jim and Mitch, warmly greet each other.
description.panel2: Jim asked Mitch to "put're there, pal!" to place Mitch's hand on his shoulder.
description.panel3: Mitch agreed to Jim's request and placed his hand on Jim's shoulder.
description.panel4: Jim began to cry, but received Mitch's comfort.

emotion: The author emphasizes the warmth of friendship and emphasizes that some small kindness from others can bring unexpected happiness and comfort.

title: Printers

description.panel1: A person is frustrated because the printer is broken again.
description.panel2: The person is panicked because they have a deadline in half an hour.
description.panel3: The person comments that printers are trying to destroy the human race.
description.panel4: In a spaceship, a printer communicates to someone unknown that the human has discovered their secret.

emotion: The author conveys a sense of humor through humorous twists and turns, making readers laugh heartily.

title: Romantic Evening

description.panel1: A man in a pink top is admiring the arrangement and describing it as romantic, with a witch standing behind him.
description.panel2: He notices all the candles on the floor.
description.panel3: Then they admire a beautiful floor painting.
description.panel4: The man suddenly realizes something and says, "Wait a minute." The witch behind him pulls out a knife.

emotion: The author sets up a seemingly romantic scene, but upon closer inspection, some elements suggest it could actually be something dangerous, suggesting that you should be vigilant when you go out and always pay attention to your safety.

title: the Aluminium Hat

description.panel1: A person wearing an aluminum hat states that they wear it so nobody can read their mind.
description.panel2: The person continues by saying that nobody knows their inner secrets and thoughts.
description.panel3: Another person, sitting across from the first, asks if they want another beer.
description.panel4: The first person sits silently, holding their cup, seemingly surprised or contemplative.

emotion: The author humorously portrays the futility of trying to prevent mind-reading as the first person's desire for another beer is easily predicted by the second person without any need for mind-reading abilities. This suggests that some of our thoughts and desires may be more transparent than we believe.

title: midnight meeting

description.panel1: A person is sleeping peacefully in bed when a noise, "Pap," is heard.
description.panel2: The person, now awake and curious, looks at a mysterious lump on their bed with a puzzled expression.
description.panel3: The person got up and turned on the bedside lamp to examine the lump more closely.
description.panel4: The mysterious lump was revealed to be a spider, causing her to scream “AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA”, while the spider simply said “Hi”.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the fear and surprise that many people experience when they find an unexpected spider in their bed at night, conveying a sense of humor.

title: Schools Out

description.panel1: Two polar bears are seen playing video games together by the ocean.
description.panel2: An adult polar bear approaches the two young bears, announcing that there is no school today because the building has melted.
description.panel3: The two young polar bears continue to play their video games, seemingly indifferent to the news.
description.panel4: The two little polar bears suddenly stood up and cheered, raising their arms to celebrate. In the background, there was a fainted polar bear floating on the sea.

emotion: The author used the humorous anthropomorphic description of polar bears to hint at the harm caused by global warming and remind readers to pay attention to environmental issues.

title: Good References.

description.panel1: An interviewer asks the interviewee if they have any references.
description.panel2: The interviewee nervously responds with a misquoted line from a famous movie, "Luke, I'm your father."
description.panel3: The interviewer corrects the interviewee's quote by stating, "I'm sorry, but it's actually 'No, I am your father.'"
description.panel4: The frustrated interviewer dismisses the interviewee by saying, "You can leave now."

emotion: The author uses humorous scenes to highlight the awkwardness that can arise when someone misuses a reference in a formal setting.

title: Notification Notifications

description.panel1: The little blue character representing social media asks the big blue character: "Are you sure you don't want notifications no?"
description.panel2: The big character calmly replies "Yes."
description.panel3: The little character asks again: "Okay... what about nowm thought?" The big character confirms again: "Yes!"
description.panel4: The little character insists: "Okay... but what abou-" and is interrupted by the big character who shouts angrily: "Fucking yes! Holy shit!"

emotion: The author humorously describes the attitudes of the characters in the comics towards social media, expressing that social media notifications have a great impact on the characters' lives and are annoying.

title: Perfect

description.panel1: A bird is starting to build a nest and optimistically saying, "This nest will be perf-".
description.panel2: The bird makes a mistake in building the nest and says, "Ah, dammit".
description.panel3: The bird is looking frustrated as it continues to struggle with the nest.
description.panel4: The bird gives up, saying, "Well fuck this".

emotion: The author humorously describes the feeling of wanting to give up when encountering certain unexpected difficulties, and satirizes those who cannot persevere in completing their predetermined goals.

title: Unconscionable.

description.panel1: A blue character is sitting at a table with a salad and a slice of pizza in front of them.
description.panel2: An angel suggests that the character should pick the salad because it's healthy, tasty, fresh, and guilt-free.
description.panel3: A devil suggests that the character should maybe eat the pizza instead.
description.panel4: The angel is annoyed as the character decides to eat the pizza.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the internal conflict people often face when choosing between healthy and unhealthy food options, and how temptation can often win over good intentions.

title: Juicing.

description.panel1: A character is ready to start creating something but wants to drink some juice first.
description.panel2: The character discovers that their juice bottle is empty.
description.panel3: The character asks their dog, Barnaby, if he knows what happened to the creative juices.
description.panel4: The dog stood next to a painting it had just finished and answered "no".

emotion: The author humorously abstracted inspiration into the entity of "creative juice", suggesting that even a dog can create a good painting after drinking it, reflecting the importance of inspiration to creators.

title: brrrrrrrrt

description.panel1: A goose begins to address a group, saying "Hey everyone, I know this is sappy..."
description.panel2: The goose continues, trying to express how beautiful it is when they all fly together, but is interrupted by another goose making a loud noise (BRRRRT).
description.panel3: The interrupting goose apologizes, saying "Haha oops, sorry, Fran" as the other geese laugh.
description.panel4: The initial goose (presumably Fran) also laughs, showing camaraderie among the group.

emotion: The author emphasizes the humorous side of trying to share sincere emotions in a group setting, but being humorously interrupted by someone, which in turn creates a more humorous atmosphere and expresses the author's praise for friendship.

title: Sweat the Small Stuff.

description.panel1: A character aggressively tells a small task to "Fuck off!"
description.panel2: The character confidently walks past an enormous task.
description.panel3: The character cowers in fear and sweats as they stand next to the huge task.
description.panel4: The character pleasantly addresses the small task, saying, "Let's get you taken care of little guy."

emotion: The author satirizes the irrational behavior of procrastinating on small, manageable tasks while being overwhelmed by larger ones.

title: Iced.

description.panel1: A girl tells a boy holding a drink, "Chris, I think you're my one true love."
description.panel2: The girl continues, "I want to be with you forever and ever," while the boy listens.
description.panel3: The girl notices the boy's drink and asks in shock, "Wait... are you drinking milk... with ice cubes?"
description.panel4: The boy narrates sadly that the girl left that day and never came back.

emotion: The author humorously exaggerates how seemingly minor differences or quirks, such as putting ice cubes in milk, can suddenly affect a relationship and lead to unexpected results.

title: All is My Cat

description.panel1: The title "Things I've Often Mistaken for My Cat" is presented along with an illustration of a cat.
description.panel2: An ice cream sundae is depicted.
description.panel3: A pile of drugs and unpaid parking tickets are shown.
description.panel4: A life-sized cardboard cut-out of the cat is displayed.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the absurdity and exaggeration of mistaking unrelated objects for their cats, expressing the author's love for their pet cat.

title: cool

description.panel1: A person wearing glasses and dark clothes is praised by another character, who calls her "cool!"
description.panel2: The character being praised appears happy.
description.panel3: The person wearing glasses continues to smile happily, while the other character appears a little hesitant.
description.panel4: The person wearing glasses also maintains a higher expression, and the other character is "a little weird" and "not very good", and then hurriedly says goodbye.

emotion: The author depicts a scene where overreacting to others' compliments can lead to a drastic change in others' attitudes, suggesting that readers should not be too enthusiastic in their daily lives.

title: Old.

description.panel1: Two cavemen are talking, with one asking if the other remembers the cave painting of the guy with a spear.
description.panel2: The second caveman responds enthusiastically, saying that the painting is the best.
description.panel3: The first caveman reveals that the painting was made 20 years ago, and the second caveman reacts with surprise.
description.panel4: Both cavemen express feeling old upon realizing how much time has passed since the painting was made.

emotion: The author humorously conveys the theme of aging and the passage of time, showing that even though aging objectively exists, people are still reluctant to accept it.

title: Music

description.panel1: One bird asks another if they've been listening to anything good lately.
description.panel2: The second bird enthusiastically talks about a new, experimental song by a band.
description.panel3: The first bird responds by mentioning ambient-anthro music that includes unusual noises like chainsaws and traffic.
description.panel4: The second bird agrees, but makes a sarcastic comment about not liking certain parts of it.

emotion: The author humorously uses birds imitating human discussions to illustrate conversations about musical preferences, emphasizing that everyone's views on music are highly subjective.

title: If Only

description.panel1: The first person complains that every time he tries to get closer to the second person, the second person pulls away from him.
description.panel2: The second person replies that the opposite is true.
description.panel3: The second person goes on to say that every time he tries to get closer to the first person, the first person pushes him away.
description.panel4: The two characters appear separated in a vertical manner, one floating above the other, expressing the desire to be together.

emotion: The author highlights the miscommunication or mutual misunderstanding in a relationship, where both people feel that their attempts to get closer to each other are thwarted by the other, leading to their separation.

title: Deep Breath

description.panel1: One character expresses feeling overwhelmed, and the other advises taking a deep breath.
description.panel2: The overwhelmed character takes a deep breath, and the other instructs him to hold it.
description.panel3: The overwhelmed character is struggling to hold his breath while being told not to stop.
description.panel4: The other character, seemingly calm, reassures that it will all be over soon.

emotion: The author is humorously illustrating that although taking a deep breath is often suggested as a way to calm down, the situation can become more stressful if the advice is taken too literally.

title: Great Job

description.panel1: A person happily compliments another person by saying, "Hey! Great job, by the way!"
description.panel2: The person being complimented responds with, "Thanks!" while the first person walks away.
description.panel3: The person who was complimented thinks to themselves, "I have no idea what I did..."
description.panel4: The person, now with a tear, continues their thought, "...but I really needed that."

emotion: The author illustrates how a simple compliment can significantly impact someone's emotions, providing them with encouragement even if they don't fully understand the reason behind the praise.

title: The Universe.

description.panel1: A pink character stands with his hands on his hips and says, "The universe doesn't care about me, and I find that comforting."
description.panel2: The character says confidently, "Because I am free to take my ...", and a voice in the sky says, "I do care about you"
description.panel3: The character looks scared, and the voice in the sky says, "I care a lot."
description.panel4: The character starts sweating in fear.

emotion: The author emphasizes that when a person gets a response he doesn't expect, he will be very flustered.

title: Thrown Off

description.panel1: The dog returns the ball to a person and says, "Here, I brought your ball back."
description.panel2: The person responds with "You're welco-" and throws the ball again before finishing the sentence.
description.panel3: Both the dog and the person are silent, with the dog sitting still.
description.panel4: The dog breaks the silence with a confused and annoyed expression, saying, "The fuck, dude?"

emotion: The author challenges the common sense of humans about dogs, that is, whether dogs are willing to pick up things that people deliberately throw out, which makes readers who have done similar actions in life smile knowingly.

title: Im Outta Here

description.panel1: A house expresses disgust by saying "EWW..."
description.panel2: The house declares, "I DON'T WANNA BE A PART OF THIS," while starting to shake.
description.panel3: The house appears to be taking off from the ground, propelled by smoke.
description.panel4: The house completely leaves the scene, leaving only a bed and two people on it.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts the anthropomorphic house evacuating from the scene, representing the author's desire to escape or stay away from an unpleasant environment.

title: Merry Spankmas

description.panel1: A man in a black hat and black coat greets his daughter, saying "Merry Christmas, sweety" while the daughter eagerly opens present next to the Christmas tree.
description.panel2: The daughter discovers that her present is a dead body and nervously calls out, "Um... Dad?"
description.panel3: The man in the black hat portrays a man in a white shirt who should buy his daughter "present, not 'present'".
description.panel4: The man in the black hat humorously comments, "Let me guess, these are all 'present'" as he notices that all the present on the Christmas tree are similar to the ones his daughter has opened.

emotion: The author uses black humor and wordplay to satirize the concept of gift giving, interpreting "gifts" in a grotesque and literal way to create a shocking Christmas scene.

title: Got the Goods

description.panel1: A character sets off a security alarm as they pass through it.
description.panel2: A security officer reassures the character, saying, "You're good, dude. Don't worry."
description.panel3: The character looks unsure and questions, "I'm good?"
description.panel4: The character is lying in bed contentedly and says, "I'm good."

emotion: The author conveys the idea of ​​comfort and overcoming anxiety. The character initially feels doubtful after triggering the alarm, but ultimately feels safe and reassured after being comforted by the security guard, showing how small affirmations can make a day.

title: Back to Basics.

description.panel1: A person sits at a table, looking frustrated, wondering why it is so hard to make new friends despite trying everything.
description.panel2: Her child excitedly tells her that he made two new friends at school today.
description.panel3: The person looks confused and looks at her child.
description.panel4: On the playground, the person is swinging with one of her child's friends, and another of her child's friends asks her child "Is that your mom?"

emotion: The author humorously describes the contrast between adults and children about making friends, expressing the author's nostalgia for the carefree days of childhood.

title: Virtual Reality

description.panel1: A sad koala is sitting next to a squirrel and saying, "I'm sad."
description.panel2: The squirrel suggests, "Maybe VR could help," and hands the koala a virtual reality headset.
description.panel3: The koala wears the VR headset and says, "Wow."
description.panel4: The koala, now appearing on the moon with the VR headset, says, "Now I'm sad on the moon."

emotion: The author expressed that while virtual reality can change the environment, it doesn't necessarily address or resolve underlying emotional issues such as sadness.

title: Slight Breeze

description.panel1: A bird is in the bushes, and it hears a rustling sound, thinking "This is it. It's my time."
description.panel2: The bird panics, saying it's not ready and reflecting on its life, expressing regret.
description.panel3: The bird realizes it's just a beetle causing the sound.
description.panel4: A gentle breeze passes by, and the bird, still in the same spot, repeats, "This is it. It's my time."

emotion: The author humorously depicts the bird's overreaction to a harmless situation, illustrating how we can sometimes misinterpret minor events as significant life moments.

title: Lake-side Gallery

description.panel1: A bird stands near a body of water and welcomes viewers to the "Lake-side Gallery."
description.panel2: The bird mentions that the gallery has a lot of great artwork.
description.panel3: The bird shows some fossils and admits they are unsure who made those.
description.panel4: The bird speculates that the art is about "two little weirdos being friends."

emotion: The author uses humor and the bird's perspective to emphasize the value of friendship and implies the magic of nature. Some things in nature have high artistic value, calling on readers to pay attention to nature.

title: dog bed

description.panel1: A girl and her father are looking at a dog bed, and the girl thinks it looks comfortable and suggests that her father should buy it.
description.panel2: A stranger asks if the bed is for her or the dog, and the girl says it is for the dog, but her father says it is for her.
description.panel3: The girl looks awkwardly at her father.
description.panel4: The girl looks awkwardly at the stranger.

emotion: The author describes a humorous and slightly awkward moment, highlighting the awkwardness that sometimes occurs in parent-child interactions.

title: Life and Lemons

description.panel1: A character labeled "Life" gives a bag of lemons to another character with the phrase, "When life gives you lemons, make lemonade."
description.panel2: The character with the lemons is left sitting alone with the bag and the word "Later..." is shown.
description.panel3: The character labeled "Life" returns to this character.
description.panel4: The character labeled "Life" angrily demands, "Where the fuck is my lemonade?!"

emotion: The author humorously describes the proverb about doing the best you can in difficult situations (making lemonade out of lemons), and the twist at the end conveys a sense of humor.

title: Against the Wind.

description.panel1: A boy excitedly shows his friend his new kite.
description.panel2: The boy's kite begins to fly away, and both boys appear distressed.
description.panel3: The boys try to hold onto the kite as it is being pulled away by the wind.
description.panel4: One boy begs the sky not to take the kite away while the other struggles to hold on.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the frustration of losing control of a precious object (the kite) for some reason, praising the comical but determined attempts to keep it.

title: A Human

description.panel1: An alien child asks their parent if they can get a human.
description.panel2: The parent responds with a firm "No."
description.panel3: The parent explains that humans kill each other for stupid reasons.
description.panel4: The parent also mentions that human poop smells horrible, leaving the child disappointed.

emotion: The author humorously criticizes certain human behaviors and tendencies, highlighting the unpleasant aspects of humanity.

title: Deal with Crap

description.panel1: The father tells his son, "One day, all this will be yours, son."
description.panel2: The father and son look at a cluttered, messy garage filled with junk.
description.panel3: The father and son stand quietly, processing the statement.
description.panel4: The father comments, "Yeah. Life is short. I'm not going to have time to deal with all this crap."

emotion: The author humorously highlights the burden of inheriting unnecessary and overwhelming clutter, while also suggesting that life is often too short for people to deal with such trivial burdens.

title: The Mighty Cyamites

description.panel1: A green character prays for help with his math homework.
description.panel2: The Mighty Cyamites, introduced as the God of Beans, appears.
description.panel3: The character clarifies that "You do not need help with beans, do you?"
description.panel4: The God of Beans proceeds to help with the math problem involving trigonometry.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts the unexpected uses of gods, emphasizing that at certain moments, what comes into play is often not the main skills that one possesses, but something more mundane and not taken seriously.

title: Jumper

description.panel1: A pink character on a building rooftop yells "I'M GONNA JUMP!" while a blue character with a megaphone watches from a lower level.
description.panel2: The pink character makes a small hop in place.
description.panel3: The pink character stands on the rooftop with a satisfied expression and arms raised.
description.panel4: The blue character says "NICE JUMP" through the megaphone, and the pink character responds with "THANKS!"

emotion: The comic shows the impact of certain scenes on certain small actions, and the final twist emphasizes how ordinary actions can be made interesting through dramatic accumulation.

title: Mean Clouds

description.panel1: A cloud proudly declares that it rained on a wedding and found it awesome.
description.panel2: Another cloud questions why the first cloud hates humans so much.
description.panel3: A man is lying in a field, looking at the clouds, and laughs at the second cloud, saying "And, this one looks like a fat whale."
description.panel4: The cloud in the picture has a sad expression.

emotion: The author emphasizes that people should pay attention to their attitude towards strangers. Even if the other party has good intentions, if you cannot give a positive response, it will cause your relationship to gradually deteriorate.

title: forever young

description.panel1: A man tells the girl with glasses that she needs a parent's signature, but the girl says she's over 18.
description.panel2: The man insists that the parents need to do something, but the girl says she's independent.
description.panel3: Another man asks what high school they attend, and the girl says she graduated not long ago.
description.panel4: The girl shows the man her ID, who checks it skeptically, then nervously says it's real.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the struggle of someone who is seen as younger than their actual age and needs to repeatedly prove their adulthood, expressing the author's desire to stay young.

title: Fowl Play

description.panel1: Two characters, a man and a woman, stand next to a decorated Christmas tree, and the woman is frustrated that the Christmas decorations have already been put up before Thanksgiving.
description.panel2: The man complains that since Black Friday starts earlier each year, there may eventually be no Thanksgiving holiday; this makes the woman look worried.
description.panel3: The woman says that greedy corporations are ruining Thanksgiving, and the man nods in agreement.
description.panel4: A turkey in winter clothes stands next to a sign that says Black Friday sales start on Thanksgiving, and comments that greedy corporations are indeed to blame.

emotion: The author uses humor to critique the commercial encroachment on Thanksgiving by highlighting how early Christmas decorations and earlier Black Friday sales are diminishing the holiday.

title: What Motivates You?

description.panel1: A person responds to a microphone interview by stating that they are motivated by money.
description.panel2: Another person shares that their motivation comes from their passion for their craft.
description.panel3: A third person says their motivation is to become a better person.
description.panel4: The final person intensely declares that they are motivated by pure spite, aiming to prove haters wrong and make them suffer.

emotion: The author highlights the various motivations that drive people, ranging from financial gain and passion to self-improvement and spite. The diverse responses showcase the different personal reasons behind people's actions and achievements.

title: Big Picture

description.panel1: A smaller tree looks at a bigger tree and thinks, "I'll never be as big as that tree."
description.panel2: The smaller tree appears sad and thinks, "Why do I even bother?"
description.panel3: A bird lands on the smaller tree and starts building a nest.
description.panel4: The bird sits in the nest and happily thinks, "I fucking love this tree."

emotion: The author expresses that despite feeling inferior or inadequate, everyone has unique qualities and can be appreciated and loved for who they are.

title: Crazy World

description.panel1: A squirrel comments on how the world is full of crazy people.
description.panel2: Another squirrel advises accepting that others have different viewpoints.
description.panel3: The first squirrel skeptically questions this advice.
description.panel4: The first squirrel concludes that everyone else is out of their mind except for itself.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the idea that people often think they are the only sane ones, while viewing everyone else as irrational or crazy.

title: Always

description.panel1: A frog expresses that it has had a horrible week.
description.panel2: A turtle responds by saying that it can always be worse.
description.panel3: The frog questions if the turtle's comment is a threat.
description.panel4: The frog demands the turtle to answer its question.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the pessimistic view of a bad situation and the unintended consequences of trying to gain comfort, highlighting the miscommunication and misunderstandings in the interaction.

title: japanese class

description.panel1: A student is demonstrating her Japanese speaking skills to her classmates.
description.panel2: The teacher compliments the student on her natural Japanese pronunciation and expresses surprise and confusion, which makes the student feel very proud.
description.panel3: The teacher mentions that some students will soon attend an anime exhibition in Japan.
description.panel4: The teacher enthusiastically asks the students to bring back photos of the anime exhibition, which makes the student nervous.

emotion: The comic humorously emphasizes the fear of some people about taking photos, reflecting the appearance anxiety of modern people.

title: Something in My Eye

description.panel1: Two birds are sitting on a branch, and one bird says, "Ugh I think there's something in my eye."
description.panel2: The second bird responds, "Hmm let me see?"
description.panel3: A close-up view of the first bird's eye reveals an insect near it.
description.panel4: The second bird, looking relaxed, says, "Looks fine to me."
