emotion: The comic humorously illustrates how a perceived problem can be overlooked or ignored by someone else despite clear evidence, highlighting a disparity in perception and attention to detail.

title: Febuary

description.panel1: A bird enjoys the winter scenery and cheerfully says "Ahh... February".
description.panel2: The bird confidently pronounces "February".
description.panel3: The bird struggles with the pronunciation and says "Febberary...".
description.panel4: The bird further mispronounces it as "Frebrury".

emotion: The author humorously highlights the common difficulty people face in pronouncing the word "February".

title: Not All Heroes ...

description.panel1: A bird is perched on a branch, and a speech bubble states, "Not all heroes can fly."
description.panel2: A close-up view of the bird with a speech bubble that says, "Not all heroes are sexy."
description.panel3: Another close-up of the bird with a speech bubble declaring, "Not all heroes solve problems with violence."
description.panel4: The bird is shown again with a speech bubble proclaiming, "Not all heroes ate half their body weight in peanuts that one time."

emotion: The author is humorously suggesting that heroism can come in many forms, and not all heroes necessarily fit traditional or extraordinary stereotypes.

title: Changing

description.panel1: A bird expresses excitement about seeing how the world will change over the next 50 years.
description.panel2: Another bird responds bluntly, suggesting the first bird won't live long enough to see it.
description.panel3: The first bird is taken aback and appears speechless.
description.panel4: The second bird clarifies that they mean changes in the design of bird feeders, hoping for one that keeps seeds dry, and both birds laugh.

emotion: The author uses humor to highlight the mundane frustrations of birds (like soggy seeds) while playfully addressing the significant span of 50 years in a lighthearted and relatable way.

title: What Should We Eat?

description.panel1: A goose is eating trash while another goose suggests that it probably shouldn't eat that every day.
description.panel2: The trash-eating goose retorts that if it tastes good, it's good for you.
description.panel3: The trash-eating goose tries to claim it's biology but begins to choke.
description.panel4: The trash-eating goose, still choking, insists it's science while the other goose says no.

emotion: The author is humorously illustrating the idea that just because something tastes good, it doesn't necessarily mean it's good for you, and blind disregard for health can lead to unhealthy consequences.

title: A Bit of A Big Deal

description.panel1: A lush garden with green plants and a tree full of purple flowers is shown.
description.panel2: A close-up of purple flowers and green leaves on the tree.
description.panel3: A zoom-in view of an ant on a large green leaf.
description.panel4: A close-up of the ant saying, "I'M A BIT OF A BIG DEAL."

emotion: The author humorously highlights the ant's perspective, emphasizing that big or significant things can often have a different scale and meaning when viewed from another point of view.

title: Be a Tree

description.panel1: A bird clears its throat and begins speaking.
description.panel2: The bird asks if anyone has figured out the perfect answer or formula, addressing birds, bugs, trees, etc.
description.panel3: The bird expresses a desire to be a tree.
description.panel4: The bird clarifies, saying it wants to be a tree both mentally and physically.

emotion: The author humorously conveys a longing for simplicity and stability, suggesting a wish to live a peaceful and grounded life like a tree.

title: Misplace Something

description.panel1: A bird is perched on a branch, looking down with a concerned expression, saying, "I've misplaced something."
description.panel2: The bird stands upright on the branch, expressing, "I've been feeling lost without it."
description.panel3: A close-up of the bird's face as it says, "I don't remember what it is."
description.panel4: The bird is seen from a distance, continuing to search among the branches, stating, "But I'm starting to feel better by looking for it."

emotion: The author conveys a message about the importance of the journey or process of searching, suggesting that the act of looking for something, even if forgotten, can bring a sense of purpose and healing.

title: Intelligent

description.panel1: Two crows discuss whether rabbits are intelligent, with one crow asking if the other thinks they are intelligent, and the second crow responding affirmatively.
description.panel2: The first crow questions if the second crow has ever seen rabbits using a stick, to which the second crow replies in astonishment.
description.panel3: The first crow condescendingly advises the second crow that using a new tool does not make one a genius.
description.panel4: The second crow interrupts by poking the first crow with the stick and shouts "HEY!"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the notion that intelligence is not simply about using tools and that one should not be condescending or proud based on minor achievements.

title: Fran was a good bird

description.panel1: A bird excitedly greets another bird, named Fran, acknowledging that Fran is alive. Another bird responds affirmatively, indicating the second bird, named Pat, is also alive.
description.panel2: Fran notices two more birds on a wire and acknowledges their survival as well, mentioning that one of them is named Pip.
description.panel3: Fran identifies even more birds as being alive, using names (like Skylar) to point them out.
description.panel4: The birds exchange farewells, expressing positivity about their encounters and wishing each other well, ending with a goodbye for Fran.

emotion: The author expresses a theme of gratitude and appreciation for life and companionship, emphasizing the importance of recognizing and cherishing the presence and well-being of friends and loved ones.

title: Synchronizing Our Wings

description.panel1: Two birds are discussing synchronizing their wing movements; one bird looks unsure about the idea.
description.panel2: They begin to synchronize their wing flaps, with one bird counting down, and the other bird appears worried.
description.panel3: The synchronization attempt quickly goes awry, causing the worried bird to panic.
description.panel4: The initiating bird is enthusiastic about trying again while the other bird looks exasperated.

emotion: The author expressed that attempts at synchronization or coordination with others can be challenging and may not always go smoothly, but persistence and a positive attitude can encourage retrying despite initial failures.

title: Live in the Moment

description.panel1: Two woodpeckers are conversing on a tree; one advises living in the moment because life is short.
description.panel2: The woodpecker continues, warning against the many potential unexpected dangers, like being electrocuted.
description.panel3: The list of dangers continues, including being hit by a truck or getting eaten by a wolf, while both woodpeckers continue pecking.
description.panel4: The conversing woodpecker advises caution to avoid snapping its neck, breaking its beak, or getting an infection, contradicting its earlier advice to live worry-free.

emotion: The author uses humor and irony to illustrate the conflict between the idea of living in the moment and the tendency to succumb to fears and anxieties about potential dangers.

title: Yummy

description.panel1: The bird is eating a caterpillar and exclaims "OOO!"
description.panel2: The bird questions the caterpillar, asking if it is poisonous and how it tastes.
description.panel3: The bird continues consuming the caterpillar, commenting on its appearance and taste.
description.panel4: The bird contemplates various unusual characteristics of the caterpillar as it eats.

emotion: The author illustrates the curiosity and thought process of a bird while eating a caterpillar, humorously contrasting the bird's enjoyment of the meal with bizarre and potentially alarming attributes of the caterpillar.

title: Be a Human

description.panel1: A bird expresses a wish to be a human for one day.
description.panel2: The bird starts to explain the reason for this wish, beginning with the desire to be able to walk anywhere.
description.panel3: The bird is interrupted by the thought that humans cannot actually walk anywhere.
description.panel4: The bird falls silent, pondering this realization.

emotion: The author conveys the idea that the freedoms and abilities we often envy in others might not be as boundless as we imagine, reflecting on the limitations and complexities of human life.

title: The Power of Flowers

description.panel1: A hummingbird sips nectar from a flower, thinking "Haha you fools!"
description.panel2: The close-up of the hummingbird's face shows it saying, "No one knows."
description.panel3: Another close-up of the hummingbird's face, now saying, "No one understands."
description.panel4: An extreme close-up of the hummingbird's eye with it thinking, "The power of flowers."

emotion: The comic illustrates a humorous take on a hummingbird's perspective, emphasizing a dramatic sense of secret knowledge about the importance and impact of flowers, likely highlighting the fundamental relationship between flowers and pollinators.

title: Lucky Bird

description.panel1: A bird finds a bunch of flowers and is excited, thinking that someone will be lucky to receive them.
description.panel2: The bird flies in the sky cheerfully while holding the flowers.
description.panel3: The bird arrives near its nest, which is situated in a tree.
description.panel4: The bird happily sits in the nest, surrounded by the flowers.

emotion: The author conveys a sense of simple joy and contentment that can be found in small gestures and the beauty of nature. The comic showcases the bird's happiness in finding and bringing flowers to its nest, implying that the small act of gathering the flowers brings happiness to itself.

title: A Trick

description.panel1: Two birds are perched on a branch, and one bird asks the other if they want to see a trick to open something, while pecking at it.
description.panel2: The other bird responds, expressing skepticism about the nature of the question.
description.panel3: The first bird insists that the trick is great and will be useful, claiming it has changed their life.
description.panel4: The skeptical bird comments that the first bird sounds cult-like, and the first bird mentions that there is a brief ceremony to perform.

emotion: The author uses humor to depict a situation where someone is skeptical about an overly enthusiastic recommendation, highlighting how some recommendations can come off as cult-like or too good to be true.

title: What Just Happened

description.panel1: A bird emits a loud "EEEEE!" sound.
description.panel2: The bird sees a group of ducklings and reacts affectionately, calling them "fwuffy wittle duckwings" and expressing a desire to have one.
description.panel3: The bird watches the ducklings, staying silent.
description.panel4: The bird looks bewildered and says, "What just happened."

emotion: The author is expressing an unexpected twist where the bird's sudden outburst is followed by an equally sudden moment of confusion, illustrating the unpredictable nature of reactions and emotions.

title: PFFFFF

description.panel1: Two owls are conversing, with one stating, "You spend too much time alone," to which the other responds dismissively, "PFF! SAYS WHO?"
description.panel2: A close-up of the responding owl shows it repeating "PFFF" and questioning, "WHO SAYS THAT?"
description.panel3: The first owl, presumably named Jenny, is now looking closer and asks, "DID YOU SAY THAT, JENNY?"
description.panel4: The responding owl looks confused and introspective, asking, "WAIT. DID I SAY THAT?"

emotion: The author humorously portrays the irony of an individual dismissing a comment about them spending too much time alone, only to realize they may have internalized and self-directed the observation. It highlights the theme of self-awareness and the subconscious internal dialogue.

title: Enhance

description.panel1: A bird reflects that it used to think it lived in an ugly place and believed it would need to search far to find beauty.
description.panel2: The bird remarks that it has learned the concept of beauty is not about looking further away, but rather looking closer.
description.panel3: An image focuses closely on a detailed, vibrant green leaf among some foliage.
description.panel4: A close-up view shows the bird observing the leaf with interest and appreciation.

emotion: The author emphasizes that beauty can often be found in our immediate surroundings if we pay closer attention and look more carefully.

title: Baby Corn

description.panel1: A bird happily proclaims that spring is the cutest season because of baby leaves.
description.panel2: The bird continues to express delight over baby flowers.
description.panel3: The bird looks at a nest full of baby birds chirping for food, appearing less enthusiastic.
description.panel4: The bird overlooks baby plants, which also look attractive.

emotion: The author is expressing the contrast between the bird's initial enthusiasm for the signs of spring in nature and its overwhelmed reaction to the responsibilities that come with having baby birds.

title: Somewhere That Way

description.panel1: A duck is seen swimming and says, "I don't really think much about my future."
description.panel2: The duck continues swimming and says, "But I have a general idea of where I'm headed."
description.panel3: The duck slightly turns and keeps swimming without saying anything.
description.panel4: The duck now faces a new direction and says, "Somewhere that way."

emotion: The author is illustrating the humorous notion that while one may not have a detailed plan or direction for the future, having a vague sense or direction might suffice.

title: Whoa

description.panel1: A bird stands near water, saying "Heh heh, water comes in..."
description.panel2: The bird looks down at the water near its feet, starting to say "Water goes ou-".
description.panel3: The bird suddenly sees its webbed feet and says "Whoa".
description.panel4: The bird realizes it's looking at its own feet and comments, "Oh it's my feet".

emotion: The author humorously depicts a bird's moment of confusion and realization about its own webbed feet, emphasizing a playful and whimsical observation of everyday life.

title: Nothing Bad

description.panel1: One fish asks another if they ever feel like nothing bad ever happens.
description.panel2: A larger fish suddenly attacks, grabbing the questioning fish.
description.panel3: The attacked fish, now in the jaws of the larger fish, yells for vengeance.
description.panel4: The remaining fish, visibly unaffected, responds that they probably won't avenge them.

emotion: The author illustrates the abruptness of life's misfortunes and the often indifferent reactions of others to our plights.

title: Fierce

description.panel1: A goose asks another what it is doing, and the other responds that it is putting on paint to look intimidating.
description.panel2: One goose tells the other to focus on the eyes while applying the paint.
description.panel3: The goose remarks how the red paint resembles blood and laughs mischievously.
description.panel4: The painted goose looks in the mirror and confidently says it feels fierce.

emotion: The author uses humor to depict the transformation of a character through a simple act (applying paint) and highlights how appearance can impact one's confidence and perception of fierceness.

title: Collection

description.panel1: A crow expresses excitement about the melting snow revealing hidden treasures.
description.panel2: The crow eagerly offers to show its collection.
description.panel3: The crow displays an assortment of collected items.
description.panel4: The crow realizes a specific item, the "jiggly squibbler," is missing from its collection.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the crow's delight in finding and collecting various objects, and its dismay upon noticing one is missing, highlighting both the bird's curiosity and attachment to its collection.

title: Do not Give up

description.panel1: A squirrel discusses how life occasionally drops unexpected burdens, symbolized by a walnut hitting your head.
description.panel2: The squirrel explains that these misfortunes catch you off guard, leading to confusion, reflection, and self-pity.
description.panel3: The squirrel emphasizes the importance of not giving in to despair and maintaining resilience in the face of adversity.
description.panel4: The squirrel is seen determinedly digging at the base of a large stump, demonstrating its resolve not to give up.

emotion: The author conveys a message of resilience, illustrating through the squirrel's experience that despite unexpected challenges and moments of despair, it is crucial to keep persevering and not give up.

title: Who You Are

description.panel1: Two birds are sitting on a branch, with one bird commenting on the crazy weather.
description.panel2: The second bird agrees, mentioning how the weather can change sometimes.
description.panel3: The second bird suddenly questions whether the first bird thinks talking about the weather is below them, asking what they would prefer to discuss.
description.panel4: The first bird appears confused and states that they don't know who the second bird is.

emotion: The author humorously expresses a situation where a simple, innocuous conversation about the weather takes an unexpectedly serious and perplexing turn, leading to a humorous confusion about identity and intent.

title: Listen

description.panel1: The text suggests that listening carefully to the natural world allows you to hear it communicating.
description.panel2: The text asks if you can hear what the natural world is saying.
description.panel3: The natural world is depicted as saying "Shhh... don't worry".
description.panel4: The final message from the natural world is "You don't matter".

emotion: The author portrays the natural world as indifferent to individual human concerns, suggesting a humbling perspective on human significance.

title: Do not Say Anything

description.panel1: A close-up of a blue jay looking thoughtful.
description.panel2: The blue jay is seen from behind as it gazes into a forest during autumn.
description.panel3: The blue jay is saying "Sometimes it's best not to say anyth—" but then trails off with a speech bubble filled with ellipses.
description.panel4: The blue jay is now alone, further into the forest, surrounded by fallen leaves.

emotion: The author conveys the idea that sometimes silence is more appropriate, capturing a moment of quiet contemplation in the serene setting of nature.

title: Hunting Midflight

description.panel1: A bird speaks to the reader, saying that learning about hunting midflight is not as exciting as it sounds.
description.panel2: The bird describes flying around and spotting a target below.
description.panel3: The bird instructs to tuck in the wings.
description.panel4: The bird then explains to increase the speed to 200 kilometres per hour.

emotion: The author humorously explains the streamlined and efficient process of a bird hunting midflight, juxtaposing the seemingly mundane explanation with the thrilling speed involved.

title: Too Much Cheese

description.panel1: A bird says, "Hey, I have an important thing to ask: Do you...uh."
description.panel2: The bird continues with, "This is hard for me to say..."
description.panel3: The bird looks thoughtful, taking a breath.
description.panel4: The bird asks, "Is there such a thing as 'too much cheese'?"

emotion: The author humorously depicts the bird's intense internal struggle to ask a seemingly trivial yet pressing question, highlighting the absurdity of overthinking minor concerns.

title: Where is It

description.panel1: A worried squirrel is frantically searching for something while thinking, "Shit shit shit, where is it?? She’s going to kill me…"
description.panel2: Close-up of the squirrel holding an object and wondering, "Is this it?"
description.panel3: A second squirrel questions the first one, saying, "Is that a joke? A walnut?? My ass is on the line here!”
description.panel4: The first squirrel responds, "Hey I'm just trying to help! And I'm hungry," adding that they also have no idea what a switchblade looks like.

emotion: The author humorously portrays a misunderstanding between two squirrels, one of whom is under pressure and trying to find a critical item, while the other, through a mix of helpfulness and ignorance, creates a comedic tension, pointing out the absurdity and simplicity of their situation.

title: A Little Intimidating

description.panel1: An owl begins to acknowledge that sometimes it can appear intimidating.
description.panel2: The owl is interrupted and changes its expression, looking surprised.
description.panel3: The owl's face becomes intensely focused and concentrated.
description.panel4: The owl apologizes, explaining it got distracted by the sound of a mouse under a snowbank a kilometer away.

emotion: The author highlights the owl's exceptional hearing ability in a humorous way, suggesting that its intense focus might contribute to it seeming intimidating.

title: Draw A Goose

description.panel1: The title of the comic is "How to Draw a Canada Goose."
description.panel2: An image of a simple, geometric sketch showing the basic shapes to outline a Canada Goose.
description.panel3: A more detailed drawing of a Canada Goose, showing realistic features and shading.
description.panel4: The detailed Canada Goose is now colored with a speech bubble saying "No way I'll end up like my parents!"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the evolution of drawing a Canada Goose from simple shapes to a detailed illustration and then adds a playful twist by giving the goose a speech bubble that reflects a common sentiment of youthful rebellion or desire for individuality.

title: Game Over

description.panel1: The title screen of the game "Hummingbird Simulator" is displayed with a hummingbird and flowers in the background.
description.panel2: The game begins with a "GO" message, showing the hummingbird flying toward a flower.
description.panel3: The hummingbird is shown making a squeaking noise ("MEEP NORP"), still flying near flowers.
description.panel4: The screen shows "Game Over" with a message stating "You died. Remember to eat every 1.5 seconds!"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the difficulty and relentless nature of a hummingbird's life, emphasizing the constant need for food by depicting it as an impossible video game challenge.

title: Red

description.panel1: Two birds notice and comment on the vibrant foliage.
description.panel2: The birds describe the foliage with specific and elaborate color descriptions.
description.panel3: The birds continue to use various poetic terms to describe the red foliage.
description.panel4: One bird simplifies the description, stating that the foliage is "really red."

emotion: The author humorously conveys how individuals can use elaborate language to describe something simple, highlighting the contrast between complex and straightforward communication.

title: Go Down

description.panel1: A seagull is flying high in the sky and remarks, "Whoa, I'm pretty high."
description.panel2: The seagull is still flying and says "Haha."
description.panel3: The seagull is flying even higher, approaching large clouds.
description.panel4: The seagull, now very high in the sky, says "...I'd like to go down now, please."

emotion: The author expresses a humorous situation where the seagull, initially thrilled by flying at a high altitude, realizes the discomfort or fear of being too high and desires to descend.

title: When I Find That Field Again

description.panel1: One raccoon enthusiastically shows off a bunch of bananas and their companion reacts with disgust.
description.panel2: The raccoon holding the bananas is incredulous while the other raccoon turns away, expressing wariness of food they've never seen growing.
description.panel3: The skeptical raccoon sarcastically asks if the other raccoon has ever seen cheese puffs in their natural habitat, implying they’re processed.
description.panel4: The first raccoon firmly asserts that they have indeed seen such a field and demands not to be mocked for it, while walking away with a haughty tail flick.

emotion: The author uses humor and the interaction between the raccoons to highlight skepticism and incredulity towards processed foods and artificial concepts, contrasting them with natural produce.

title: When I am Gone

description.panel1: A bird is perched on a branch among the leaves of a tree.
description.panel2: The bird wonders what will happen when it's gone and whether its unique perspective will be missed.
description.panel3: The bird looks contemplative while sitting on the branch, gazing at the sky.
description.panel4: The bird concludes that everyone will miss its puns.

emotion: The author expressed a humorous take on self-reflection, portraying a bird concerned about its legacy, ultimately finding comfort in the thought that its puns will be missed.

title: A Bad Omen

description.panel1: A vulture warns, "Beware!"
description.panel2: The vulture, perched on a tree, predicts, "Storm's-a-brewin'."
description.panel3: The vulture reacts to sunlight shining through the clouds with "Ugh! Sun!"
description.panel4: The vulture comments, "That's a bad omen."

emotion: Through this comic, the author humorously depicts a vulture misinterpreting good weather (sunlight) as a bad omen, likely highlighting the bird's typically pessimistic or gloomy nature.

title: I Have No Idea

description.panel1: A crow is describing the taste of something it is eating, mentioning hickory, elderflower, and something sharp, while making smacking noises.
description.panel2: The same crow continues describing the taste, now mentioning chokecherry and fresh spruce tips, with more smacking noises.
description.panel3: Another crow joins the first one and looks quizzically at it.
description.panel4: The second crow asks if the first one knows what it was eating, and the first crow replies that it has no idea.

emotion: The author is humorously highlighting the contrast between the detailed, sophisticated description given by the crow and its ultimate cluelessness about what it was actually eating, poking fun at the pretense of overly detailed food critiques.

title: Ideal Speciman

description.panel1: A bird, who appears to be boastful, states that it didn't ask to be this beautiful.
description.panel2: The bird continues its boastful attitude, rhetorically asking if the observer thinks it chose its crimson color of perfection.
description.panel3: The bird highlights another feature, its prominent crest, asking rhetorically about it.
description.panel4: The bird concludes by stating that it's hard to be humble when one is the archetype of handsome.

emotion: The author illustrated a humorously conceited bird, using exaggeration and self-praise to highlight the difficulty of staying humble when one possesses notable attributes.

title: Announcement

description.panel1: A crow announces to everyone it has an important announcement.
description.panel2: The crow tries to get the attention of sparrows that are chirping and moving around.
description.panel3: The crow tries to get the attention of a rabbit that is chewing on grass.
description.panel4: The crow expresses frustration, stating it would be done by now if everyone had been listening.

emotion: The author highlights the difficulty of getting attention and effectively communicating when others are distracted or not listening.

title: The Best Home

description.panel1: A bird mentions that they saved the best home for last and points out that there will be a lot of interest in it.
description.panel2: Another bird comments on how the home seems cramped, with the first bird insisting it's "snug."
description.panel3: The first bird highlights the home's potential and great location as they sit on top of a store's sign.
description.panel4: Other birds arrive, starting a "bidding war," leading the first bird to chase them away while claiming the home.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the competitive nature of finding a desirable home, with birds anthropomorphized to reflect the challenges of the real estate market.

title: Good Day

description.panel1: A raccoon peeks out of a tree hole, thinking to itself, "Feels like one of those days...".
description.panel2: The raccoon yawns widely from the tree hole.
description.panel3: The raccoon looks out again with a relaxed demeanor and responds with "Yep".
description.panel4: The raccoon faces forward with a content expression and declares, "One of those not giving a shit days".

emotion: The author expresses the idea of having a carefree day where one is indifferent to concerns or responsibilities.

title: Cigarette Butts

description.panel1: Two pigeons are eating and one pigeon says, "Mmm!", while the other adds, "These bread-crumbs are great!"
description.panel2: The two pigeons continue to eat, and one of them says, "Haha!"
description.panel3: One pigeon starts to speak, saying, "Remember when you thought you were eating crumbs, haha, but it was cigarette butts!"
description.panel4: The other pigeon stops eating and with a horrified expression, says, "No," with cigarette butts hanging from its beak.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a moment of realization and disgust, emphasizing the confusion and unpleasant surprise of mistaking cigarette butts for food.

title: Give up

description.panel1: A bird is pecking at the ground, looking for food.
description.panel2: The bird stops pecking and looks forward thoughtfully.
description.panel3: The bird continues to stand still, appearing contemplative.
description.panel4: The bird suddenly exclaims, "WHEN DID I GIVE UP ON MY DREAMS?"

emotion: The author is expressing a moment of existential reflection, where the bird suddenly questions its mundane routine and realizes a disconnect from its dreams and aspirations, prompting a self-reflective and philosophical inquiry about its life choices and purpose.

title: Really Don't Understand

description.panel1: A raccoon asks, "You know what I really don’t understand?" to another raccoon.
description.panel2: The first raccoon responds with "What’s that?" while the second raccoon looks contemplative.
description.panel3: The second raccoon states, "Well, like the VAST MAJORITY of all things," indicating a broad sense of confusion.
description.panel4: The first raccoon humorously replies, "I try not to think about it too much," suggesting avoidance of overwhelming thoughts.

emotion: The author expresses the notion of being overwhelmed by the complexity and vastness of understanding everything in life, and the humor and relief in choosing not to overthink it.

title: Draw A Squirrel

description.panel1: The title of the comic is presented: "How to Draw A Squirrel."
description.panel2: A basic geometric outline of a squirrel is drawn with simple shapes.
description.panel3: A more detailed, realistic drawing of a squirrel is shown.
description.panel4: The detailed squirrel is now shaded black and has a speech bubble saying, "It wasn't raining when Noah built the ark!"

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the progression from a basic drawing to a detailed one, ending with the squirrel imparting a piece of doomsday survival wisdom, expressing an unexpected and humorous twist showing the squirrel's preparedness mindset.

title: Parkour

description.panel1: A fluffy cat is shown jumping forward with the word "PARKOUR" in the background.
description.panel2: The same cat is laying on a windowsill next to a plant, with "PARKOUR" written above.
description.panel3: The cat is sitting under a chair, surrounded by the word "PARKOUR."
description.panel4: The cat is lying on its back on a set of stairs with the word "PARKOUR" nearby.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the discrepancy between the cat's mundane activities and the dynamic action implied by the word "parkour," highlighting the contrast between expectation and reality.

title: Fledgling

description.panel1: A bird exclaims "BAH!" in apparent frustration or anger.
description.panel2: The bird reminisces, saying "When I was young! When I was a fledgling..."
description.panel3: The bird trails off, saying "... you know..."
description.panel4: The bird declares, "We all agreed that everything was perfect and that absolutely nothing would change for any reason whatsoever!"

emotion: The author expresses a sentiment of nostalgia and resistance to change, showcasing how the bird yearns for the simplicity and perceived perfection of its youth, while comically highlighting the unrealistic expectation that nothing would ever change.

title: She's the One

description.panel1: A skunk is wandering through the snow in front of a row of buildings.
description.panel2: The skunk stops and looks up, gasping in surprise.
description.panel3: The skunk sees a black and white cat sitting on a balcony, looking down.
description.panel4: The skunk declares, "That skunk's going to be my girlfriend," mistakenly identifying the cat as another skunk.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates misconceptions in romantic interest, showing how the skunk mistakenly identifies a cat as a potential romantic partner due to similar colors and patterns.

title: What A Gorgeous Day

description.panel1: A bird peeks out from behind a tree trunk and admires the beautiful day.
description.panel2: The bird comments that it should make the most of the gorgeous day.
description.panel3: The bird starts to burrow back into the tree trunk, making a "SHUF SHUF" sound.
description.panel4: The bird completely retreats back into the tree trunk, disappearing from view.

emotion: The comic humorously highlights the contrast between the bird's intention to take advantage of the beautiful day and its actual action of staying inside, perhaps poking fun at the tendency to make grand plans but ultimately doing nothing.

title: Music Pig

description.panel1: A pig is singing "LA LA LA".
description.panel2: The same pig continues singing "OINK OINK OINK".
description.panel3: The pig sings "NOTHING BAD...".
description.panel4: The pig finishes with "EVER HAPPENS TO PIGS!", appearing cheerful and oblivious.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a pig blissfully unaware of the potential dangers it may face, ironically singing about how nothing bad ever happens to pigs. This creates a juxtaposition between the pig's naive optimism and the reader's likely awareness of the real fate of pigs.

title: Ice Cream

description.panel1: One bird asks another if they like ice cream while standing beside an ice cream cone.
description.panel2: The second bird responds saying they don't really like ice cream, and the first agrees.
description.panel3: Both birds stand silently looking at the ice cream cone.
description.panel4: One bird suggests they should give the ice cream back because a child is crying.

emotion: The author explores the theme of the bird's indifference to ice cream and the moral consideration of returning something to its rightful owner, emphasizing empathy and doing the right thing.

title: ppffffssssss

description.panel1: A squirrel is eating something and is making a munching sound.
description.panel2: The squirrel farts, making a "PFFFFSSSSS" sound, and looks a bit amused.
description.panel3: The squirrel looks embarrassed and starts sweating.
description.panel4: The squirrel continues to look embarrassed and distressed as the fart sound lingers.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a relatable and awkward moment through the actions and expressions of the squirrel, using it as a comical representation of an accidental and embarrassing fart in public.

title: Call Your Girl Friend

description.panel1: A bird is singing "CHEERY CHEERY CHEERY" while standing on the ground.
description.panel2: The bird continues singing "CHIRP CHIRP CHIRP."
description.panel3: The bird pauses and looks to the side.
description.panel4: The bird suddenly screams loudly, "CALL YOUR GIRLFRIEND."

emotion: The author humorously captures a bird’s transition from typical chirping to unexpectedly shouting a humorous phrase, likely to surprise and amuse readers. The comic plays on the sudden shift from normal bird behavior to an anthropomorphic action for comedic effect.

title: And Then ...

description.panel1: A young penguin confidently states, "I am going to be young..."
description.panel2: The penguin continues, "And beautiful..."
description.panel3: The penguin concludes with, "Forever!"
description.panel4: The penguin, now older and chubbier, is shown with the text "And then..."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the inevitability of aging and how youthful expectations of eternal beauty often contrast sharply with the realities of growing older.

title: NO

description.panel1: Two raccoons are sitting and leaning on the side of a dumpster.
description.panel2: The raccoon on the left asks, "Do you ever think about... you know..."
description.panel3: The raccoon on the right responds with a speech bubble saying, "NO."
description.panel4: The raccoon on the left clarifies, "No, you don't know or no, you don't think about it?" The raccoon on the right repeats, "NO."

emotion: The author uses humor to depict a scenario where one character tries to ask a serious or thoughtful question, but the other character dismisses it with a concise and repeated refusal, highlighting a lack of interest in deep conversation or perhaps a humorous evasion.

title: The Lowest Echelon

description.panel1: A seagull starts a conversation about prejudice and stereotypes.
description.panel2: Another seagull agrees and expresses its frustration at being considered the lowest in the animal kingdom.
description.panel3: The seagulls are seen pecking at the ground, one of them picks up something small.
description.panel4: One seagull reveals that it is eating a cigarette butt when asked what it’s eating.

emotion: The author uses humor to comment on the irony of seagulls discussing stereotypes and prejudice while engaging in behavior that reinforces their negative reputation, highlighting societal tendencies to stereotype based on actions.

title: Faaaaalllll

description.panel1: A branch with a few leaves is shown.
description.panel2: The same branch is depicted again, now without any leaves.
description.panel3: A bird lands on the branch abruptly, causing it to bend and making an onomatopoeic sound "SKAAAAAATK".
description.panel4: The branch is now straight again, with the bird having flown away.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the sudden arrival and departure of a bird, showcasing the action and the disturbance it causes to the otherwise peaceful scene.

title: This Is My Tree

description.panel1: A gray squirrel climbs a tree while a brown squirrel calls out, "Wait! I'm not gonna hurt you!"
description.panel2: The brown squirrel approaches the gray squirrel, saying, "I just wanna tell you something..."
description.panel3: The brown squirrel gestures towards the tree and continues, "Sh sh sh... listen... listen to me... see this tree?"
description.panel4: The brown squirrel now looks aggressive and declares, "This is my fucking tree."

emotion: The author humorously highlights the territorial nature of animals using exaggerated language, depicting a confrontation over territory in an unexpected, humorous manner.

title: Like A Big Fat Tank

description.panel1: A person is eating enthusiastically, surrounded by snacks like chips and cheese.
description.panel2: Another person observes and comments, "Wow, you're a tank."
description.panel3: The eating person reacts with confusion and surprise, saying, "Whhaaat?"
description.panel4: The eating person, now teary-eyed, asks, "Like a big fat tank?"

emotion: The author highlights a misunderstanding that leads to an emotional reaction, showing how a comment intended as a compliment can be perceived negatively due to differences in interpretation.

title: Commuter

description.panel1: The man is stuck in heavy traffic during his commute.
description.panel2: The man turns to look at something off-panel.
description.panel3: The man exclaims in frustration, "Oh man!"
description.panel4: The man reveals his identity as one of the "miserable 401 commuters."

emotion: The author wants to highlight the common frustration and sense of shared misery experienced by people who regularly commute on Highway 401.

title: LinkedIn

description.panel1: A man greets a cat with "Hi there, kitty" while standing outside.
description.panel2: The man looks closely at the cat and asks if it knows someone named "Lady".
description.panel3: The man, with a surprised expression, asks the cat if it is on LinkedIn.
description.panel4: Both the man and the cat are shown close-up with the man stating "Networking".

emotion: The author uses humor to parody social networking on LinkedIn by showing a man trying to "network" with a cat in the same way people connect with each other on the platform.

title: Drawing

description.panel1: A person spots raccoons near a trash can and exclaims "Oh! Hey!"
description.panel2: The person compliments the raccoons, saying they like drawing their "kind."
description.panel3: The raccoons appear angry, growling and hissing at the person.
description.panel4: The person introduces themselves to the raccoons as "Josh: Ambassador to the Beasts!"

emotion: The author humorously depicts a situation where a person tries to be friendly and complimentary towards raccoons, only to be met with hostility, and then confidently declares their role as mediator or communicator with animals.

title: The First Thing

description.panel1: Two rabbits are sitting in the snow, and one says "BRRRR".
description.panel2: The other rabbit asks, "WANNA KNOW THE FIRST THING I'M GONNA DO IN THE SPRING?!”
description.panel3: A large bird suddenly swoops down and grabs the rabbit who asked the question.
description.panel4: The remaining rabbit sits alone looking at the spot where the other rabbit was taken.

emotion: The author humorously portrays the unpredictability of life and how plans can be abruptly interrupted by unforeseen events.

title: Fresh

description.panel1: A man is looking at a Subway sign that says "eat fresh."
description.panel2: The man then looks at another sign advertising "fresh bananas."
description.panel3: He observes a truck with the slogan "Always Fresh" on its side.
description.panel4: The man reflects, thinking to himself, "I always forget that word doesn't mean anything."

emotion: The author is expressing skepticism about the overuse and potential meaninglessness of the word "fresh" in advertising, implying that it has become a vague or empty marketing term.

title: Grape

description.panel1: The character questions what some dried-up grapes might be.
description.panel2: The character wonders what the dried-up grapes taste like.
description.panel3: The character tastes the dried-up grapes and immediately reacts with disgust, saying "BLEGH."
description.panel4: The character, looking unhappy, comments "GROSS."

emotion: The author humorously explores the theme of curiosity leading to an unpleasant discovery.

title: Hah! Got me!

description.panel1: A man sitting on the edge of his bed looks annoyed and says, "Oof. Stupid bladder."
description.panel2: The man flushes the toilet after using the bathroom.
description.panel3: The man bends over the sink and repeatedly tells himself, "Don't look in the mirror. Don't look in the mirror."
description.panel4: The man looks at the mirror and is startled to see a reflection with a menacing appearance, exclaiming, "AHH Dammit..."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the man's internal struggle and inevitable fall into temptation to look at the mirror against his own advice, leading to a startling and humorous consequence.

title: Cleaning with fire is just better!

description.panel1: A man opens his refrigerator and looks inside with a concerned expression.
description.panel2: The contents of the refrigerator are shown, including visibly expired and moldy food.
description.panel3: The man scratches his chin, contemplating what to do about the messy and spoiled state of his refrigerator.
description.panel4: The same man is seen standing outside, arms crossed, with the refrigerator on fire in the background, expressing a sense of finality in his solution.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates an extreme and impractical solution to a common household problem of dealing with a dirty and spoiled refrigerator, emphasizing the lengths to which someone might go to avoid cleaning it properly.

title: KICK!

description.panel1: A person discovers a dead bird on their doormat and a cat sitting nearby, looking guilty.
description.panel2: The person yells that they had previously asked for no birds.
description.panel3: The person takes the dead bird outside.
description.panel4: The person kicks the bird into an area filled with a large number of other dead birds.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the frustration and the seemingly futile attempts of someone trying to control a cat's natural hunting behavior, as well as the accumulation of the cat’s hunting "gifts."

title: Level up

description.panel1: A man in a suit stands in an office, looking professional and poised.
description.panel2: The man receives a firm handshake from another person, indicated by sound effects.
description.panel3: A "LEVEL UP!" screen appears, similar to video games, showing that the handshake gained the man "+50 exp" and a "Firm Grip Bonus."
description.panel4: The man enthusiastically looks at a box that says "MBA III," indicating he has unlocked a new tie as a reward in the game of work.

emotion: The author uses gaming terminology and imagery to humorously depict everyday corporate actions like handshakes as if they provide experience points and rewards, highlighting the gamified nature of modern professional achievements.

title: This was a good day

description.panel1: A construction worker looks down at a hole in the ground while standing in the rain.
description.panel2: The construction worker continues to look at the hole, seemingly lost in thought.
description.panel3: The construction worker lifts his head and looks around with a smile.
description.panel4: The construction worker laughs out loud, appearing content despite the rainy weather.

emotion: The comic illustrates the ability to find joy and satisfaction in one's work or life regardless of challenging circumstances, emphasizing a positive attitude even on difficult days.

title: A Total Bummer

description.panel1: Introduction to Ornithocheirus as a fearsome predator from over 110 million years ago.
description.panel2: Despite being known only from fossilized fragments, Ornithocheirus is described as a clever and affectionate creature.
description.panel3: Ornithocheirus is also depicted as paralyzingly indecisive, leading to a life of regret.
description.panel4: The comic concludes that Ornithocheirus was a bummer to hang out with.

emotion: The author humorously contrasts the fearsome reputation of the ancient predator Ornithocheirus with its supposed emotional traits and indecisiveness, ultimately suggesting it was not enjoyable company.

title: From cute to scary

description.panel1: A construction worker observes a bear in the distance and comments on how cute and fuzzy it looks.
description.panel2: Another person informs the construction worker that there's a bear on his land, to which he responds nervously.
description.panel3: The construction worker tries to shoo the bear away while yelling that he doesn't want it around anymore.
description.panel4: The construction worker looks concerned and injured, nursing his hand while a colleague looks on.

emotion: The author illustrates the irony and unexpected danger that arises when humans underestimate wild animals, initially seeing them as cute but ultimately recognizing their potential threat.

title: Really Beautiful Sound ?

description.panel1: A bird sitting on a branch is calling out with the words "HEY LADIES".
description.panel2: Another bird is depicted saying "SINGLE AND HORNY".
description.panel3: A different bird is chirping the sounds "DEE TEE EFF".
description.panel4: A person is standing by a window with musical notes coming through and thinks to themselves "BEAUTIFUL".

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how the vocalizations of birds, when heard by humans, are often interpreted as beautiful music, despite the fact that the actual "messages" being communicated by the birds might be crude or straightforward.

title: Best Sounds

description.panel1: A person, seemingly in a winter setting, asks rhetorically if others wish people would stop being so opinionated sometimes.
description.panel2: The person is shown silently looking around at the snowy landscape.
description.panel3: An empty, peaceful winter scene is depicted, emphasizing the quiet.
description.panel4: The same person sarcastically remarks that any idle silence seems to be a lost opportunity for people to prove they make the best sounds.

emotion: The author expresses a frustration with people constantly needing to fill silence with their opinions, sarcastically suggesting that silence is often interrupted by those eager to share their thoughts.

title: Be Better

description.panel1: Two people discuss the unusually problematic weather.
description.panel2: They acknowledge that the weather is significantly different from historical averages.
description.panel3: One person expresses difficulty accepting the heavy precipitation.
description.panel4: They find solace in the fact that seasons will change and things will get better.

emotion: The author is using a conversation about the weather as a metaphor to convey that despite current challenges or difficulties, there is hope that change is inevitable and the future holds improvement.

title: Confidence

description.panel1: A man is looking at a website analytics page while eating chips.
description.panel2: The man continues eating chips, appearing thoughtful.
description.panel3: A close-up of the website analytics page shows detailed visitor data.
description.panel4: The man, with a smug expression, says, "Maybe I should work for the NSA!"

emotion: The author humorously depicts how easily accessible and detailed website analytics can make an average person feel confident and knowledgeable, suggesting an exaggerated sense of capability, akin to that of working for a high-level surveillance organization like the NSA.

title: "Missed Connection"

description.panel1: A man and a woman are sitting next to each other on public transportation, with the man glancing at the woman.
description.panel2: The man and woman continue to sit quietly, with the man now looking straight ahead.
description.panel3: The woman gets up and leaves the bus, while the man watches her go.
description.panel4: The man smiles and thinks to himself, "I bet she's going to write a 'missed connection' about me!"

emotion: The author highlights the theme of unspoken attraction and the common fantasy that a brief, silent encounter on public transportation will lead to a 'missed connection' post, reflecting on the romanticized notion of finding love in fleeting moments.

title: Fat Legs

description.panel1: The young man is sitting on the sofa, struggling to pull up a pair of jeans.
description.panel2: The young man makes a joking comment, saying, "Heh! Well, they don't call them 'fatty jeans'!"
description.panel3: The young man looks down, appearing sad and resigned as he continues to struggle with the jeans.
description.panel4: Later, the older version of the man, now a grandfather, is walking with a cane, while a grandchild runs playfully toward him, calling "Grampa!"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the cyclical nature of life and body changes, showing how a young man's struggle with his jeans continues into old age but shifts focus to the next generation, implying acceptance and the inevitability of aging.

title: Ugh

description.panel1: A skier is frustrated by a noisy snowmobile passing by and says "ugh".
description.panel2: The skier continues skiing away, and an owl watching from a tree echoes "ugh".
description.panel3: The owl expresses annoyance by itself, saying "ugh" again.
description.panel4: The owl comments to a nearby mouse, "People? Ugh."

emotion: The comic humorously highlights how animals might share similar frustrations with human activities, emphasizing a shared sentiment of annoyance toward disturbances caused by people.

title: Advertise

description.panel1: A man sees a fruit and decides to eat it to satisfy his hunger.
description.panel2: The man removes a sticker from the fruit.
description.panel3: The man tastes the fruit and finds it sticky and unpleasant.
description.panel4: An advertisement for potato chips states, "Why risk eating anything else?" implying chips are a safer choice.

emotion: The author humorously criticizes the perception of unhealthy snacks like potato chips being portrayed as a more reliable or safer food choice compared to fresh fruits due to negative experiences.

title: I Can't Believe

description.panel1: A person is sitting alone in a café, appearing deep in thought.
description.panel2: The person is holding their head with one hand, looking distraught, and thinking, "I CAN'T BELIEVE I RAN OVER THAT DOG."
description.panel3: The person continues to look downcast, holding a cup with both hands.
description.panel4: The logo of Tim Hortons with the slogan "Always Fresh" is shown.

emotion: The author contrasts the somber, guilt-ridden mood of the person with the upbeat marketing message of Tim Hortons, possibly highlighting a sense of irony or dissonance between personal anguish and commercial environments.

title: Communication Barriers

description.panel1: A person wearing a winter hat and coat is holding a camera and tells others to "say freeze."
description.panel2: The same person asks someone named Lily to cooperate, mentioning the photos are for Nana.
description.panel3: The camera shows a close-up of two birds perched on a pine tree branch.
description.panel4: The person looks frustrated and asks the birds to stop fooling around.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the challenges and frustrations of communicating with animals, illustrating a literal miscommunication scenario.

title: Because you are so old

description.panel1: An older woman comments to a younger person that they seem like they should be in high school, saying they must always get I.D.-ed.
description.panel2: The younger person, looking thoughtful, is seen sitting in a car wearing a beanie.
description.panel3: The younger person, still contemplative, is sitting in front of a meal at home.
description.panel4: The younger person, now lying in bed, reflects aloud that the older woman must never get I.D.-ed.

emotion: The author highlights the difference in perceptions of age and the humorous assumptions people make based on appearances.

title: Be A Bird

description.panel1: A person looks at a bird and thinks, "It must be pretty great to be a bird..."
description.panel2: The bird is flying freely, and the person considers the bird's freedom from rules, jobs, school, stress, and the need to prove its own worth, as well as its lack of concept of time.
description.panel3: The person further imagines that the bird has no fears for the future and suggests that maybe it will do an unpaid internship for experience.
description.panel4: The person comments that nobody needs a plan at such a young age, noting that the bird seems to be doing fine without one.

emotion: The author expresses a humorous contrast between the carefree life of a bird and the societal pressures and expectations placed on young people, highlighting the anxiety and unrealistic demands they face.

title: It's A Bit Strong

description.panel1: Jannie serves tea to her mom, who expresses gratitude.
description.panel2: Jannie's mom hesitantly points out that Jannie is holding the sugar bowl instead of the teapot.
description.panel3: Jannie and another person look at each other in shock upon realizing the mistake.
description.panel4: Jannie's mom tries to eat the solid sugar, commenting on its strong taste and asking for maple syrup.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a mix-up in the kitchen, highlighting how mistakes can lead to unexpected and amusing outcomes.

title: Nervous presentation

description.panel1: A nervous person starts their presentation about spider reproduction.
description.panel2: A bizarre, imaginary creature enthusiastically encourages the presenter.
description.panel3: The presenter excessively sweats and the creature suggests an absurd solution.
description.panel4: The presenter looks disturbed while the audience appears indifferent or bored.

emotion: The author humorously portrays the intense anxiety and irrational thoughts a person might experience during a public presentation, as opposed to the typically indifferent reaction of the audience.

title: I Missed

description.panel1: A person is expressing frustration about how indifferent someone named Nedry was about working with dinosaurs, and another person is reacting with concern and shouting "LOOK OUT!"
description.panel2: A chaotic scene where a person is being chased by a bulldozer while another person watches.
description.panel3: The person who was being chased is now safe and laughing, saying "WHOOPS, HAHA!"
description.panel4: The person who expressed frustration in the first panel has glasses fogged up and says "I MISSED."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a situation where someone's distraction or mild frustration causes them to completely miss a potentially dangerous or chaotic event happening right beside them.

title: Dr. Scientist

description.panel1: A scientist proudly announces to an audience that he is the first successful subject of time travel, presenting himself from the future.
description.panel2: The audience reacts with amazement and surprise.
description.panel3: The scientist realizes and explains that his future self already came back to prevent him from building the time machine, deeming it a waste of his genius.
description.panel4: The crowd continues to view him as charming and intelligent, despite his admission.

emotion: The author humorously addresses the paradoxes and ego often associated with time travel narratives, while also commenting on the perception and admiration of intelligence, even when faced with contradictory information.

title: Disappearing DEX

description.panel1: A person asks Squeaky the cat if they have seen another cat named Dex.
description.panel2: A close-up of Squeaky looking curious or thoughtful.
description.panel3: The person's feet in front of a skeleton with a cat collar around its neck.
description.panel4: Squeaky is seen sitting casually with the person pondering where Dex could be while scratching the ground.

emotion: The author humorously suggests that Dex has been missing for so long that he has become a skeleton, yet the person is oblivious, continuing to search for him.

title: Different eras

description.panel1: In 2012 Canada, one character expresses surprise when another reveals they don't own a cellphone.
description.panel2: In 1692 Amuhkrica, a character nonchalantly admits to being a witch, and is met with disbelief.
description.panel3: In 1348 Europe, a character refuses to share a flea-infested bed and mentions personal hygiene, surprising the other character.
description.panel4: In 2000 BCE outside a cave, one character is shocked when another admits they don’t own a comically large club.

emotion: The author illustrates how societal norms and expectations have evolved over different historical periods, highlighting how what is considered unusual or shocking varies with time and culture.

title: And on such small paper!

description.panel1: A person is angrily trying to get a printer to work, shouting at it to print.
description.panel2: The person is extremely frustrated, cursing the printer and wanting it to suffer.
description.panel3: The printer starts to print, making mechanical noises.
description.panel4: The printed paper has the message "I'll see you there, Jekyll." in a whimsical font (Curlz MT), and the person is shocked by the silliness of the font.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the frustration people often feel with printers and how, despite the anger, the final output can be unexpectedly trivial or comical, adding to the irony and frustration.

title: Chin cleavage and ALL

description.panel1: A person exclaims that their foot is killing them.
description.panel2: Another person accuses the first person of being overly dramatic and frequently using hyperbole.
description.panel3: The first person insists their foot is "literally" stabbing them, holding a knife to illustrate the pain.
description.panel4: The second person sarcastically responds that if the first person were any more dramatic, they would die from eating their own face.

emotion: The author uses exaggerated humor to comment on how some people tend to be overly dramatic about their discomfort or pain, often using hyperbole to describe minor issues.

title: Yet so intriguing..

description.panel1: A person is kneeling over the toilet, appearing to be sick, while another person stands behind them, comforting them and saying "I know, I know".
description.panel2: The person is still kneeling over the toilet with a distressed expression, while the other person watches.
description.panel3: A cat enters the scene, and the standing person tries to shoo it away by saying "Shoo, cat".
description.panel4: Close-up of the cat, expressing its disgust, with a thought bubble saying "This bitch is disgustin".

emotion: The author humorously highlights the gross reality of dealing with a hangover, as perceived by an unimpressed cat.

title: Useful Glove

description.panel1: A single glove with text written on it stating, "I'm no good for nobody."
description.panel2: The glove is seen falling from a tree, and a bird nearby shouts, "Hey, that's not true!"
description.panel3: The glove is now shown lying on the ground.
description.panel4: The bird is seen using the glove to keep its nest and chicks warm.

emotion: The author expressed that even things that seem useless or unwanted can still have value and purpose in different contexts.

title: Hungry

description.panel1: A bird expresses irritability and wonders why they are in such a mood, and another bird offers to run a brain scan.
description.panel2: A close-up of the irritable bird who dismisses the idea of a brain scan, considering it ridiculous.
description.panel3: The second bird points out that the irritable bird is hungry.
description.panel4: The irritable bird's stomach gurgles as they realize the truth in the second bird's observation.

emotion: The author uses humor and anthropomorphized birds to illustrate the common experience of hunger affecting one's mood.

title: Answer

description.panel1: Two birds are seen pecking at something on the ground with the sound effect "MUNCH MUNCH MUNCH."
description.panel2: One bird tells the other, "I THINK THIS IS THE ANSWER TO YOUR STRUGGLES."
description.panel3: The bird that was speaking is now sitting on a large cross structure.
description.panel4: The second bird, looking confused, responds with "WHAT."

emotion: The comic uses humor to depict a scenario where a bird suggests a solution to another bird's problems, only to propose something completely unexpected and confusing.

title: Fries

description.panel1: Two crows notice a pile of fries left unattended in a park setting.
description.panel2: One crow exclaims that finding fries like this must be a trap, indicating its suspicion.
description.panel3: Despite suspecting a trap, the crow starts eating the fries and comments on them being warm.
description.panel4: Another crow, surprised by the first crow's actions, questions if it will be saving any fries for them.

emotion: The author humorously portrays crows exhibiting human-like suspicion and greed, ultimately giving in to temptation despite initial doubts.

title: Surprises

description.panel1: Two birds are walking and discussing, with one saying they are no longer interested in eating roadkill and the other agreeing, both wondering where they should go.
description.panel2: One bird suggests following them because they have an idea that can be a surprise, and the other bird agrees to follow.
description.panel3: One bird enthusiastically exclaims "YESSSSSS" with great excitement.
description.panel4: The two birds walk together with one expressing that they love surprises.

emotion: The author highlights the excitement and joy of unexpected adventures and the pleasure of anticipating pleasant surprises.

title: Intelligent Species

description.panel1: Two birds are pondering the possibility of other intelligent species existing in the universe.
description.panel2: One bird lists characteristics of an intelligent species, such as problem-solving skills, communication, social structure, and self-awareness.
description.panel3: Another bird adds the ability to fly as an essential characteristic, while the first bird agrees.
description.panel4: The first bird assertively concludes that a strong beak is also necessary for intelligence.

emotion: The author uses humor to illustrate the anthropocentric perspective of intelligence, showing how these birds view their own traits like flying and a strong beak as essential for intelligence.

title: AMIA Pattern

description.panel1: A bird is perched on a tree branch, saying "PATTERNS ARE EVERYWHERE".
description.panel2: A close-up of a bird next to a pattern on the background says "SOMETIMES YOU CAN'T SEE THEM BUT THEY'RE THERE".
description.panel3: The bird is now walking on a snail; the background shows similar patterned lines from previous panels.
description.panel4: A bird is superimposed over a repeated pattern of flying birds and it asks, "AM I A PATTERN?".

emotion: The author uses the bird's perspective to illustrate the omnipresence of patterns in the world and highlights the idea that sometimes patterns are not immediately visible but can be present in everyday life. The ending question prompts a reflection on whether the bird itself is part of the pattern, suggesting a contemplation of self and environment.

title: solipsism

description.panel1: A girl approaches her younger brother, who is sitting on the floor, and asks him what he is thinking about.
description.panel2: The younger brother responds, stating that he feels like he is the only real person in the world and everyone else is just a part of his imagination.
description.panel3: The girl appears contemplative, seemingly processing her brother's statement.
description.panel4: The girl replies frankly, stating her age as "I'm 8."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a young child's exploration of existential thoughts, specifically solipsism, juxtaposed with a straightforward, age-appropriate response that showcases the simplicity and innocence of a child's perspective.

title: can't sleep

description.panel1: The character lies in bed, unable to sleep due to feeling lonely and angsty.
description.panel2: The character decides to listen to some music to help with the sleeplessness.
description.panel3: Music starts playing, but the lyrics emphasize the feeling of being all alone.
description.panel4: The character realizes the music is not helping as the lyrics continue to highlight loneliness.

emotion: The author conveys the irony of trying to alleviate loneliness with music, only to have the lyrics of the songs reinforce the feelings of loneliness.

title: cutting onions

description.panel1: The character is holding a knife and an onion, thinking "cutting onions can't be that bad."
description.panel2: The character starts chopping the onion and comments, "This isn't bad at all."
description.panel3: The character pauses, looking slightly uncomfortable with a blank expression.
description.panel4: The character has teary eyes and is sniffing, obviously affected by the onion.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the common experience of underestimating the effects of cutting onions, only to be proven wrong as the character inevitably starts tearing up from the onion's vapors.

title: bedtime

description.panel1: A person lies in bed with a satisfied expression and thinks about being glad they got to bed early tonight.
description.panel2: The person starts to realize they now have a lot of time before falling asleep.
description.panel3: The person continues to be in the same sleeping position, now contemplating what to do with the extra time.
description.panel4: The person is wide-eyed, indicating they are now overthinking or reflecting on life.

emotion: The author humorously captures the ironic situation of going to bed early only to end up overthinking or reflecting on life, preventing actual sleep.

title: wasteful

description.panel1: A person laments being still hungry despite finishing their meal, while two other people express that they are full.
description.panel2: A burger is thrown into a trash can because someone can't eat another bite.
description.panel3: The hungry person's eyes widen in shock after seeing the burger being thrown away.
description.panel4: The hungry person sits at the table with a distressed expression, thinking about how they could have eaten the discarded burger, while the others remain indifferent.

emotion: The author highlights the frustration and wastefulness of discarding food, particularly in front of someone who is still hungry.

title: homest ... ?

description.panel1: Two individuals are excitedly discussing a webcomic called "Homestu," expressing their enjoyment of it.
description.panel2: A person is trying to remember and clarify what "Homestu" is, showing uncertainty.
description.panel3: At an anime convention, the individuals notice many cosplayers dressed as characters from "Homestu," commenting on its recent increase in popularity.
description.panel4: One individual observes that the cosplayers look different than they remember, implying a change or evolution in how the webcomic is portrayed or perceived.

emotion: The author is highlighting the phenomenon of how quickly fandom culture can evolve and how popular interpretations or portrayals of a given media, like a webcomic, can change over time, sometimes diverging significantly from original memories or concepts.

title: 5:00 am

description.panel1: A tired person yawns and decides it's bedtime, expressing they are very sleepy.
description.panel2: The person then notices something on their desk, with a questioning "Hm?" expression.
description.panel3: The person looks at a note that reads "work I forgot to do" with an "oh" reaction.
description.panel4: Despite the situation, the person optimistically thinks that at least they can make a comic out of this, while still looking exhausted.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the experience of feeling tired and ready for bed, only to remember unfinished work, and then turning the situation into inspiration for a comic, highlighting a blend of exhaustion, forgetfulness, and creative adaptation.

title: locked

description.panel1: The character enters from an open door and thinks about testing the door's lock.
description.panel2: The character tests the outside door and notes that it actually locks.
description.panel3: The character discovers that the gate outside is also locked.
description.panel4: The character realizes they are locked outside as they look up at the house from the backyard.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the character's unintended consequence of locking themselves outside while testing the effectiveness of the door locks.

title: without u

description.panel1: Friends are seen doing something fun while ignoring the person standing alone.
description.panel2: The person standing alone expresses a desire to join in.
description.panel3: The person admits feeling too awkward to ask to join.
description.panel4: The friends notice the person’s awkwardness and offer help.

emotion: The author highlights the difficulty and awkwardness some people face when trying to join social activities, as well as the importance of friends being attentive and inclusive.

title: IRL meeting

description.panel1: Two individuals face a pair of artists at a signing table, asking if they are the creators of the comic '4-Panel Life.'
description.panel2: One of the individuals expresses admiration for the comics, and one of the artists gratefully acknowledges the compliment.
description.panel3: The artist is silent with a blank expression while the individual continues to stand there.
description.panel4: The artist feels stressed and internally exclaims that they have nothing interesting to say, feeling apologetic.

emotion: The author highlights the anxiety and social pressure that creators might feel during in-person interactions with their audience, particularly when they struggle to find engaging things to say in response to compliments.

title: sleep over

description.panel1: A girl informs others that she sometimes snores very loudly and tells them to feel free to wake her up if it happens.
description.panel2: Her friends reassure her not to worry about it, joking that they can't even imagine her snoring loudly.
description.panel3: Later, the girl is seen sleeping while snoring loudly, shown by a big "SNOOORE" sound effect.
description.panel4: Her friends are awake and obviously disturbed by the loud snoring, wondering how such a loud sound can come from someone so small.

emotion: The author humorously portrays the girl's loud snoring habits, emphasizing the difference between expectations and reality regarding the intensity of her snoring and its surprising effect on her friends.

title: not u

description.panel1: A character waves happily towards someone or something off-screen.
description.panel2: Another character, happy and confident, raises their hand to reciprocate the wave.
description.panel3: The initial character meant to wave at someone behind the current character, leaving the current character confused and embarrassed.
description.panel4: The character who misunderstood the wave is now blushing and awkwardly scratching their head as the other two characters interact in the background.

emotion: The author expressed a common social misunderstanding where someone mistakenly believes they are the intended recipient of a friendly gesture, leading to an awkward and humorous situation.

title: cosplay confidence

description.panel1: A person with glasses, wearing a black shirt, looks determined and says, "OK. THAT'S IT. I'M GOING TO DO THIS."
description.panel2: The same person is shown partially undressed, putting on a turquoise wig and a costume.
description.panel3: Now fully dressed in a cosplay outfit, they confidently proclaim, "I DO WHAT I WANT. I WEAR WHAT I WANT."
description.panel4: They nervously stand in public, sweating and uncertain, surrounded by abstract figures, thinking, "ah..."

emotion: The author expresses the internal struggle and mix of confidence and anxiety that can accompany wearing a costume or engaging in cosplay, highlighting the contrast between initial determination and the vulnerable feeling of being judged in public.

title: first day

description.panel1: A young girl is confused on her first day of elementary school, asking "Where am I?"
description.panel2: The same girl, now older on her first day of middle school, is holding a map and asking, "Where are my classes?"
description.panel3: The girl, now in high school, holds the map silently with a confused expression.
description.panel4: On her first day of college, the girl, holding the map, meets another student who greets her as "FRIEND," and she responds with "ok."

emotion: The author illustrates the progression of a girl's school experience, showcasing her initial confusion and gradual adaptation to new environments until she reaches college, where making friends becomes a natural conclusion.

title: hair

description.panel1: The character explains that they spend a lot of time styling their hair.
description.panel2: The character apologizes for being late, saying they had to fix their hair, and another character remarks that it isn't very noticeable.
description.panel3: On another occasion, the character says they didn't have time to style their hair, but the other character compliments it enthusiastically.
description.panel4: The character appears confused as the other character comments on how much time it must have taken to style their hair, despite the character not having brushed it at all.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the unpredictability of how one's hair can be perceived, regardless of the actual effort put into styling it.

title: receiving compliments

description.panel1: The character politely responds to a compliment with "Thank you!"
description.panel2: The character, now slightly flustered, responds to another compliment with "T-Thank you!!"
description.panel3: The character becomes even more embarrassed and responds with "Thank you..."
description.panel4: The character, overwhelmed, becomes speechless and blushing.

emotion: The author illustrates how receiving multiple compliments can make a person increasingly flustered and eventually speechless from embarrassment.

title: glasses

description.panel1: A person is sleeping at night with various items, including a pair of glasses, scattered around the bed.
description.panel2: In the morning, the person wakes up and reaches for their glasses, confused about their location.
description.panel3: The person, with an annoyed expression, holds their head in frustration.
description.panel4: The person finds that they were already wearing their glasses, creating a humorous realization moment.

emotion: The author humorously highlights a common, relatable experience of misplacing items, only to realize they were already in use, emphasizing the sometimes absurd nature of human forgetfulness.

title: grocery shopping

description.panel1: The girl notices a sale while shopping with her dad, who is wearing a hat labeled "DAD".
description.panel2: The girl closely examines the item on sale.
description.panel3: The girl realizes she lost track of her dad but feels confident she can find him because of his hat.
description.panel4: The girl looks confused in a sea of people wearing similar hats.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a common shopping scenario where a child loses sight of their parent and realizes that finding them might not be as easy as initially thought when many shoppers look alike.

title: the paramount

description.panel1: A tired and lost individual asks a stranger for directions to "The Paramount" and is told it is a few blocks away.
description.panel2: The individual, still tired, walks through the area, passing by several people.
