description.panel3: The individual arrives at a building labeled "The Paramount."
description.panel4: The individual is confused and asks for directions again, where they are told "The Paramount" is half a mile away.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the confusion and frustration often experienced when following vague or unclear directions.

title: cents

description.panel1: The character with glasses asks if the other character wants an 18 or 20-ounce option, to which the other character quickly responds with "20 please!"
description.panel2: The character with glasses asks why 20 ounces, and the other character explains that it's just a few extra cents, so it makes sense.
description.panel3: The character with glasses explains the pun "make cents" while starting to laugh.
description.panel4: The character with glasses and the other character continue to laugh at the pun.

emotion: The author uses a play on words with the phrase "make sense" and "make cents" to create a pun that leads to shared amusement between the characters.

title: virtual roller-coaster

description.panel1: A person excitedly explains to someone else that they can program the settings for the virtual roller coaster, leading to the other person's shocked response.
description.panel2: The excited person reassures the worried individual by saying they will set it to be not scary.
description.panel3: The excited person confidently assures the hesitant individual that they know what they are doing, while the other person remains silent.
description.panel4: Both individuals are seen in the virtual roller coaster, which turns out to be extremely intense and scary, contrary to the reassurance; the confident individual laughs it off, saying "haha whoops".

emotion: The author humorously illustrates that even when someone confidently assures others about their control over a situation, things may not always turn out as planned, resulting in unexpected outcomes.

title: conversation

description.panel1: A message pops up saying "Hello Jen!" and Jen thinks, "Oh, someone's trying to talk to me?"
description.panel2: Jen looks determined, thinking "I gotta say something good, something interesting...!"
description.panel3: The panel is blank except for the message "Jen: xD".
description.panel4: Jen facepalms, seemingly frustrated or disappointed with her response.

emotion: The author expresses the anxiety and pressure of trying to come up with an interesting response in a conversation, but ultimately defaulting to a simple or unsatisfactory reply, followed by self-frustration.

title: bad feelings

description.panel1: The character confidently exclaims, "I CAN DO THIS YEAH!"
description.panel2: The character notices a large wave of stress approaching and reacts with concern, saying "oh no."
description.panel3: The character is beginning to be overwhelmed by the wave labeled with various negative emotions like stress, doubt, and paranoia, saying "not again."
description.panel4: The character is fully submerged in the wave, surrounded by stress, doubt, paranoia, and low self-esteem, and is asking "why."

emotion: The author is illustrating the struggle with recurring negative emotions and how sudden waves of stress and doubt can overwhelm one's initial confidence and positivity.

title: buggy

description.panel1: One character expresses disgust at a slug while the other worries it might get squished.
description.panel2: The concerned character attempts to move the slug using a stick.
description.panel3: The character struggles as the slug won't stick to the stick.
description.panel4: The other character yells in horror as they see the first character simply picking up the slug.

emotion: The author humorously portrays a person's failed attempt to carefully move a slug using a stick and the ensuing revulsion from directly handling the slug.

title: thanks for the support !!

description.panel1: The character contemplates doing something other than drawing comics about themselves.
description.panel2: They consider looking at their empty inbox as an alternative task and affirm their ability in doing so.
description.panel3: The character's inbox has sections for compliments, encouragement, and other positive messages, but it is empty.
description.panel4: They ultimately decide to draw comics forever, mentioning they will clean their room later.

emotion: The author conveys a humorous take on procrastination and self-acceptance, highlighting the character's inclination to continue drawing comics despite considering other activities.

title: well armed

description.panel1: The character wakes up in the night feeling something heavy pressing down on them.
description.panel2: The character is confused and worried, questioning what the heavy object could be, eventually realizing it is an arm.
description.panel3: Panicking, the character throws the arm away.
description.panel4: The character then realizes that the arm is their own, which had fallen asleep.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a relatable experience of waking up to a limb that has fallen asleep, initially causing confusion and panic before realizing the benign nature of the situation.

title: cosplay

description.panel1: A person excitedly says they are going to hang out with friends in costume.
description.panel2: The person is startled by a knock and realizes the postman is here.
description.panel3: They panic, thinking that normal people are not supposed to see them in their cosplay outfit.
description.panel4: The postman awkwardly hands over the package while commenting on the person's hair.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the sudden anxiety and embarrassment a cosplayer might feel when unexpectedly seen in costume by someone outside of the cosplay community.

title: spring

description.panel1: A person is being told that it's not winter anymore and it's a nice day outside.
description.panel2: The person acknowledges the statement by agreeing that it's indeed a nice day.
description.panel3: The person dramatically removes their blanket and hair tie.
description.panel4: The person expresses their love for spring.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the transition from winter to spring, emphasizing the joy and freedom that comes with warmer weather.

title: my pals

description.panel1: A character is excitedly telling someone that they should meet their friends because they are nice and will get along well.
description.panel2: The character introduces their friend to two other friends, confidently saying, "Hey guys! This is my pal!"
description.panel3: The two friends respond negatively, saying things like "You're icky" and "Go away".
description.panel4: The character, panicking, tries to reassure their friend by saying that the others were just joking and to not leave.

emotion: The author illustrates the discomfort and awkwardness that can arise when introducing friends who do not get along as expected, highlighting the discrepancy between expectations and reality in social interactions.

title: faq

description.panel1: A character named Jen is asked why they usually don't have eyes.
description.panel2: Jen comments on how often they receive this particular question.
description.panel3: Jen indicates that upon closer examination, the reason is apparent.
description.panel4: Jen reveals that they normally have no face at all, holding up a mask.

emotion: The author humorously addresses the frequent question of why a character appears faceless by revealing the unexpected and absurd reality that the character actually has no face.

title: wigged out (hahaha)

description.panel1: A person places a cleaned wig in the bathroom to dry.
description.panel2: They assure themselves they won't forget about the wig, while someone else inquires about the internet.
description.panel3: Sometime later, the person has a feeling they are forgetting something.
description.panel4: The person is startled and screams upon seeing the wig, realizing they forgot it.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the common experience of confidently telling oneself they won't forget something, only to completely forget and be surprised by it later.

title: daily life social struggles

description.panel1: A group of people are talking about something that one person hasn't seen.
description.panel2: One person in the group asks another person how many times they have seen the thing they are discussing.
description.panel3: The questioned person awkwardly admits they haven't seen it at all.
description.panel4: The group reacts with shock and surprise to the person's admission.

emotion: The author expresses the social anxiety and awkwardness one can feel when they are not familiar with a popular topic of conversation among their peers, highlighting the pressure to keep up with societal trends.

title: families only

description.panel1: A person informs others that they need to use the restroom.
description.panel2: The person finds the public restroom closed and notices a family restroom available.
description.panel3: The others tell the person they can still use the family restroom despite not being a family.
description.panel4: The person realizes they don't need to have a child with them to use the family restroom.

emotion: The author highlights a misconception that family restrooms are exclusively for those with children, emphasizing that individuals can use these facilities even if they do not have a child with them.

title: classified

description.panel1: A character confidently writes their secrets in a notebook, believing no one will find them.
description.panel2: Another character discovers the notebook and exclaims in surprise.
description.panel3: The first character decides to hide their secrets in a computer file, thinking it is secure.
description.panel4: The second character, once again, easily finds the secret file on the computer.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the idea that no matter how secure you think your secrets are, they can still be easily discovered by others.

title: common interests

description.panel1: A person expresses a wish that more of their friends liked a specific TV show.
description.panel2: A friend responds that they like that TV show.
description.panel3: The person gets very excited and enthusiastically questions the friend about their knowledge and opinions of the show.
description.panel4: The friend admits they have only seen one episode, and the person suggests they watch more, to which the friend replies noncommittally.

emotion: The comic illustrates how shared interests can lead to overly enthusiastic reactions, and highlights the disparity that can exist between a casual fan and an avid enthusiast.

title: space

description.panel1: Two people are talking about something that makes the character on the right uncomfortable.
description.panel2: The uncomfortable character pauses and looks away.
description.panel3: The character continues to look away, saying they "kinda..."
description.panel4: The character feels a sense of isolation, visualized by space imagery while the other two people fade out.

emotion: The author expresses the feeling of isolation and disconnection when uncomfortable topics are discussed.

title: coin toss

description.panel1: One character proposes to decide a matter with a coin flip and the other agrees.
description.panel2: The first character flips the coin, stating "heads I win, tails you lose."
description.panel3: The coin lands on tails, and the first character declares they win.
description.panel4: The second character realizes the trick and angrily protests.

emotion: The author humorously highlights an unfair coin toss where one person rigs the outcome to win no matter what, and the other person takes a moment to realize they were deceived.

title: drama's infatuation

description.panel1: A person appears tired as they open the door, remarking about what a long day it has been.
description.panel2: Inside, a figure labeled "Drama" sits with folded hands, saying they have been waiting.
description.panel3: The person looks serious and slightly annoyed, questioning how drama got inside.
description.panel4: Drama puts a hand on the person's face, stating they've been apart for too long and calling out "Drama" repeatedly.

emotion: The author humorously depicts how drama persistently re-enters someone's life, regardless of their efforts to avoid it, highlighting the ongoing and sometimes intrusive nature of drama.

title: bad mood

description.panel1: A cheerful character greets another by saying "HEY GUESS WHAT?"
description.panel2: The cheerful character energetically shares that they had a great day.
description.panel3: The other character, depicted with a dark cloud above their head, responds with a subdued "That's nice" indicating they are in a bad mood.
description.panel4: The cheerful character looks uncomfortable and thinks, "And this is awkward.."

emotion: The author expresses the clash of emotions when a cheerful person tries to share their happiness with someone who is in a bad mood, and the awkwardness that can arise in such situations.

title: fridge status

description.panel1: The character is pleased that their art has achieved "fridge status" for the first time in years.
description.panel2: The character questions why a simplistic drawing was chosen for display on the fridge.
description.panel3: The next day, the character sees the simplistic drawing still on the fridge, but it has been joined by another drawing.
description.panel4: The character reacts by hanging up another, more detailed piece of their art, hoping it will be considered more worthy of display.

emotion: The author expresses a humorous take on the artist’s pride in having their work displayed, but also their frustration and desire for their more serious or detailed work to be appreciated and showcased.

title: cos-shame

description.panel1: A group of people are looking at various items, and an employee asks if they need help finding something.
description.panel2: One person from the group responds that they are looking for items for cosplay.
description.panel3: The employee is confused about the term "cosplay," and another person in the group asks someone named Jen to explain.
description.panel4: Jen explains that cosplay is dressing up as cartoon characters in public, and the employee questions why someone would do that, while Jen awkwardly responds it's for fun.

emotion: The author is illustrating a humorous and awkward interaction where someone has to explain the concept of cosplay to someone who is unfamiliar with it, highlighting the difference in understanding and acceptance of niche hobbies.

title: my life

description.panel1: The character is at school surrounded by people talking and laughing.
description.panel2: The character is at home alone in front of a computer, looking neutral.
description.panel3: The character hears a notification sound while still in front of the computer.
description.panel4: The character becomes excited upon receiving a message.

emotion: The author expresses through this comic the contrast between the character's social experiences at school and the excitement and anticipation felt when receiving a message at home, suggesting a sense of loneliness and the importance of social connection even when alone.

title: Y'know ...

description.panel1: The character sees a screen showing a game titled "Mario Party" and states "I PARTY."
description.panel2: The character sees a screen showing a game with characters labeled "Dog: 'Sup bro?" and states "I SOCIALIZE."
description.panel3: The character sees a screen showing a game where objects are being rolled up and says "I ROLL STUFF UP AND THROW IT INTO SPACE."
description.panel4: The character sees a screen showing a game with a map and exclaims "I HAVE A LIFE." with a caption "And I just got one more!"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how the character substitutes real-life activities like partying, socializing, and having a life with their virtual equivalents in video games.

title: cute

description.panel1: A girl decides to dress cute today for no specific reason.
description.panel2: The girl is dressed up, saying "Yeah!" confidently and looking happy.
description.panel3: The girl's face is covered with a blindfold, and she quietly says "Yeah..."
description.panel4: The girl's entire face is hidden behind glasses and a scarf, and she says "Yeah" in a muted tone.

emotion: The author expresses the humorous contrast between the girl's initial confident decision to dress cute and the reality of covering up her face, making her less recognizable or visible.

title: 7:00 am

description.panel1: A person is in bed, covered by a blanket, with an alarm clock showing 7:00 am buzzing.
description.panel2: The person reaches out from under the blanket and touches the alarm clock, trying to turn it off.
description.panel3: The person, still mostly under the blanket, grabs the alarm clock.
description.panel4: The person puts the alarm clock inside a drawer or box, effectively silencing it.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the common struggle of waking up early and the creative methods people use to silence their alarms and catch a few more moments of sleep.

title: ice

description.panel1: A character happily comments on the warm and nice weather while walking outside.
description.panel2: The character steps onto something labeled "ICE" with a smiley face.
description.panel3: The character continues to walk without noticing the ice.
description.panel4: The character slips and falls as a result of stepping on the ice.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how unexpectedly stepping on ice can lead to an unexpected and abrupt fall, even on what seems like a warm and pleasant day.

title: nice day

description.panel1: A person is outside on a sunny day, remarking "What a nice day!"
description.panel2: The person suddenly starts coughing.
description.panel3: Two creatures appear, holding a smoky substance, contributing to the pollution.
description.panel4: The person looks distressed and is crying.

emotion: The author is highlighting the disruption of an initially pleasant day by air pollution, expressing concern about environmental issues.

title: lost ?

description.panel1: A person sitting at a desk notices a piece of paper and is curious about its origin.
description.panel2: A close-up shows the person examining the paper labeled "Potentially important stuff."
description.panel3: The person looks confused, trying to remember if they placed the paper there.
description.panel4: The person decides it must have been someone else who put the paper there and dismisses the concern.

emotion: The author humorously highlights a common experience of forgetting or misplacing important documents and the tendency to doubt one’s memory or assume that someone else is responsible.

title: fun with scanners !

description.panel1: The character is sitting and staring ahead, thinking about how easy their job is.
description.panel2: The character is listening to music with a relaxed expression, highlighting that all they do is listen to music.
description.panel3: The character continues to look ahead, mentioning that the scanner works while they relax.
description.panel4: The character is in a panic as the scanner malfunctions, destroying files and causing chaos.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the misconception that some jobs are easy and carefree, showing that unexpected problems can disrupt even the most seemingly simple tasks.

title: hot sale

description.panel1: A person notices a "Hot Sale" sign on a shelf and reacts with an "Ah!".
description.panel2: The person evaluates the items on sale, expressing approval ("nice").
description.panel3: The person continues to evaluate more items, again saying "nice" and "EWW" to some.
description.panel4: It is revealed that other people were watching the person, feeling uncomfortable, while the person realizes they were being checked out.

emotion: The author expressed the awkwardness and discomfort that can occur when someone realizes they are being observed critically by others.

title: one leg up

description.panel1: A character realizes that they should shave their legs.
description.panel2: The character is in the middle of shaving their legs.
description.panel3: The razor's battery runs out while the character is still shaving.
description.panel4: The character stands up, dissatisfied, as only one leg is shaved.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the frustration of running out of battery power in the middle of an essential grooming task, leading to an unfinished and awkward result.

title: 11:00 am

description.panel1: A person at a computer desk asks another person for a favor, who sighs in response.
description.panel2: The person asking for the favor pleads, claiming they don't ask for much.
description.panel3: The second person visualizes a timeline throughout the day of the first person asking for multiple favors: at 11:00 am for breakfast, at 3:00 pm for laundry, at 4:00 pm for grocery shopping, at 6:00 pm for cooking, and at 8:00 pm for massages.
description.panel4: The first person, sitting at a desk, denies the request with a straightforward "no."

emotion: The author expresses the idea that the person who is claiming not to ask for much actually asks for many favors throughout the day, highlighting a perceived difference in the frequency of requests and underscoring the second person's weariness or reluctance to fulfill yet another favor.

title: sweet catch

description.panel1: A person accidentally bumps a milk carton with their elbow, causing it to start falling.
description.panel2: The person quickly catches the milk carton with their knee.
description.panel3: The person expresses satisfaction with their quick reflexes by saying "nice!"
description.panel4: Despite catching the carton, the person reacts with surprise as the milk starts spilling out.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a moment of quick reflexes turning into a mishap, demonstrating that even successful recoveries can have unintended consequences.

title: fun with scanners

description.panel1: A woman sitting at a desk states that her job is very easy.
description.panel2: The woman expresses that all she does is sit there and listen to music.
description.panel3: The woman continues by saying that the scanner does all the work.
description.panel4: The scanner malfunctions and destroys the documents, causing the woman to react with shock and distress.

emotion: The author humorously shows the potential consequences of over-relying on machines and underestimating the need for vigilance in seemingly easy jobs.

title: health form

description.panel1: The character with glasses is filling out a health form that asks about pregnancy and prescriptions.
description.panel2: The person labeled "MOM" looks at the character with glasses with wide eyes as they consider the form.
description.panel3: The "MOM" character looks more intensely at the character with glasses.
description.panel4: The character with glasses ticks "NO" on the health form for the pregnancy question.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts the pressure and concern a mother might project onto her child when filling out personal health-related forms.

title: crunchy ...

description.panel1: A person holding a can of tuna asks, "This tuna is still good right?"
description.panel2: The person starts eating the tuna, making a "chew chew chew" sound.
description.panel3: The person hears a "crunch" while eating the tuna and looks concerned.
description.panel4: The background changes to a red "ABORT" pattern, and the person sweats and looks horrified as they realize something is wrong.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the experience of realizing something is wrong with food after taking a bite, creating a moment of panic and regret.

title: useless(part2)

description.panel1: Someone hands a water bottle to a person.
description.panel2: The person holding the bottle confidently states that they can carry more than just the bottle.
description.panel3: The person accidentally drops the water bottle.
description.panel4: Three observers labeled "PAL" watch as the person kneels on the ground in defeat with the dropped bottle.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the irony of overconfidence, showing how someone claiming they can handle more ends up struggling with the simplest task.

title: useless(part1)

description.panel1: A person asks for help carrying some boxes.
description.panel2: Another person enthusiastically agrees to help.
description.panel3: The person who agreed to help is standing idly while others labeled "PAL" are carrying the boxes.
description.panel4: The person who initially offered help is deemed "useless," expressing frustration with "but I..."

emotion: The author is illustrating the disparity between the intention to help and actual helpful actions. Despite the willingness to assist, without action, the offer becomes meaningless and is perceived as useless.

title: bath invader

description.panel1: A person is enjoying a bath, happily singing "I'm in da tub."
description.panel2: A spider on a web descends, saying "lol me too."
description.panel3: The person notices the spider and reacts with horror.
description.panel4: The spider, still hanging on its web, asks "how's it goin’?" and the person, now crouched in the tub, responds with "please leave."

emotion: The author humorously depicts an all-too-relatable situation where a relaxing moment is interrupted by the sudden and unwelcome presence of a spider, highlighting the common fear or discomfort many people feel around spiders.

title: the dentist

description.panel1: The dentist tells the patient that they will put something in their mouth to take X-rays and the patient agrees.
description.panel2: The patient looks uncomfortable as the dentist warns that it might hurt but assures it will only take a second.
description.panel3: The patient is struggling and internally telling themselves not to cry.
description.panel4: The dentist encourages the patient to hang in there, noting they are almost done, while the patient looks visibly distressed.

emotion: The comic humorously highlights the common anxiety and discomfort people feel during dental procedures, particularly when the process is uncomfortable but necessary.

title: tough

description.panel1: A character expresses the desire to not be protected all the time.
description.panel2: The character states a wish to become stronger to protect others.
description.panel3: The character resolves to be tough and stand up for themselves, wanting to avoid being babied.
description.panel4: Two other characters condescendingly comment on the main character's attempt to act tough, treating it as adorable.

emotion: The author highlights the struggle of wanting to grow and be independent, but facing condescension and not being taken seriously by others.

title: art

description.panel1: A person enthusiastically expresses their excitement about drawing something great.
description.panel2: A close-up of the person's hand as they draw a figure with messy lines and blue color.
description.panel3: The person looks dejectedly at their drawing.
description.panel4: The person snaps their pencil in frustration.

emotion: The author expresses the frustration and disappointment that can occur when one's artistic efforts do not meet their expectations.

title: glasses collision

description.panel1: A person with glasses asks "HUG?" with open arms.
description.panel2: Another person, also wearing glasses, excitedly responds "HUG!" with open arms.
description.panel3: The two people collide while hugging, causing their glasses to clash.
description.panel4: Both individuals react to the painful collision, expressing discomfort.

emotion: The author humorously portrays the unexpected and sometimes painful consequences of wearing glasses, even during simple, affectionate interactions like hugging.

title: virtual roller coaster

description.panel1: A person excitedly notices control settings for a virtual roller coaster while the other person looks surprised.
description.panel2: The excited person reassures the other that they'll set it to not be scary, but the other person is clearly nervous.
description.panel3: The excited person confidently insists they know what they're doing, while the nervous person is unsure and silent.
description.panel4: Both individuals appear on the roller coaster, with the nervous person screaming in fear and the excited person laughing, having set it to a scary mode.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates how someone’s assurances of knowing what they’re doing can lead to unexpected and frightening experiences due to their mistaken confidence.

title: after easter

description.panel1: A character points out to their friend that Easter candy is on sale.
description.panel2: The friend enthusiastically suggests that they should buy some candy.
description.panel3: The first character firmly says "NO" while holding a small stack of candy boxes.
description.panel4: The first character, now holding an even larger stack of candy boxes, reluctantly repeats "NO...."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the struggle of resisting the temptation to buy discounted holiday candy, despite the initial intention to refrain from indulgence.

title: many followers

description.panel1: The creator excitedly presents their four-panel comics to two friends, who express that they find the comics great.
description.panel2: One friend encourages the creator to share the comics online, believing others will also like them.
description.panel3: The creator doubts that anyone else would be interested in their comics, laughing at the idea.
description.panel4: The creator is shown with 100,129 followers, indicating a large fanbase, surrounded by many fans.

emotion: The author highlights the importance of sharing creative work, even if the creator doubts its appeal, as it could be more appreciated by others than anticipated.

title: arm-wrestling

description.panel1: A person asks to be matched up against someone strong for an arm-wrestling match.
description.panel2: The opponent indifferently agrees to the match, nodding in agreement.
description.panel3: On the count of "Go!", the opponent quickly slams the person's arm down, winning the match in an instant.
description.panel4: The person who lost is left confused and the opponent sparkles, implying confidence or skill.

emotion: The author humorously highlights that underestimating someone based on their appearance can lead to unexpected outcomes.

title: what happened

description.panel1: A person is reflecting on how they used to be in better shape when they were younger.
description.panel2: The younger version of the person is joyfully running outside.
description.panel3: The younger version of the person is actively engaging in various sports.
description.panel4: The same person is now inside, happily celebrating their birthday in front of a computer.

emotion: The author highlights the contrast between the character's active and outdoor-oriented past with their more sedentary and technology-focused present, implying a shift in lifestyle over time.

title: hey listen

description.panel1: A person eagerly approaches another saying, "Hey, hey, listen to this!"
description.panel2: The enthusiastic person talks about how great their day was, being in a great mood.
description.panel3: The other person, with a blank expression, responds flatly, "That's nice."
description.panel4: The enthusiastic person continues to be happy, seemingly unaffected by the flat response.

emotion: The author portrays a scenario where an initially enthusiastic person's excitement is met with an indifferent response, highlighting how reactions from others do not necessarily diminish one's personal happiness or excitement.

title: not bad

description.panel1: A character is thinking to themselves wishing more of their friends liked a certain series.
description.panel2: A friend approaches and says they like the series.
description.panel3: The character enthusiastically bombards the friend with questions and excitement.
description.panel4: The friend clarifies they have only watched one episode, and the main character agrees it’s not bad.

emotion: The author expresses the excitement of finding a friend who shares an interest, even if their level of engagement is different.

title: left-overs

description.panel1: A person excitedly anticipates eating food they have been saving.
description.panel2: The person looks into an empty refrigerator, finding the food missing.
description.panel3: The person appears to be in a state of realization or disappointment.
description.panel4: The person sees their dad eating the saved food and reacts with dismay.

emotion: The author expresses the common and relatable disappointment of expecting to enjoy saved food, only to find that someone else, such as a family member, has eaten it.

title: trying to sleep

description.panel1: The character in bed, struggling to sleep, has the word "FINALLY" indicating they have finally managed to drift off.
description.panel2: The character is shown sleeping soundly with the word "ASLEEP" indicating they are now asleep.
description.panel3: Another character enters the room and loudly asks if the sleeping character wants to hear about a weird dream they had.
description.panel4: The character who just woke up looks annoyed and the intruding character continues to describe their dream in detail, oblivious to having disturbed the sleeper.

emotion: The author highlights the frustration of being woken up after finally managing to fall asleep, portraying a relatable and humorous everyday scenario.

title: buy bye

description.panel1: The character contemplates buying something online but hesitates because it's expensive.
description.panel2: The character convinces herself that the purchase will be worth it in the long run.
description.panel3: The character decides to make the purchase.
description.panel4: The character finds out that the item is sold out.

emotion: The author is expressing the frustration and disappointment that comes with hesitating on making a purchase, only to find out the item is no longer available when they finally decide to buy it.

title: the artist

description.panel1: The character is sitting alone and uninterested in a general group project setting.
description.panel2: The character raises their hand and asks, "Teacher, may I work alone?" in the group project setting.
description.panel3: In a group art project setting, the character appears overwhelmed as several people crowd around looking at their work.
description.panel4: The character raises their hand and asks, "Teacher, may I work alone?" in the group art project setting.

emotion: What the author expresses through this comic: The author highlights the preference of some individuals, particularly artists, to work alone rather than in group settings where they might feel overwhelmed or distracted.

title: take a walk

description.panel1: Person is sitting on a couch, lamenting that they should do something to burn off steam, such as taking a walk, while the dog whines.
description.panel2: The person notices the whining dog and asks if the dog also needs to burn off steam.
description.panel3: The person pets the dog, acknowledging that both of them could use the exercise and suggests going for a walk.
description.panel4: While on the walk, the person struggles to keep up as the dog enthusiastically pulls on the leash.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the shared need for exercise between a person and their dog, and the resulting challenge of the walk, showing the dog's enthusiasm contrasted with the person's struggle to keep up.

title: Assist

description.panel1: A robot asks a man if he could get some assistance.
description.panel2: The robot asks the man if he would mind clicking all the photos with bicycles.
description.panel3: The robot thanks the man for his help after the man completes the task.
description.panel4: The robot excitedly announces to another robot that they have successfully gained access.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the irony of a robot, which is a representation of advanced technology, needing human assistance to complete a CAPTCHA—a test designed to differentiate human users from robots.

title: Cover

description.panel1: A father reassures his son by saying he will cover for him.
description.panel2: The father tells everyone that his son didn't wet his pants, but that it was actually him (the father) who did it.
description.panel3: The father admits, somewhat reluctantly, that he peed on his son.
description.panel4: The son stands beside his father, looking distressed as the father points towards him.

emotion: The author highlights a humorous and exaggerated situation where a father tries to protect his son from embarrassment, only to end up creating a unique and awkward explanation that shifts the embarrassment to himself.

title: In Quarantine

description.panel1: A person looks out of a window with a dog and a cat beside them.
description.panel2: The cat tells the dog, "You know, he's the one in quarantine. Not us."
description.panel3: The cat holds a key while both the cat and the dog head toward a door.
description.panel4: The person looks longingly out of a window as a car drives away, implying the animals left.

emotion: The comic humorously expresses a role reversal where pets perceive their human as being the one confined by quarantine, highlighting the contrasting perspectives between humans and animals during such times.

title: Marking

description.panel1: A person introduces their new puppy to a friend.
description.panel2: The friend asks what the puppy is doing, to which the owner responds that the puppy is marking his territory.
description.panel3: The friend is concerned about the puppy marking his territory in the house, but the owner seems unfazed.
description.panel4: The punchline is revealed as the puppy is using a label maker to mark various items in the house with labels that say "Buddy’s."

emotion: The author uses humor in this comic to play on the common expectation that a dog will mark its territory by urinating, but instead, the puppy is humorously shown using a label maker to achieve the same goal.

title: Gravity

description.panel1: A rabbit is lying in bed looking tired and reluctant to get up.
description.panel2: The rabbit continues to lie down, looking determined to stay in bed.
description.panel3: The rabbit reaches for their phone while still in bed.
description.panel4: The rabbit sends a text that reads, "I WON'T BE ABLE TO COME TO WORK TODAY, BECAUSE GRAVITY."

emotion: The author humorously expresses how sometimes people feel too lazy or unmotivated to get out of bed, jokingly blaming gravity as the reason for not going to work.

title: Not Fully Automated

description.panel1: The character uses a urinal with an automatic flush sensor.
description.panel2: The character washes their hands using a sink with automatic soap and water dispensers.
description.panel3: The character dries their hands using an automatic hand dryer.
description.panel4: The character hesitates, sweating, in front of a door with a manual handle, realizing they must touch it to exit.

emotion: The author highlights the irony and potential inconvenience of having automated systems in a restroom, yet still requiring manual contact to open the door, which can compromise hygiene.

title: Monster

description.panel1: A thief hits a cashier on the head with a gun.
description.panel2: The thief demands all the money in a bag.
description.panel3: The cashier asks if the thief wants the money in a paper or plastic bag.
description.panel4: The thief chooses a paper bag, indicating he is not a monster.

emotion: The author uses humor to contrast the seriousness of a robbery with the thief’s concern for using a paper bag, highlighting an absurd juxtaposition between criminal behavior and environmental consciousness.

title: Pineapple Pizza

description.panel1: A person holding a clipboard asks an inmate what he wants for his last meal; the inmate responds with "Pizza."
description.panel2: The person with the clipboard asks if he wants any toppings; the inmate replies "Just pineapple."
description.panel3: The person holding the clipboard stares blankly, processing the inmate's request.
description.panel4: The person with the clipboard marks the "Special Meal Request" form with "DECLINED."

emotion: The comic humorously addresses the polarizing topic of pineapple as a pizza topping by showing that the request was so controversial that it was declined, even for a last meal.

title: Good Talk

description.panel1: A person holding a bottle says they have made a lot of mistakes but have learned enough to offer advice.
description.panel2: The person earnestly advises to never, ever have kids.
description.panel3: The person stares blankly ahead, creating a pause.
description.panel4: The person, now speaking to a younger individual, says "Good talk, son," implying that the advice was directed at their own child.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the irony of a parent advising their own child against having children, reflecting on the challenges and perhaps regret related to parenting.

title: Fly

description.panel1: A fly is seen vomiting a yellowish liquid onto the ground.
description.panel2: The fly happily eats the vomited liquid.
description.panel3: The fly moves away, leaving behind more of the liquid.
description.panel4: Two raccoons are looking at a piece of cake, with one raccoon asking if the other won't eat it just because a small fly landed on it.

emotion: The comic humorously highlights the contrast between the disgusting behavior of flies and the common human perception that a fly landing on food renders it inedible.

title: No!

description.panel1: A "toilet genie" appears, offering the man a wish while he is sitting on the toilet, and the man urgently asks to go back in time one hour.
description.panel2: The man is holding a burrito and enjoying it as he travels back in time.
description.panel3: The man slackers his past self and takes the burrito away.
description.panel4: The present version of the man firmly tells his confused past self, "NO!" while holding the burrito.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the regret and consequences of impulsive decisions, particularly related to overeating or consuming something that leads to a bad situation, like a spicy burrito that may have caused the man's discomfort.

title: Puppy

description.panel1: An adult figure tells Toby that he got him a puppy.
description.panel2: The adult figure adds that Toby does not have to feed the puppy or pick up its poop, and Toby looks happy.
description.panel3: Toby sees the puppy in the neighbor's yard and questions why it is there.
description.panel4: The adult figure responds that the puppy lives in the neighbor's yard, implying it isn't actually Toby's responsibility.

emotion: The author is expressing a humorous take on the idea of giving a child the joy of having a puppy without any of the responsibilities, by revealing that the puppy lives in the neighbor's yard and not actually with the child.

title: Cat

description.panel1: A character dressed as an adventurer cautiously approaches a pedestal with a golden idol on top.
description.panel2: The character grabs the idol from the pedestal with a look of determination.
description.panel3: The character begins to replace the idol with a weighted bag, suggesting a trap mechanism.
description.panel4: The character, now revealed to be a cat, gleefully runs away after successfully switching the idol.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a classic case of an adventurer stealing a treasure, revealing the adventurer to be an unexpectedly playful cat, blending action and comedy in an unexpected twist.

title: Stock up on Food

description.panel1: A staff member informs a customer that the continental breakfast is meant to be enjoyed in the lounge.
description.panel2: The staff member explains that the food is not intended for stocking up like groceries.
description.panel3: The staff member rudely remarks that there isn't enough food to fill the customer's "fat face."
description.panel4: The customer is seen taking a large amount of food.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates a situation where a customer disregards the intended usage of continental breakfast, emphasizing the disconnect between the staff's instructions and the customer's actions. It also highlights the potential for rudeness in customer service interactions.

title: Misheard

description.panel1: A person commands a dog to "sit."
description.panel2: The dog begins to squat in a position that suggests it is about to defecate.
description.panel3: The dog finishes defecating while the person looks on in surprise.
description.panel4: The dog explains, "I might have misheard you."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a situation where a dog misunderstands a simple command, highlighting the humorous consequences of miscommunication.

title: Make Fire

description.panel1: A person dressed as a caveman is kneeling and attempting to create fire, with a frustrated expression, saying, "Come on..."
description.panel2: The person continues to attempt to make fire, looking determined, saying, "I can do this..."
description.panel3: The person exclaims in joy with outstretched arms, successfully creating a fire, and says, "Yes! Fire! I have fire!"
description.panel4: The scene shifts to a modern setting where the person is celebrating their fire-making achievement indoors, while two children watch with concerned expressions, one of them asking, "When does father go back to work?"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a situation where enthusiasm for primitive skills, like fire-making, can lead to inappropriate or misguided situations when applied in a modern context.

title: Imagination

description.panel1: An adult asks what gift was given and is told it was just an empty box.
description.panel2: The giver explains that the box is full of imagination while two kids play with it.
description.panel3: The adults comment on how cute and precious the children are.
description.panel4: The kids reveal their imaginative thought of fitting a dead body in the box, with one suggesting they'd have to cut it up.

emotion: The author highlights the humor in the unexpected ways children can use their imaginations, challenging adults' often idealized view of children's play.

title: Approved

description.panel1: A person anxiously waits for payment to process at a store counter.
description.panel2: The person becomes more nervous as they continue to wait for the payment to process, while the cashier maintains a neutral expression.
description.panel3: The payment terminal displays "APPROVED!"
description.panel4: The person and cashier celebrate the successful payment with joy while confetti and stars scatter around them.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the common anxiety and subsequent relief associated with waiting for a payment to be approved at a store.

title: Flying Penguin

description.panel1: A penguin looks up at birds flying in the sky.
description.panel2: The penguin is seen leaving a flight school, proudly holding a diploma or certificate.
description.panel3: The penguin pilots a small airplane, flying alongside a group of birds.
description.panel4: The airplane flown by the penguin releases confetti or similar celebratory items into the air.

emotion: The author expresses the idea that determination and education can help overcome natural limitations, as the penguin can achieve flight despite its species' typical inability to fly.

title: Call Phone

description.panel1: A woman says she can't find her phone and asks a man to call it.
description.panel2: The man jokingly calls out to the phone as if it were a pet.
description.panel3: The woman walks away, unimpressed, saying the man's joke is hilarious sarcastically.
description.panel4: The man continues the joke, praising the phone as if it responded to his call.

emotion: The author uses humor to play on the literal interpretation of the phrase "call my phone," contrasting it with the common understanding of using a different phone to ring the lost one.

title: Santa Suppression

description.panel1: A boy asks a girl what she asked Santa for Christmas, and she responds that Santa doesn't exist.
description.panel2: The girl asks how the boy knows Santa doesn't exist, and he confidently replies that he just knows.
description.panel3: A flashback to last year shows a young boy catching Santa in the act of delivering presents.
description.panel4: Santa intimidatingly tells the boy that he saw nothing, and he does not exist, causing the boy to nervously agree.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the idea of Santa's existence being a well-guarded secret, with Santa himself taking drastic measures to ensure children do not reveal the truth about him.

title: At any length.

description.panel1: One character angrily asks another character why they can't admit that they are better than them.
description.panel2: The second character accuses the first of turning this into a metaphorical contest of measuring their worth.
description.panel3: The first character responds by saying they would win even in that hypothetical contest and is willing to prove it.
description.panel4: The first character aggressively demands proof by literally measuring, demonstrating absurd literalism.

emotion: The author uses hyperbolic humor and absurdity to critique the futile nature of arguments driven by ego and the concept of pointless competitions to prove personal superiority.

title: Noted.

description.panel1: A green character is looking over at a pink character who is writing a note.
description.panel2: A teacher notices the pink character passing the note and intervenes, asking the student to read it for the class.
description.panel3: The pink character nervously reads out the worrying content about climate change and feeling powerless.
description.panel4: The teacher and the green character react with surprise as the pink character finishes the note with an offensive remark about the teacher.

emotion: The author expresses the tension and humor that arises when the serious concern of climate change is juxtaposed with a student's lack of respect for their teacher, highlighting the contrast between genuine worries and everyday school antics.

title: Mistaken.

description.panel1: The purple character apologizes for not finishing their homework, and the yellow character reassures them, saying it's okay because everyone makes mistakes.
description.panel2: The yellow character continues by asserting that they themselves never make mistakes, just as a new character, pink, comes running in.
description.panel3: The yellow character inadvertently punches the pink character in the face.
description.panel4: The yellow character, with blood on their fist, goes back to reassuring the purple character, reiterating that everyone makes mistakes.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the irony of claiming to be perfect while making a mistake, reinforcing the idea that everyone is capable of making errors.

title: The Path to Success.

description.panel1: A character is heading straight toward a finish flag with a caption saying, "The path to success isn't a straight line."
description.panel2: The character is navigating a twisting path, with a caption stating, "There are twists and turns, ups and downs..."
description.panel3: The character is shown moving persistently along the winding path, accompanied by the caption, "But if you're persistent, you'll eventually get th-"
description.panel4: The character, confused, stands in front of an infinite loop and says, "wait."

emotion: The author expresses that while success is often depicted as a clear and direct path, it is actually filled with unexpected challenges and complexities. Persistence is important, but the journey may be more complicated and unending than anticipated.

title: Loser.

description.panel1: The green character states, "I'm a loser," while the pink character listens.
description.panel2: The pink character places a hand on the green character's shoulder and says, "Hey, come on now."
description.panel3: The pink character gestures with both hands while saying, "Without losers, you can't have winners!"
description.panel4: The green character's face brightens up as the pink character says, "We need you!"

emotion: The author conveys a message of encouragement and the idea that every role, including being a "loser," is important and necessary for there to be "winners."

title: Hide and Seek.

description.panel1: Two characters sit at a table, and the yellow character nostalgically says they miss playing hide and seek, adding that their dad is the best at it.
description.panel2: The yellow character remarks that they haven't found their dad, even though it's been over twenty years.
description.panel3: The blue character looks sympathetically at the yellow character, resting a hand on their shoulder.
description.panel4: The scene shifts to a red-hooded figure hiding behind a curtain, saying, "How has he not found me yet?!"

emotion: The author humorously expresses the idea of a hide and seek game extending to absurd lengths, highlighting the contrasting perceptions between one character's prolonged search and the other character's persistent hiding.

title: Dead On.

description.panel1: A genie warns a person to be careful with their wishes.
description.panel2: The person wishes for a unicorn.
description.panel3: The genie grants the wish, but the unicorn is dead, and the genie defends that the wish did not specify the unicorn had to be alive.
description.panel4: The person is delighted and hugs the dead unicorn, stating that this outcome is perfect.

emotion: The author humorously shows the importance of being specific in one's wishes and that sometimes people's expectations or desires can be unexpected or unconventional.

title: Feedback.

description.panel1: A pink character is sitting alone, eating a sandwich.
description.panel2: Two other characters, one green and one purple, approach the pink character and taunt them for eating a PB&J sandwich alone.
description.panel3: The pink character denies the accusation, saying it's not true.
description.panel4: The pink character clarifies that the sandwich is only peanut butter.

emotion: The author is humorously highlighting the specificity of the pink character's situation, exaggerating the taunting and the pink character's literal correction to create a comedic misunderstanding.

title: Meme Speed.

description.panel1: A blue character shows a new meme on their phone, but the green character dismisses it by saying the meme is dead.
description.panel2: The green character shows the same meme on their phone and calls it the hot new thing.
description.panel3: The green character laughs and calls the meme good, but then immediately dismisses it again as dead when showing it to the blue character.
description.panel4: The blue character is confused and tries to understand the inconsistency in the green character's statements.

emotion: The author is highlighting the fickleness and rapid changes in the world of internet memes, where what is considered trendy can shift quickly and people’s opinions about it can be inconsistent and contradictory.

title: Adult Friendship.

description.panel1: Two individuals casually express a desire to spend time together, saying "We should hang out sometime!" and responding "Totally!".
description.panel2: The same individuals, now slightly older, repeat their desire to hang out while continuing with their routines.
description.panel3: The individuals, now elderly, once again mention hanging out sometime but have not done so yet.
description.panel4: Two gravestones are engraved with the individuals' final conversation, showing "We should hang out sometime" and "Totally!".

emotion: The author highlights the tendency of people to express intentions of socializing without ever committing, suggesting that procrastination and avoidance can lead to missed opportunities and regrets in relationships.

title: Proposition.

description.panel1: A pink character points and asks a yellow character, "Oh hey! What's that?"
description.panel2: The pink character is blushing while the yellow character looks confused, and an airplane is writing "WILL YOU MARRY M" in the sky.
description.panel3: The full message "WILL YOU MARRY MY DAD?" is revealed in the sky, and both characters look surprised.
description.panel4: The pink character, crying and embracing the yellow character, says, "I want a mom so bad."

emotion: The author humorously expressed the pink character's deep desire for a mother by using an exaggerated and surprising proposal scenario involving the yellow character and the pink character's dad.

title: Talk to the Hand.

description.panel1: A pink figure dismissively tells a blue figure, "PSH! TALK TO THE HAND!" while holding up their hand.
description.panel2: The blue figure, blushing and nervous, compliments the pink figure by saying, "H-Hi. You look... handsome," while the pink figure holds up a hand in response.
description.panel3: The pink figure tells the blue figure, "YOU LOOK LIKE SOMEONE WHO COULD USE A HAND," pointing their finger towards the blue figure.
description.panel4: The pink figure and the blue figure are sitting together at a dining table, with the pink figure happily pointing fingers in a cheerful atmosphere.

emotion: The author uses humor to depict a transformation in the interaction between the characters, starting from initial rejection and ending with a friendly and supportive connection, emphasizing the theme of kindness and understanding.

title: End Suffering.

description.panel1: The blue character requests the pink character to end their suffering.
description.panel2: The pink character apologetically points a gun at the blue character, who is surprised and distressed.
description.panel3: The blue character, now upset, clarifies that they just wanted to be cheered up, not to be hurt.
description.panel4: The pink character reacts angrily, saying they were only trying to fulfill the blue character's wishes of ending their misery.

emotion: The author used exaggerated and dark humor to express the importance of clear communication and understanding others' wishes, highlighting the consequences of misinterpretation.

title: If Walls Could Talk.

description.panel1: A person is standing next to a wall, saying, "A lot of memories in this house. If walls could talk..."
description.panel2: The person looks surprised as the wall responds, "We can talk, Andrew."
description.panel3: The wall continues, "But every time we try to talk the outlets start screaming," and the outlet is depicted making loud "AAAAAHHHHH" noises.
description.panel4: The frightened person is backing away as the outlet continues to scream "AAAAAHHHHH."

emotion: The comic humorously explores the idea of inanimate objects being able to communicate, but with a twist as the outlets incessantly scream, making effective communication impossible and adding an unexpected layer of absurdity.

title: The Saddest Story.

description.panel1: The blue character notices that the yellow-haired character is crying in bed and asks if they are crying.
description.panel2: The yellow-haired character explains that they are sad because they were going to eat ice cream before bed but had already brushed their teeth.
description.panel3: The blue character suggests that the yellow-haired character could brush their teeth again, but the yellow-haired character says they are too lazy to do so.
description.panel4: The blue character starts crying too, saying that the situation is just so sad.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the trivial yet relatable struggles people face in their daily lives, emphasizing how minor inconveniences can sometimes feel overwhelmingly sad.

title: Happy Cloud.

description.panel1: An artist says, "Let's put a happy little cloud here," while painting on a canvas.
description.panel2: The cloud painted on the canvas expresses sadness by saying, "Actually, I'm sad."
description.panel3: The artist angrily asks, "What the fuck did you say?" and the cloud, frightened, responds, "I-I'm happy..."
description.panel4: The now visibly happy cloud, modified by the artist, is on the canvas, and the artist comments, "There, now, isn't that wonderful?"

emotion: The author humorously portrays the idea of enforced positivity, illustrating that sometimes people are pressured to appear happy even when they are not genuinely feeling that way.

title: Fulfillment.

description.panel1: A pink character expresses feeling empty inside to a blue character.
description.panel2: The blue character suggests that they have something that might help and starts looking for it.
description.panel3: The blue character indicates that they need somewhere to store their used soap bar collection, implying the pink character can help.
description.panel4: The pink character, now filled with soap bars, feels fulfilled and has found a purpose.

emotion: The author conveys a humorous message about finding fulfillment and purpose in unexpected ways, often through helping others with their unique needs.

title: Crying Shame.

description.panel1: A blue character asks a pink character why their eyes look strange.
description.panel2: The pink character explains that their eye muscles are very powerful and strong, while the blue character curiously asks why.
description.panel3: The pink character reveals that their eye muscles have held back every single tear they've ever had; the blue character finds this sad.
description.panel4: The pink character starts crying intensely and asks if it is really sad, demonstrating the emotional build-up they have been suppressing.

emotion: The author illustrates the consequences of suppressing emotions and suggests that holding back feelings can lead to an intense emotional release.

title: River.

description.panel1: A person philosophically states a famous quote about the changing nature of a river and a man.
description.panel2: Another person, wearing a lifeguard uniform, tells the floating person to stop pooping in the lazy river.
description.panel3: The floating person responds angrily, calling the lifeguard an ignoramus.
description.panel4: The floating person claims to have pooped again and mocks the lifeguard for not capturing them.

emotion: The author juxtaposes philosophical wisdom with absurd and crude behavior to create a humorous and ironic situation, highlighting the disconnect between deep thoughts and inappropriate actions.

title: Sun Upset.

description.panel1: Two characters are excitedly discussing their plan to party all night long.
description.panel2: The Sun arrives, enthusiastically agreeing to join the party, which causes discomfort to the two characters.
description.panel3: The two characters become upset and yell at the Sun, telling it to go away because it's midnight.
description.panel4: The Sun appears sad as it sets, while the two characters continue to look grumpy and dissatisfied.

emotion: The author highlights the irony of expressing a desire to party all night yet becoming annoyed when the Sun appears, illustrating the common sentiment of enjoying nighttime activities but disliking the consequences (the return of daylight).

title: Raise.

description.panel1: Two individuals are playing cards, and one declares, "I'll see your bet, and I'll raise you."
description.panel2: The other player, confused, asks, "Raise me what?" The first player responds, "Raise you, like a son."
description.panel3: Both players sit in silence, processing the unexpected statement.
description.panel4: The formerly confused player exclaims, "Daddy!" and the first player affectionately replies, "Come here, my boy!"

emotion: The comic humorously plays on the double meaning of the word "raise," transforming a typical poker raising scenario into a touching and absurd twist where one player offers to become the other's parent, leading to an unexpected and heartwarming moment.

title: Government Secrets

description.panel1: A mysterious figure in a trench coat asks a child if they want to know government secrets.
description.panel2: The mysterious figure leans in closely, stating that the government doesn't want the child to know something, causing the child to gasp.
description.panel3: The child expresses shock and disbelief, while the mysterious figure prepares to reveal the "secret."
description.panel4: It turns out the "secret" is a party surprise being thrown by the government, which displeases a character dressed as a government official who feels the surprise is ruined.

emotion: The author humorously subverts expectations by turning the idea of discovering government secrets into a lighthearted scenario where the "secret" is actually a surprise party, playfully critiquing the seriousness typically associated with government secrecy.

title: Money Maker.

description.panel1: A character on a stage instructs another character to "shake yo' money maker."
description.panel2: The second character appears confused and unsure of what the first character means.
description.panel3: The second character starts shaking an art degree, thinking that it is their "money maker."
description.panel4: The first character corrects them forcefully, indicating they were wrong.

emotion: The author humorously conveys the idea that simply having an art degree does not necessarily translate to easily making money, highlighting the often challenging economic reality for art degree holders.

title: Cast.

description.panel1: A blue character in a wheelchair asks a pink character if they'd like to sign their cast, and the pink character agrees.
description.panel2: The pink character begins signing the cast.
description.panel3: The pink character notices something alarming while reading the cast.
description.panel4: The blue character appears sinister, as the pink character realizes they've signed over ownership of their soul.

emotion: The comic humorously explores the theme of unintended consequences, showing how a seemingly harmless action (signing a cast) can lead to an exaggeratedly dire outcome (giving away one's soul).

title: Firm.

description.panel1: The interviewer asks the interviewee what attracted them to their firm.
description.panel2: The interviewee hesitantly responds that accounting makes them excited.
description.panel3: The interviewer and interviewee share an awkward, silent, and tense moment.
description.panel4: The interviewer is now overly excited and leaning over the desk, encouraging the interviewee to continue.

emotion: The comic humorously exaggerates an awkward and inappropriate job interview scenario, highlighting unusual reactions and breaking professional norms in a comedic way.

title: Graded on a Curve.

description.panel1: A student expresses disappointment to the teacher for receiving a B- grade.
description.panel2: The teacher reacts angrily, stating that the B- grade is not right.
description.panel3: The teacher points at the student's assignment which details a poorly written explanation about the importance of maps, confirming the B- grade.
description.panel4: The teacher walks away satisfied, while the student looks back after realizing the lower grade was justified.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the student's realization that a poor-quality assignment justifiably led to a low grade, despite initial disappointment or disagreement with the assessment.

title: White Space.

description.panel1: A blue character looking surprised and worried while using a laptop.
description.panel2: A pink character, likely their mother, discovering what the blue character is looking at and asking, "Is that porn?!", while the blue character nervously responds, "Mom, no!".
description.panel3: The blue character, looking very uneasy, says, "I-It's a vase..." while the pink character remains skeptical.
description.panel4: The pink character looking at the laptop screen which shows an optical illusion that looks like both a vase and two faces, saying "Hmmm...".

emotion: The author demonstrates the humorous scenario of misunderstandings, showing how an innocent optical illusion can be misinterpreted and lead to a comedic situation involving mistaken assumptions and panic.

title: Worth a Shot.

description.panel1: The blue character with a red dot on his head is asked by the pink character, "What's up with that red dot on your head?"
description.panel2: The blue character explains to the pink character that he hired a sniper to put him out of his misery if he ever does something painfully embarrassing.
description.panel3: The blue character trips while walking up the stairs, causing an embarrassing moment.
description.panel4: The blue character, lying on the ground, says "Goodbye friend" to the pink character, who looks concerned and sad.

emotion: The comic humorously conveys the extreme and impractical lengths one might go to avoid or escape embarrassment, highlighting the sometimes irrational fear of being publicly humiliated.

title: Back up.

description.panel1: A person flushes a toilet.
description.panel2: The toilet overflows dramatically, causing the person to fall.
description.panel3: Water gushes out of the house, and someone outside exclaims in shock.
description.panel4: People celebrate because the water has ended a drought and saved the town.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a situation where a common household mishap (an overflowing toilet) leads to an unexpectedly beneficial outcome for the community (ending a drought and saving the town).

title: Sun Block.

description.panel1: A character is peacefully sleeping while the sun is setting outside the window.
description.panel2: The sun rises and happily says "GOOOOD MORNI-" which wakes up the character unpleasantly.
description.panel3: A cloud covers the sun, saying "I GOT YOU BRO" while the sun mumbles "NGH."
description.panel4: The character thanks the cloud as it blocks the sunlight, allowing the character to go back to sleep.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the struggle of waking up early due to sunlight and the relief of getting extra sleep when it's blocked out.

title: Morbid Curiosity.

description.panel1: A scared blue character asks a hooded figure if they are the Grim Reaper here to kill them.
description.panel2: The hooded figure responds that they are actually the "Grim Peeper" who just watches people die.
description.panel3: The hooded figure looks on as a distressed blue character encounters a goose.
description.panel4: The hooded figure excitedly watches the situation unfold while eating popcorn as the blue character screams in distress.

emotion: The author humorously subverts the traditional fear associated with the Grim Reaper by introducing a character called the "Grim Peeper" who merely observes death rather than causing it, highlighting a morbid curiosity in a comical way.

title: Worse and worse.

description.panel1: A blue character says, "Everything is getting worse and worse," to a pink character.
description.panel2: The pink character places a comforting hand on the blue character and says, "Hey, that's not true."
description.panel3: The pink character holds up a jar and says, "Ragú has a new and improved recipe!"
description.panel4: The blue character, holding the jar, says, "That's it, though."

emotion: The author expresses a sense of pessimism about the state of the world, with a humorous twist by pointing out that the only positive change is a trivial improvement in a product's recipe. This underscores a feeling of disappointment or minimal progress in the face of broader negative trends.

title: Time.

description.panel1: The green character is excited and happy that it has reached its 30th birthday.
description.panel2: Suddenly, the green character is told it is now its 50th birthday, causing confusion and shock.
description.panel3: The green character is now told it is its 70th birthday, and it is distressed and bewildered by how quickly time has passed.
description.panel4: The yellow character, holding a shovel, indicates a grave and tells the now old and tired green character to get in.

emotion: The author illustrates the fleeting and relentless nature of time, highlighting how quickly life can pass by, and the suddenness with which one can find oneself at the end of their life journey.

title: Let It All Out.

description.panel1: A purple character tells a yellow character that it's healthy to cry.
description.panel2: The yellow character, with tears forming, asks if they should let it all out, and the purple character affirms.
description.panel3: An image of Earth from space is shown, depicting continents and water.
description.panel4: The Earth is now completely blue, suggesting it is covered in water, indicative of the yellow character having cried an enormous amount.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the idea that crying can be so cathartic and intense that it might seem like it could flood the world, emphasizing the emotional relief of letting all one's emotions out.

title: Flavor Savior.

description.panel1: A person asks God why He made healthy food taste bad while holding a vegetable.
description.panel2: God appears frustrated and asks if He has to show the person how to properly cook healthy food.
description.panel3: God advises the person to sauté the vegetables and not forget to season and taste the food while cooking.
description.panel4: The person finds the cooked food delicious and God confirms it.

emotion: The author is expressing that healthy food can be delicious if properly cooked and seasoned.

title: Happy Thanksgiving.

description.panel1: Four characters are sitting at a table with a Thanksgiving meal, and one of them says, "Happy Thanksgiving! Let's eat!"
description.panel2: A pink character aggressively starts eating their food with "CHOMP CHOMP" sounds.
description.panel3: The pink character continues to devour their food at an intense pace while the others look on in surprise and distress.
description.panel4: The pink character begins to grab food from a green character's plate, causing frustration and fear among the others.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts the chaos and sometimes inconsiderate behaviors that can occur during group meals, such as Thanksgiving, highlighting the tension between eager eating and sharing.

title: Walkin' Buds.

description.panel1: One bird presents a badge to another bird, stating they can't fly but have each other.
description.panel2: The second bird dismissively claims to be lazy and capable of flying, then damages the badge.
description.panel3: The first bird holds a "Walkin' Buds Club" shirt and appears disheartened.
description.panel4: The second bird, wearing the "Walkin' Buds Club" shirt, walks alongside the first bird, indicating companionship.

emotion: The author conveys a message about the value of friendship and camaraderie, regardless of individual abilities or limitations. Through the act of gifting the shirt, it shows how one can cherish and support friends, even if they're initially dismissive or reluctant.

title: Death Toll.

description.panel1: A character is sitting on a toilet with a blank expression.
description.panel2: The character is startled as the Grim Reaper appears, saying "NO! NOT HERE! NOT LIKE THIS!"
description.panel3: The Grim Reaper extends a hand with a speech bubble saying "20 BUCKS," while the character looks surprised.
description.panel4: The character, handing over money, asks resignedly, "DO YOU HAVE TO DO THIS EVERY TIME I GO TO THE BATHROOM?" and the Grim Reaper responds "YES."

emotion: The author humorously portrays the inconvenience of bothersome interruptions during private moments, using the extreme scenario of the Grim Reaper demanding a toll every time the character uses the bathroom.

title: Here Comes Gabe.

description.panel1: Two characters are standing together, and one says, "Here comes Gabe..."
description.panel2: A third character (Gabe) joyfully approaches and greets, "Hello, my friends!"
description.panel3: The two original characters respond with a smile, saying, "He's so nice and friendly."
description.panel4: The two characters' expressions change to disgusted, and one says, "Disgusting."

emotion: The author expresses the idea that people may outwardly appear friendly but can harbor negative feelings or judgments towards others. The comic highlights a contrast between outward friendliness and inner disgust.

title: King of the Mountain

description.panel1: Two individuals are shown climbing different mountains with the caption "Everyone has their own mountain to climb."
description.panel2: A character is climbing a trash-covered mountain, expressing determination to be the king of this 'trash mountain.'
description.panel3: The character encounters a raccoon on top of the trash mountain who hisses aggressively at them.
description.panel4: The raccoon is now wearing a crown, asserting dominance as the 'king' of the trash mountain, leaving the character dejected.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the idea that everyone has their own challenges to overcome, but acknowledges that some pursuits might turn out to be more futile or absurd than initially anticipated.

title: Carpet Scratching 2.

description.panel1: A dog is scratching itself vigorously while a squirrel asks why the dog is kicking itself. The dog responds that it has an itch.
description.panel2: The dog continues to scratch itself energetically, while the squirrel comments that the dog might have scratched the itch enough.
description.panel3: The dog looks relieved and says "phew," indicating it thinks the itch is gone.
description.panel4: The dog resumes scratching itself even more intensely, and the squirrel, alarmed, exclaims "DUDE!"

emotion: The author humorously depicts the relentless and sometimes absurd nature of trying to alleviate an itch, highlighting how it often feels like an endless and exaggerated process.

title: Growth.

description.panel1: A moth proudly states that it has become a moth while a worm says "dang!"
description.panel2: A butterfly proudly states that it has become a butterfly while the same worm says "dang!" with more enthusiasm.
description.panel3: The butterfly asks the worm what it has become.
description.panel4: The worm confidently replies that it has accepted itself for who it is, and both the moth and butterfly exclaim "DAAAAAANG!"

emotion: The author conveys a message about self-acceptance and the idea that personal growth is not only about transformation but also about embracing and accepting oneself for who one is.

title: Oh Kitty.

description.panel1: A woman scolds a man lying on a couch, calling him a lazy bum, while a cat stands beside her.
description.panel2: The man, still lying on the couch, claims that the cat would never talk to him like that.
description.panel3: The cat responds with an insult, saying "F*** you."
description.panel4: The man laughs and compliments the cat, saying "Haha, oh kitty! You're the best."
