emotion: The author humorously depicts a scenario where a man is insulted by both a person and a cat, showing his contrasting reactions to the same treatment from different sources, ultimately highlighting the humor in his indifference to the cat's insult.

title: Rubbed The Wrong Way.

description.panel1: A person is grateful and enjoying petting a dog, thinking, "Thank you for putting your hands on me."
description.panel2: The person attempts to reciprocate by rubbing the dog, but the dog tries to stop them.
description.panel3: The dog expresses displeasure and confusion, thinking, "The fuck, dude."
description.panel4: The person continues to pet the dog, and the dog further expresses shock, thinking, "Wow what the fuck dude."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a situation where a person's attempt to reciprocate affection towards a dog is misunderstood and rejected by the dog. It plays on the idea that the actions we find comforting or enjoyable are not always perceived the same way by others.

title: Stopped.

description.panel1: A koala asks, "How do I stop being such a shitty person?"
description.panel2: A turtle responds, "Stop being shitty."
description.panel3: The koala inquires, "Is that hard?" and the turtle replies, "Sorta."
description.panel4: The turtle concludes, "Dang... there's nothing I can do."

emotion: The author illustrates the difficulty and seeming simplicity in the journey of self-improvement, highlighting the paradox of simple advice being hard to follow and the individual's struggle in enacting change.

title: Killer View.

description.panel1: A green character successfully tosses a crumpled paper ball into a trash can.
description.panel2: The green character celebrates the successful toss and wishes someone had seen it.
description.panel3: Another character appears at the window, indicating they did see the toss.
description.panel4: The green character looks pleased and proud, basking in the acknowledgment.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the desire for recognition and the satisfaction that comes from being seen and acknowledged for small achievements.

title: Learning Experience.

description.panel1: A snake looks sad and says, "I've made so many mistakes."
description.panel2: The snake looks at a turtle, which responds, "Mistakes are learning experiences."
description.panel3: The snake, still looking sad, says, "I was supposed to learn? Sigh, one more mistake..."
description.panel4: The turtle says, "You could learn from this one," and the snake responds with "Nah."

emotion: The author humorously expresses the idea that while mistakes are often viewed as opportunities for learning, some individuals may still choose to overlook the lessons these experiences provide.

title: Change.

description.panel1: A frog tells a turtle, "I want to change the world."
description.panel2: The frog asks the turtle, "For the better?"
description.panel3: The frog and the turtle sit in silence.
description.panel4: The frog urgently asks, "Answer me, turtle," while the turtle looks sad.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the weight of the responsibility and complexity that comes with wanting to change the world, questioning whether the change will indeed be positive, as emphasized by the turtle's silence and somber expression.

title: Infantry.

description.panel1: A child asks an adult, "Where do babies come from?"
description.panel2: The adult responds, "No one knows. It's one of life's greatest mysteries."
description.panel3: The adult and child panic as they see a baby emerging from the floor.
description.panel4: The baby is fully emerged and says, "You thought baby proofing the house would stop us?"

emotion: The author humorously depicts the concept of babies appearing unexpectedly and how baby-proofing measures can sometimes feel futile, using an exaggeration to play on parental anxieties and the mystery surrounding the origin of babies from a child's perspective.

title: No Reason.

description.panel1: The yellow character asks the pink character why they are so happy.
description.panel2: The pink character responds that there is no particular reason for their happiness.
description.panel3: The yellow character is confused and ponders the pink character's response.
description.panel4: The yellow character appears distressed and questions how someone can be happy without a specific reason.

emotion: The author expresses the confusion and difficulty some people may have in understanding how others can find happiness without a specific cause, highlighting different perspectives on emotional experiences.

title: You'll Change Your Mind.

description.panel1: A young person says they are never having kids, and an older person encourages them by saying they'll change their mind.
description.panel2: The once young person is now middle-aged, and the even older person repeats that they'll change their mind.
description.panel3: The middle-aged person is now elderly, visiting the older person's grave which reads "You'll change your mind."
description.panel4: The now elderly person tells a younger person that they'll change their mind.

emotion: The author expresses a cyclical pattern of generational advice and the persistence of certain beliefs over time, highlighting how perspectives and advice can be passed down even if the original prediction does not come true.

title: Ask Not.

description.panel1: A trainer asks Ray if he has any questions after explaining everything needed for the job.
description.panel2: Ray confidently responds that he has no questions and is 100% sure.
description.panel3: Ray serves a customer while looking uncertain.
description.panel4: Ray, sweating and nervous, admits to having many questions.

emotion: The comic illustrates the contrast between Ray's initial confidence and his subsequent realization of confusion, highlighting the common experience of underestimating the complexity of a new task.

title: Fashion Statement.

description.panel1: A green character tells a pink character holding a sign that says "ANYTHING HELPS" that they don't need help.
description.panel2: The green character elaborates, saying the pink character needs to put on "big boy pants," roll up their sleeves, and pull themselves up by their bootstraps.
description.panel3: The pink character responds that they can't afford clothes.
description.panel4: The green character struggles to come up with a response, saying phrases like "tighten your belt," "buckle down," and ultimately ends with frustration.

emotion: The author is expressing the irony and frustration of offering clichéd, impractical advice to someone in need, highlighting the disconnect between such advice and the reality of those who lack basic resources.

title: Confession.

description.panel1: A police officer sternly asks a suspect where they were on the night of February 25th.
description.panel2: The suspect nervously confesses to canceling plans with friends and staying home alone.
description.panel3: The suspect admits to ordering pizza and eating excessively instead of cooking a healthy dinner.
description.panel4: The suspect reveals that they spent the rest of the night aimlessly surfing the web and feeling self-loathing, causing the officer to ask them to stop.

emotion: The author humorously portrays an interrogation scene where the suspect's confession is overly detailed and mundane, highlighting the trivial nature of their activities on the night in question.

title: So Many Things.

description.panel1: A frog expresses that there are many things it wants to do before dying.
description.panel2: A turtle responds to the frog, suggesting it should just do those things.
description.panel3: The frog calls the turtle naive in a condescending manner.
description.panel4: The frog explains that doing the things it wants to do would require actually doing them.

emotion: The author highlights the common human dilemma of procrastination and the gap between wanting to do many things and actually taking action to accomplish them, often pointing out the humorous irony in how our aspirations don't always translate into actions.

title: Busy.

description.panel1: The yellow character greets the blue character and asks how he's been.
description.panel2: The yellow character says he's been super busy and the blue character agrees, saying he's also been very busy.
description.panel3: The yellow character asks how work has been, and the blue character replies that work has been super busy.
description.panel4: Both characters emphasize how extremely busy they are, using stronger language and appearing stressed.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a common social interaction where people express being overly busy, often escalating to extreme descriptions of their busyness, reflecting a shared societal sentiment of constant stress and overwhelm.

title: Creation Miff.

description.panel1: A divine figure contemplates, saying "I should make life."
description.panel2: The figure creates a small pink human-like creature with a "poof."
description.panel3: The creature is anxious and confused, asking "WHY??"
description.panel4: The divine figure appears unsure and distressed, responding "I DON'T KNOW!"

emotion: The author is humorously highlighting the inexplicable nature of life's creation and the uncertainty that even a creator might experience about their own actions.

title: Perish the Thought.

description.panel1: A pink character holding a drink looks worried as a purple character holding a drink says, "Hey dude, I'll be right back."
description.panel2: The pink character panics and says, "No! Don't leave me alone with..."
description.panel3: The pink character imagines a pair of eyes greeting him by saying, "Hi."
description.panel4: The pink character imagines a speech bubble from the eyes saying, "So, your parents are getting old, huh?" as he looks uncomfortable.

emotion: The author is expressing the anxiety some people feel about being left alone in social situations, especially when faced with awkward conversations or questions.

title: Donut Story.

description.panel1: A character laments that the donuts aren't selling while another listens.
description.panel2: The second character examines the donuts with a thoughtful expression.
description.panel3: The second character enthusiastically proclaims that the donuts need "sex appeal."
description.panel4: A donut is visually enhanced with vibrant colors and a glowing aura.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the idea that marketing and presentation can significantly impact the appeal and success of a product.

title: Name Names.

description.panel1: One character greets another by saying, "Hey Andy! How's it going, dude?"
description.panel2: The second character corrects the mistake, saying, "Oh heyyy... uhh... Shawn?"
description.panel3: The first character is excited and surprised, exclaiming they've remembered someone's name.
description.panel4: The first character proudly states Shawn's name while Shawn responds with a somewhat unimpressed "Yes, good job."

emotion: The author humorously portrays the difficulty some people face in remembering names and exaggerates the sense of accomplishment when they finally do remember correctly.

title: Objectified.

description.panel1: A character is sitting at a computer screen and happily decides to buy a "NEW OBJECT" online, thinking it will make them happy.
description.panel2: Later, the character receives the "NEW OBJECT" from another character.
description.panel3: The character stands holding the "NEW OBJECT" with a neutral expression.
description.panel4: The character continues to hold the "NEW OBJECT," still displaying a neutral expression.

emotion: The author conveys the message that material possessions, such as new objects, do not necessarily bring lasting happiness or fulfillment.

title: Keeping it Real.

description.panel1: The blue character enthusiastically asks the purple character, "How goes it, my dude?"
description.panel2: The purple character responds with "not good" while the blue character claps his hands on the purple character’s shoulders in a consoling manner.
description.panel3: The two characters embrace each other, and the blue character says "same" while patting the purple character on the back.
description.panel4: The purple character bids farewell to the blue character by saying "take care, my guy," to which the blue character nonchalantly replies, “nah,” and waves.

emotion: The author highlights the value of authenticity and mutual support in friendships, showing that it's okay to admit when things aren't going well and to offer genuine comfort to one another.

title: Internet Fix.

description.panel1: A character is speaking at a podium, stating that the internet has deteriorated and asking for ideas to fix it.
description.panel2: Another character raises their hand from the audience, and the speaker acknowledges them.
description.panel3: The audience member suggests that people should be nice to each other online.
description.panel4: The speaker dismisses the suggestion as a terrible idea, and the audience member accepts the response.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the irony and cynicism in today's online interactions by suggesting that simply being nice to each other is considered an impractical solution to fixing the internet's problems.

title: It's Simple.

description.panel1: One character asks the other how they manage to stay happy all the time.
description.panel2: The second character responds that they stay happy by simply ignoring their problems.
description.panel3: The first character questions whether ignoring problems just creates more problems.
description.panel4: The second character cheerfully replies that they also ignore those additional problems.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a flawed coping mechanism where ignoring problems is presented as a simple yet ineffective solution to maintaining happiness.

title: Favorite Things.

description.panel1: A dog asks another dog what their favorite thing to do is, and the second dog responds with "Run."
description.panel2: The first dog excitedly agrees, exclaiming "Oh my gosh! Me too!"
description.panel3: The first dog asks what the other's favorite food is, and the second dog replies with "Everything."
description.panel4: The first dog, again excited, responds with "Oh. My. Gosh!"

emotion: The author illustrates the dogs' simple pleasures and excitement in discovering shared interests, emphasizing innocent enthusiasm and joy in friendship.

title: Best Wishes.

description.panel1: A pink figure expresses a wish for a cheeseburger to a genie.
description.panel2: A blue figure, distressed and revealing they have terminal cancer, pleads while the pink figure eats the cheeseburger.
description.panel3: The genie reminds the pink figure they have an additional wish.
description.panel4: The pink figure, still holding the cheeseburger, contemplates what to wish for next as the genie looks on.

emotion: The author highlights the contrasting reactions to crisis and the sometimes misplaced priorities of people when given opportunities to make impactful decisions.

title: Roommate Dog 2.

description.panel1: The green character begins to tell the dog about a time they thought a fart was going to be just a little one.
description.panel2: The dog interrupts and notices someone outside the window.
description.panel3: The dog yells out the window at the purple character to get away from their house.
description.panel4: The dog casually resumes the conversation, asking the green character to continue.

emotion: The comic humorously highlights a comical interruption where the dog swiftly switches from aggression towards an intruder to calmly returning to a mundane conversation, showcasing an abrupt yet funny contrast in behavior.

title: Medium of Exchange

description.panel1: A pink character enthusiastically presents a bucket filled with various likes, hoping it will cover their rent, to a displeased landlord.
description.panel2: The landlord sternly inspects the bucket of likes, appearing dissatisfied.
description.panel3: The pink character nervously stands by as the landlord, still looking displeased, examines the bucket of likes.
description.panel4: The pink character is now depicted homeless, sitting inside a cardboard box, still clutching a single like in a bucket.

emotion: The author uses the comic to highlight the absurdity of valuing social media approval (likes) as a form of currency in real-world scenarios, emphasizing that such intangible metrics do not hold actual monetary value when it comes to essential needs like paying rent.

title: Free Hand.

description.panel1: The left hand is criticizing the right hand, calling it worthless because it can't draw, write, or throw.
description.panel2: The right hand is suddenly summoned to report to the pelvic region.
description.panel3: The left hand comments with "Again?", implying this happens often.
description.panel4: The right hand attempts to continue the conversation but is interrupted by another urgent call.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the interruptions the right hand faces due to urgent, repetitive tasks, emphasizing the frequent disturbances in life that prevent one from focusing on other activities.

title: Double Dip.

description.panel1: A teal character sneezes loudly, saying "ACHOO!"
description.panel2: A purple character says "bless you" to the teal character.
description.panel3: The teal character sneezes loudly again, saying "ACHOO!"
description.panel4: The purple character angrily yells "YOU GREEDY MOTHERFUCKER" at the teal character.

emotion: The comic humorously portrays a situation where a second sneeze after receiving a polite "bless you" response is seen as excessive and greeted with exaggerated frustration or anger.

title: Time to Kill.

description.panel1: The Grim Reaper tells a person that they are scheduled to die at 3 PM.
description.panel2: The person lying against the tree points out that it is only 2 PM, because daylight savings ended today.
description.panel3: The Grim Reaper and the person realize they have an hour to spare, and the person comments that they have an hour to "kill."
description.panel4: The Grim Reaper and the person sit together peacefully under the tree, waiting for the time to pass.

emotion: The author uses humor and irony to create a playful twist on the concept of "killing time", illustrating an unexpected moment of downtime shared between the Grim Reaper and his intended victim due to a daylight savings time change.

title: After Party.

description.panel1: The character waves goodbye to guests, thanking them for coming.
description.panel2: The character sits down on the couch, expressing relief that the night is over.
description.panel3: The character leans back on the couch, intending to relax.
description.panel4: The character reflects on the night, worrying about all the foolish things they said.

emotion: The author illustrates the anxiety and self-doubt that often follow social interactions, where one may overthink and stress about their own behavior after a party or social event.

title: Life Balance.

description.panel1: The character struggles to juggle "relationships," "health," and "work," while tripping over a toy.
description.panel2: The character is barely managing to hold the items while slipping on a banana peel.
description.panel3: The character loses balance and falls drastically while still holding onto "relationships," "health," and "work."
description.panel4: The character is on the ground with the items scattered, next to a sign saying "CAUTION VERY SLIPPERY ICE."

emotion: The author illustrates the challenge and inevitable difficulty of balancing relationships, health, and work, often leading to moments of failure despite efforts to manage them all.

title: Wavering.

description.panel1: A pink character waves at a yellow character, who appears confused.
description.panel2: The yellow character looks around uncertainly.
description.panel3: The pink character continues to wave, trying to get the yellow character's attention, who is still unsure if the wave is directed at them.
description.panel4: A purple character greets both the pink and yellow characters, clarifying the pink character's wave was for them both.

emotion: The author highlights the common social situation of misinterpreting whether someone's gesture is directed at us or not, and the relief that comes with clarity in social interactions.

title: Choose Your Own Adventure!

description.panel1: The character stays up late working on a laptop, indicated as Option A.
description.panel2: The character goes to bed early, indicated as Option B.
description.panel3: The character who chose Option A is shown tired, holding a cup and saying, "I'm so tired..."
description.panel4: The character who chose Option B is shown tired, holding a cup and saying, "I'm so tired..."

emotion: The author expresses the idea that regardless of whether one stays up late or goes to bed early, the result is still feeling tired.

title: Meet and Greet.

description.panel1: The pink character sits indoors and declares, "I need to go out and meet people!"
description.panel2: The blue character walks up to a mailbox and greets the pink character with a "Hi."
description.panel3: The blue character, looking confused, says "Huh?" while the pink character is seen in the background.
description.panel4: The pink character returns indoors and comments, "That's enough for one week."

emotion: The author humorously expresses that sometimes the effort to socialize can quickly feel overwhelming, and even a small interaction can seem sufficient for a longer period.

title: Greet em and Weep.

description.panel1: The green character asks the pink character, "Why are you crying?"
description.panel2: The pink character counters by asking the green character, "Why aren't you crying?"
description.panel3: The green character remains silent, contemplating the question.
description.panel4: Now the green character is crying while the pink character is smiling with tears.

emotion: The author likely wants to convey the idea that emotions, such as sadness, can be shared and contagious, and that questioning someone else's emotional state can lead to self-reflection and empathy.

title: Stung

description.panel1: A determined character vows to protect bees, stating they are dying and must be saved.
description.panel2: A bee demands fifty dollars, threatening to sting if the demand is not met.
description.panel3: The bee additionally claims it will take the character's girlfriend to show her a 'real man.'
description.panel4: The bee informs the character that his mother is allergic to bees, leaving the character sweating and worried.

emotion: The author highlights the irony and humor in the situation where the character's initial noble intent to protect bees is met with the bee's unexpected and aggressive demands, ultimately making the character's task more absurd and challenging.

title: Grapefruit

description.panel1: A grapefruit approaches a bunch of grapes and asks, "Grape?"
description.panel2: The grapes seem unimpressed and frown at the grapefruit.
description.panel3: The grapefruit reluctantly agrees to something by saying, "FIIINE..."
description.panel4: Surrounded by grapes with a sullen expression, the grapefruit says, "GRAPE!"

emotion: This comic humorously depicts a scenario where the grapefruit conforms to peer pressure from the grapes, highlighting themes of acceptance and the desire to fit in.

title: Hurticulture.

description.panel1: A content flower is smiling while sitting in a pot.
description.panel2: A masked, red-petaled flower says "SUP" to the content flower while being held in a purple pot by a hand.
description.panel3: The content flower looks confused and asks "WHO ARE YOU?" The masked flower responds, "SUP PLANT."
description.panel4: The content flower looks alarmed, exclaiming "NOOOOO!!" as the hand removes the smiling flower from its pot, with the masked flower remaining indifferent.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a situation where a new, more dominant presence replaces an established, content individual, causing distress and resistance from the latter.

title: Call in Cthulhu

description.panel1: A person stands on a cliff yelling "I CALL UPON YOU, CTHULHU!!" under a stormy sky.
description.panel2: Cthulhu angrily rises from the water, saying "YOU DARE AWAKEN ME FROM MY SLUMBER?!"
description.panel3: The person tells Cthulhu, "YOU SLEPT IN, DUDE. YOU'RE LATE FOR WORK."
description.panel4: Cthulhu looks worried, wearing a fast-food worker's hat, and says, "OH SHIT! MY BOSS IS GONNA KILL ME!!"

emotion: The author humorously contrasts the ominous and powerful mythological figure of Cthulhu with mundane, everyday concerns by depicting him as a fast-food worker who overslept and is worried about being late for work. This juxtaposition pokes fun at the contrast between cosmic horror and ordinary life.

title: Worked Up

description.panel1: The character is sitting dejectedly in front of a spilled cup.
description.panel2: The character angrily declares, "FUCK THIS! I'M GETTING MY LIFE TOGETHER!" with a determined expression.
description.panel3: The character calmly picks up the spilled cup.
description.panel4: The character, with a stern expression, says, "THAT'S ENOUGH PROGRESS FOR ONE DAY!"

emotion: The author uses humor to express how making even small, simple efforts can feel significant and challenging when trying to make a change in one's life.

title: Modern Horror Story

description.panel1: The character, appearing tired and frustrated, sits on a chair and says, "What a shitty day."
description.panel2: The character, now seated comfortably, optimistically says, "At least my fav show will make the bad feels go away" while turning on the TV.
description.panel3: The character, realizing that watching their favorite show isn't improving their mood, thinks, "It's not working?"
description.panel4: The character, now sweating and visibly distressed, panics as they realize, "IT'S NOT WORKING."

emotion: The author expresses the anxiety and distress of not finding solace in favorite activities or entertainment, reflecting the challenging reality that sometimes familiar comforts fail to alleviate a bad mood or stress.

title: Oh Vlad!

description.panel1: A character looks worried and says, "Oh no, blood in my stool" while standing next to a toilet.
description.panel2: Another character with a blue face pops up from behind the stall divider and excitedly asks, "Did somebody say blood?!"
description.panel3: The first character, with a relieved expression, exclaims, "Oh Vlad!"
description.panel4: The blue-faced character, Vlad, grins and says, "Don't flush," while the first character stands beside the toilet.

emotion: The author uses dark humor to playfully depict a vampire character (Vlad) who is excited by the mention of blood, even in an unusual context like a medical concern in a bathroom, blending morbidity with absurdity for comedic effect.

title: Boof.

description.panel1: An alien in a spaceship declares they are here to destroy Earth, and a human and a dog react in distress.
description.panel2: The dog responds to the alien by saying "boof."
description.panel3: The alien, charmed by the dog's cuteness, decides not to destroy the planet.
description.panel4: The dog is celebrated as Earth's savior with a banner while repeating "boof."

emotion: The author humorously highlights the disarming power of cuteness, showing how an innocent, simple act can diffuse a potentially catastrophic situation.

title: Friends and Enemies

description.panel1: The yellow character advises the blue character to keep their friends close, but the blue character responds by saying they don't have friends.
description.panel2: The yellow character then suggests keeping enemies close, to which the blue character also replies that they don't have enemies.
description.panel3: The yellow character, looking exasperated, asks if the blue character has anyone at all.
description.panel4: The yellow character, looking frustrated, says they will be the blue character's enemy.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the blue character's lack of social connections, demonstrating the yellow character's persistent efforts to find a role in the blue character's life, even if it means becoming their enemy.

title: Ghost Orientation

description.panel1: A blue ghost welcomes new ghosts (including a yellow ghost) to ghost orientation and tells them to pay attention.
description.panel2: The blue ghost demonstrates ghost activities by brushing up against a person and causing them to itch.
description.panel3: The person feels a random itch and starts scratching where the blue ghost brushed them.
description.panel4: The yellow ghost expresses surprise that causing itches is the ghost's eternal task, and the blue ghost confirms this.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the mundane duties of ghosts, suggesting that their eternal task is simply to cause minor annoyances like making people feel random itches.

title: Cat Calls

description.panel1: A blue character expresses frustration about being catcalled, and a green character agrees.
description.panel2: The blue character is surprised that the green character also experiences catcalling.
description.panel3: The green character answers a phone call and hears a cat's meow.
description.panel4: The green character, sweating and angry, yells into the phone, demanding to know what the caller wants.

emotion: The comic humorously highlights the frustration and confusion of being catcalled, using a literal cat call as a punchline to emphasize the absurdity.

title: Questionable.

description.panel1: A person in a purple shirt asks a person in an orange shirt if they are a good person and requests brutal honesty.
description.panel2: The person in the purple shirt tells the person in the orange shirt they can handle the truth.
description.panel3: The person in the orange shirt responds with "you", implying the purple-shirted person is not good.
description.panel4: The person in the purple shirt starts crying and admits they are "garbage," feeling intensely bad about themselves.

emotion: The author highlights the fragility of self-esteem and how blunt honesty, even when requested, can deeply hurt someone.

title: Try. Fail. Repeat.

description.panel1: The yellow character advises the blue character to keep trying after failing, but the blue character says they have already tried.
description.panel2: The yellow character asks how many times the blue character has tried.
description.panel3: The blue character looks intensely into the yellow character's eyes to check their persistence.
description.panel4: The blue character tells the yellow character that it is time to give up.

emotion: The author expresses the idea that although persistence is often advised, there may come a point when one must recognize the need to stop trying.

title: Mixed Signals

description.panel1: The blue character asks the pink character, "Are you alright?"
description.panel2: The pink character, despite having tears streaming down their face, responds with "Two thumbs up!"
description.panel3: The blue character points out, "But you're crying."
description.panel4: The pink character emphasizes, "Just focus on the thumbs," while the blue character looks unsure.

emotion: The author highlights the conflicting emotions and the superficiality of positive signals that may mask underlying sadness, emphasizing the idea of hiding true feelings behind a facade.

title: Drinking Problem

description.panel1: A green character, sweating from a run, asks a pink character for a drink.
description.panel2: The pink character hands over their drink to the green character, who thanks them.
description.panel3: The green character starts to drink from the bottle.
description.panel4: The green character, surprised and displeased, exclaims "MILK?!".

emotion: The author uses humor to depict the unexpected and potentially unpleasant surprise of drinking milk instead of a more typical refreshment like water or an energy drink after a run.

title: Driven Out.

description.panel1: An exorcist is holding a cross and commanding a red demon to leave a person.
description.panel2: The red demon appears reluctant but agrees to leave, exiting through a door.
description.panel3: The red demon politely asks the exorcist for a ride since it lives far away.
description.panel4: The red demon suggests getting fries during the drive, while the exorcist drives with a stoic expression.

emotion: The author humorously portrays an exorcism as a routine and almost friendly interaction, subverting typical expectations of such scenarios by depicting the demon as cooperative and polite.

title: Down

description.panel1: The character realizes that the internet is down.
description.panel2: The character contemplates clicking the refresh button all day.
description.panel3: The character jokes about stepping away from the computer to improve their life.
description.panel4: The character ends up continuously clicking the refresh button.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the common struggle of being overly dependent on the internet, opting to persistently try to restore connectivity rather than making more productive use of the downtime.

title: Fair Point

description.panel1: A person sternly says to a child, "LET'S GET THINGS STRAIGHT, KID."
description.panel2: The person continues, emphasizing, "LIFE IS HARD, LIFE ISN'T FAIR, AND NO ONE OWES YOU ANYTHING!"
description.panel3: The scene shifts to a hospital room where a new baby is being held by their mother with celebratory balloons in the background.
description.panel4: The new baby, responding to the previous person, says, "LISTEN, DUDE. I DIDN'T SIGN UP FOR THIS SHIT."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the unsolicited and often harsh advice adults give to children about life's difficulties, and contrasts it with a newborn's reaction, highlighting the absurdity that a newborn, just entering the world, hasn’t agreed to any of life's challenges either.

title: Positive Force

description.panel1: A yellow character asks the pink character named Gabe how he stays positive during hard times.
description.panel2: Gabe, the pink character, explains that haters want to see him upset.
description.panel3: Gabe confidently states that staying upset will never happen.
description.panel4: Gabe cheerfully waves at an angry red character, demonstrating his positive attitude.

emotion: The author expresses that maintaining a positive mindset despite external negativity is a powerful and resilient approach to dealing with adversity.

title: Sport

description.panel1: A blue character is holding a rock while a pink character looks on.
description.panel2: The blue character throws the rock at the pink character, saying, "I call it 'sport'".
description.panel3: The pink character looks at the rock and says, "Sport is fu".
description.panel4: The blue character tackles the pink character from behind as the pink character looks surprised and scared.

emotion: The author humorously portrays physical sports as being unexpectedly rough and potentially unpleasant, emphasizing the sudden change from fun to painful.

title: Pretty Penny.

description.panel1: The cashier informs the customer that the total is $4.99.
description.panel2: The customer gives the cashier a five-dollar bill and tells her to keep the change.
description.panel3: The customer jokes about the single penny change being insignificant.
description.panel4: The customer, now panicked and emotional, desperately says he needs the penny back.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts how a small amount of money, initially viewed as insignificant, can suddenly become important at the last moment.

title: Dream Breaker

description.panel1: The green character tells the blue character that they broke up with them in a dream last night.
description.panel2: The blue character reassures the green character that it was just a dream.
description.panel3: The green character, still curious, asks how the breakup happened in the dream.
description.panel4: The blue character takes out a notepad and humorously begins detailing the elaborate way the breakup happened in the dream.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how dreams can feel intensely real and affect our emotions upon waking, and highlights the amusing way people sometimes get caught up in the details of dream scenarios.

title: Slow Friends

description.panel1: A character expresses feeling lonely by saying, "I have no friends," to a snail.
description.panel2: The snail responds by offering friendship, saying, "I'll be your friend."
description.panel3: The snail asks if they can stay over for a few days, saying, "By the way, I need a place to crash for a few days."
description.panel4: The character enters their home and the snail says, "Welcome home, you are out of chips," indicating that they have made themselves comfortable.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the sometimes unexpected demands that can come with newfound friendships.

title: Good Reception

description.panel1: A purple character shows a phone to a blue character, saying "LOOK AT THIS FUNNY THING."
description.panel2: The blue character looks at the phone with a neutral expression.
description.panel3: The blue character responds with "heh," still maintaining a neutral expression.
description.panel4: The purple character, now teary-eyed with joy and sparkles around them, says, "HE LIKED IT."

emotion: The author highlights how even a minimal positive reaction can deeply affect someone who values validation and approval.

title: Highs and Lows

description.panel1: A short person asks a tall person, "What's the worst part about being tall?"
description.panel2: The tall person responds, "People always asking me if I play basketball," and the short person reacts with "Dang."
description.panel3: The tall person then asks the short person, "What's the worst part about being short?"
description.panel4: The short person replies, "Almost everything," leaving both characters standing silently.

emotion: The author highlights the different stereotypes and challenges associated with being tall or short, emphasizing through humor that while tall people often face annoying questions, short people feel they encounter a wider range of difficulties.

title: Having a Moment

description.panel1: Two characters are happily holding drinks together.
description.panel2: The purple character pauses and raises a fist, saying "EXCUSE ME FOR A MOMENT".
description.panel3: The purple character screams loudly in frustration, saying "AAAAAHHHHHHH!!".
description.panel4: The purple character returns to the blue character, smiling and raising their drink again, saying "K, BACK TO IT!".

emotion: The author expresses the idea of briefly taking time to express intense feelings or frustration and then returning to normal activities, possibly highlighting the importance of acknowledging and releasing emotions to maintain a positive state of mind.

title: Blank Canvas

description.panel1: A character is looking at a blank piece of paper with a pencil nearby, with the text "Don't let a blank canvas intimidate you."
description.panel2: The character starts drawing on the paper with the text "Start with a single line. Don't worry, just draw."
description.panel3: The character sweats and looks worried as they draw, with the text "...that's the worst line I have ever seen."
description.panel4: The character continues to draw more frantically, making the paper messier, with the text "Now you're just making it worse."

emotion: The author humorously highlights the anxiety and self-criticism that can come with starting a creative project, showing how the pressure to create something perfect can lead to increased stress and mistakes.

title: Marseducial.

description.panel1: One character advises another on how to survive a trip to Australia.
description.panel2: The advisor warns not to be seduced by koalas, mentioning they are infested with chlamydia.
description.panel3: The traveler looks at a koala clinging to a tree.
description.panel4: The koala seductively says, "You know you want it," and the traveler blushes.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the perceived dangers and misconceptions about wildlife in Australia, using the specific example of koalas and their association with chlamydia, and plays on the theme of seduction in an unexpected and funny context.

title: Guest Comic - Extra Fabulous

description.panel1: A blue character tells a blonde character, "You are beautiful."
description.panel2: The blonde character responds, "I have a boyfriend."
description.panel3: A pink character wearing sunglasses tells the blue character, "You are beautiful."
description.panel4: The blue and pink characters walk away happily together, leaving the blonde character behind.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the idea of mutual appreciation and being receptive to compliments, and highlights that a compliment doesn't always need a defensive response.

title: Wheelhouse

description.panel1: A character expresses feeling unmotivated to work out at home, considering it impossible.
description.panel2: The character sits pensively while hearing a "squeak squeak" noise.
description.panel3: The character looks towards the source of the noise, a hamster running energetically on its wheel.
description.panel4: The character, sweating and confused, wonders aloud "how the f***?" while the hamster continues to run.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the contrast between the character's lack of motivation and the hamster's relentless energy, suggesting feelings of irony and self-reflection about personal motivation.

title: Okay Then

description.panel1: The snake asks the turtle if everything will be okay, and the turtle responds with an emphatic "No."
description.panel2: The snake then asks if most things will be okay, and the turtle responds again with "No."
description.panel3: The snake asks if anything will be okay, and the turtle responds with "Also no."
description.panel4: The turtle finally adds that whether anything will be okay depends on the snake's definition of "okay."

emotion: The author highlights a pessimistic view of life where assurances of positivity ("okayness") are elusive and subjective to individual definitions and perspectives.

title: Stress Management

description.panel1: The character is stress eating, consuming a bag of chips with a worried expression.
description.panel2: The character is stress drinking, holding a wine bottle and glass, looking anxious.
description.panel3: The character is stress relaxing, sitting on a chair and sweating while watching TV.
description.panel4: The character is stress stressing, lying on the floor overwhelmed and sweating.

emotion: The author illustrates the various ways people try to manage their stress, humorously highlighting that these activities often fail to alleviate stress and might even exacerbate it.

title: Wolf Down.

description.panel1: A sheep asks Nate if he has seen Ben lately while Nate is eating something.
description.panel2: The sheep realizes Nate is eating another sheep (likely Ben) and exclaims that Nate is a wolf in disguise.
description.panel3: Nate clarifies that he is not a wolf but rather just a sick individual.
description.panel4: Nate continues to eat the other sheep unconcerned, while the asking sheep looks disturbed.

emotion: The author uses dark humor to highlight the shocking and disturbing nature of Nate's behavior by initially leading the reader to believe there may be an innocent explanation before revealing the twisted truth.

title: Cool Shirt

description.panel1: A blue character is bleeding and asking a green character for help.
description.panel2: The blue character tells the green character to use their shirt as a tourniquet to slow the bleeding.
description.panel3: The green character apologizes, saying they are contractually obligated to keep the shirt on.
description.panel4: The blue character reluctantly admits that the shirt is cool, and the green character thanks them.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the absurdity of prioritizing contractual obligations or material possessions over helping someone in a serious situation.

title: See You in Hell

description.panel1: A pink character angrily says "I'll see you in hell" to a green character, who responds confidently with "Counting on it."
description.panel2: The green character is in hell, saying "Hey Bill" to an off-screen character.
description.panel3: The pink character arrives in hell, and the green character exclaims, "Matt! You came!" The pink character responds, "Told ya I would!"
description.panel4: Both characters are smiling and sitting together in hell.

emotion: The author expresses a humorous take on loyalty and friendship, showing that true friends stick together even in the most unlikely and dire situations.

title: Performance Review

description.panel1: Two characters are sitting at a table, and one tells the other that it’s time for their performance review.
description.panel2: The first character praises the second character for doing amazing work, beyond their wildest dreams, describing it as absolutely perfect.
description.panel3: The first character then mentions that the second character did use one of their allotted sick days.
description.panel4: The first character tells the second character to clear out their office by the end of the day.

emotion: The author is likely highlighting the absurdity and inconsistency in some workplace performance reviews, where minor infractions like using a sick day can overshadow outstanding performance.

title: New Shoes

description.panel1: The character is happily wearing a new pair of shoes.
description.panel2: Another character bumps into the first character and yells, "WATCH WHERE YOU'RE GOIN'!"
description.panel3: The first character takes a shower while still wearing the new shoes.
description.panel4: The first character goes to bed, still wearing the new shoes and smiling contentedly.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the joy and attachment one can feel towards new shoes, to the point of refusing to take them off in any situation, regardless of the inconvenience.

title: Beat.

description.panel1: An older man angrily asks a younger person if their parents ever gave them a proper whooping, to which the younger person replies, "No, sir."
description.panel2: The older man exclaims that when he caused trouble, his parents beat him severely.
description.panel3: The older man, clenching his fists and looking furious, claims that his parents destroyed his tiny child body with their beatings.
description.panel4: The older man arrogantly states that he is a better person than the younger person because of the beatings he received.

emotion: The author is critiquing the mentality that physical punishment in childhood is necessary for personal development, highlighting its absurdity and the flawed notion of superiority it can create in some people.

title: Mom Strength

description.panel1: A child is holding a green teddy bear, and an adult with curly hair warns another adult that one day they will set their kid down for the last time.
description.panel2: The second adult, looking determined, refuses this idea, saying "We'll see about that!"
description.panel3: The determined adult is seen lifting weights and strengthening their arms.
description.panel4: Later, the now stronger adult is happily holding the child up in the air while saying, "C'mon, son! We're goin' shopping!"

emotion: The author humorously expresses the determination and strength parents, and particularly mothers, have to keep holding onto their children, both emotionally and physically, as long as they can.

title: Pointed Out

description.panel1: A porcupine expresses that no one likes being around it.
description.panel2: A turtle asks if it's because of the quills, and the porcupine responds that it's because it's boring.
description.panel3: The turtle simply responds with "oh."
description.panel4: The porcupine adds that the quills don't help either.

emotion: The author uses humor to convey that there may be multiple reasons why someone might feel isolated or unliked, and that perceived self-attributes (like being boring) might overshadow more obvious reasons (like having quills).

title: Sure

description.panel1: A yellow character asks a pink character, "You think I'll ever be happy?"
description.panel2: The pink character confidently responds, "As sure as the sun will rise."
description.panel3: Both characters continue to sit and look at the night sky in silence.
description.panel4: The pink character nervously sweats while looking at a watch that shows 9:50 am.

emotion: The comic humorously conveys the idea that while optimism can be reassuring, reality doesn't always align with expectations, leading to unexpected and sometimes comical situations.

title: Guest Comic - Cheer Up, Emo Kid

description.panel1: The Grim Reaper stands next to a frightened character, saying, "Time to go, bitch."
description.panel2: The Grim Reaper and the frightened character are in a car together, with the Grim Reaper saying, "It's a green light! Go!"
description.panel3: The Grim Reaper exits the car and says, "Thanks for the ride. I'm off to work!"
description.panel4: The Grim Reaper is seen entering a building labeled "Greener Pastures Retirement Home."

emotion: The comic humorously depicts the Grim Reaper needing a ride to work at a retirement home, suggesting a playful twist on the concept of death and inevitability.

title: Hard Read

description.panel1: A blue character wearing a headband claims they will read the mind of the pink character.
description.panel2: The blue character is alarmed and says they only hear screaming, while the pink character laughs.
description.panel3: The blue character asks if the screaming ever stops, with the pink character still laughing.
description.panel4: The pink character simply responds with "no."

emotion: The author is humorously depicting the inner turmoil or constant mental noise that people might experience, suggesting it's something that never ceases.

title: Sheltered.

description.panel1: A frog asks a turtle why it has a shell and the turtle responds that the shell protects it from physical harm.
description.panel2: The frog then asks how the turtle protects itself from emotional harm, to which the turtle replies that its aloof personality serves that purpose.
description.panel3: The frog inquires about psychological harm, and the turtle answers that its total apathy shields it from that.
description.panel4: Impressed, the frog exclaims that the turtle is indestructible.

emotion: The author humorously highlights various defenses individuals might use to protect themselves from different types of harm, portraying the turtle as having a comprehensive set of protections that make it seemingly invulnerable.

title: Check Up

description.panel1: The character suggests that it's important to check on your friends from time to time.
description.panel2: The character sends a message to a friend asking how they are.
description.panel3: The friend responds with "terrible," to which the character replies "ok."
description.panel4: The character proudly claims, "I'm such a good friend."

emotion: The author highlights the superficial nature of checking in on friends without genuinely engaging or caring about their well-being.

title: Knock out.

description.panel1: A person is reading a book and hears a knock on the door.
description.panel2: The person approaches the door and opens it slightly, asking "Hello?" to the person outside.
description.panel3: The person outside angrily tells the indoor person to "beat it," indicating that the conflict is between him and the door.
description.panel4: The person outside punches the door, expressing frustration and challenging the door, which responds mockingly.

emotion: The author humorously depicts an irrational, over-the-top conflict with an inanimate object (the door) to highlight absurdity and perhaps the human tendency to direct frustration toward non-living entities.

title: Big or Small

description.panel1: The blue character says they don't like small talk.
description.panel2: The blue character adds that they also don't like big talk.
description.panel3: The blue character clarifies they don't like any talk that falls between small talk and big talk.
description.panel4: The blue character, frustrated, asks the purple character what they just said, and the purple character replies with "so."

emotion: The author humorously depicts a character who claims not to like any form of conversation, highlighting an extreme preference for silence or non-communication.

title: Go with the Flow

description.panel1: The yellow fish asks the red fish why it can't fit in.
description.panel2: The yellow fish inquires if the red fish has tried being normal, to which the red fish responds "Nah."
description.panel3: The yellow fish suggests acting ordinary, but the red fish says "Nope."
description.panel4: The yellow fish advises being regular and not weird, but the red fish dismisses it, saying "Not happening."

emotion: The author highlights the importance of being true to oneself rather than conforming to societal norms or expectations.

title: Response Time

description.panel1: A character spills coffee from a mug, labeled "ON A GOOD DAY."
description.panel2: The character, still holding the empty cup, dismissively says, "EH, IT HAPPENS."
description.panel3: The same character spills coffee again, labeled "ON A BAD DAY."
description.panel4: The character, now on the floor and visibly upset, exclaims, "I CAN'T TAKE THIS ANYMORE!!"

emotion: The author illustrates that how we react to minor inconveniences like spilling coffee can vary greatly depending on our mood and overall state of mind. On a good day, small mishaps are easily shrugged off, but on a bad day, they can feel overwhelming.

title: Holy Ghost

description.panel1: A person says they are going as a sexy ghost this year.
description.panel2: A second person questions how the ghost costume is sexy.
description.panel3: The ghost person realizes that their costume is on incorrectly.
description.panel4: The ghost person turns around, revealing that the cut-out for their face is in the wrong place.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a misunderstanding or mistake in a costume design, showing that what one person intends as a creative concept can be perceived very differently if executed incorrectly.

title: Wrong Side on the Bed

description.panel1: A character lying on their side in bed thinks to themselves, "I should try sleeping on my back."
description.panel2: The character turns to lie on their back and a voice says, "FLIP OVER."
description.panel3: The voice continues to insist, "FLIP OVER!" as the character looks concerned.
description.panel4: The voice yells loudly, "FLIP OVER!!" and the character looks visibly distressed and sweating.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the struggle of attempting to change ingrained sleeping habits, highlighting the internal conflict and resistance one might face when trying to make even small changes in their routine.

title: Oh Boy...

description.panel1: A character joyfully pets a small dog, asking it if it's a good boy.
description.panel2: The character continues petting the dog enthusiastically and praising it.
description.panel3: The dog cautiously approaches another person, who looks like they're going to pet it too.
description.panel4: The character pleads with the dog, saying "Please... I really need this," while the dog looks uncertain.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts a character's desperate need for emotional validation and comfort from petting a dog, highlighting the lengths to which they will go to feel that connection.

title: Live Laugh Lone

description.panel1: A character is shown with a checkmark next to the word "LIVE," indicating that they are living.
description.panel2: The character is depicted laughing with a checkmark next to the word "LAUGH."
description.panel3: The character is seen without a checkmark next to the word "LOVE."
description.panel4: The character appears sad and is crying, with no checkmark next to the word "LOVE."

emotion: The author conveys that while the character has managed to live and laugh (checked off the first two items), they have not found love, as suggested by the character's sadness and the unchecked box next to "LOVE." This suggests a feeling of loneliness despite achieving other aspects of a fulfilling life.

title: Donor Don'tor

description.panel1: A green character is asked for ID by a pink cashier at a store counter.
description.panel2: The pink cashier notices that the green character is listed as an organ donor on their ID.
description.panel3: The green character asks the pink cashier for their heart, and the pink cashier reacts with confusion and refusal.
description.panel4: The pink cashier accuses the green character of lying about being an organ donor.

emotion: The author humorously explores the concept of organ donation by depicting a literal and absurd interpretation of an organ donor as someone who would give away their organs on the spot.

title: Isolated Incident

description.panel1: A yellow character asks a blue character if isolating during the pandemic has affected them.
description.panel2: Both characters are seen silently looking at each other.
description.panel3: The yellow character, sweating and nervous, admits they forgot the blue character can't hear their thoughts.
description.panel4: The blue character responds verbally, saying "Not at all."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the awkwardness and communication issues that can arise from prolonged isolation during the pandemic, highlighting how it might affect one's social skills and interactions.

title: Imposition

description.panel1: The character is lying on their stomach in bed with a caption stating that sleeping on the stomach is bad for you.
description.panel2: The character shifts to lying on their side and states, "OK, I'll sleep on my side."
description.panel3: The caption states that side sleeping is also bad, and the character responds, "Back it is," turning onto their back.
description.panel4: The character, now lying on their back, sees a caption saying this position is "not recommended."

emotion: What the author expresses through this comic: The comic humorously addresses the conflicting advice about different sleeping positions and highlights the frustration and confusion that can arise from trying to follow health recommendations.

title: Heartbroken

description.panel1: A blue character is holding a broken heart and looking sad while a pink character with a concerned expression stands beside them.
description.panel2: The pink character reaches out and offers to help, saying "Let me fix it."
description.panel3: The blue character is shown fixing the broken heart, stating "Good as new!"
description.panel4: The pink character expresses gratitude, saying "Thanks," while the blue character holds the restored heart.

emotion: The author illustrates the theme of emotional support and healing, showing that helping someone mend their broken heart can lead to mutual feelings of appreciation and gratitude.

title: Devil May Cry

description.panel1: The character is being interviewed and asked what motivated them to achieve something.
description.panel2: The character confidently responds that "the devil made me do it."
description.panel3: The news shows a TV screen announcing the character as a gold medalist and thanking the devil.
description.panel4: The devil is seen sitting in front of the TV, crying tears of pride, and saying, "I'm so proud."

emotion: The author humorously portrays the idea of attributing success to the devil's influence, in a positive light, suggesting that even the devil can feel pride and support for someone's achievements.

title: Size Matters.

description.panel1: A person is pointing to an anthropomorphic "Big Gov" character, declaring it's time for big government to go.
description.panel2: The same person is seen pushing the "Big Gov" character away, signaling it to "go on and get."
description.panel3: The person presents a smaller anthropomorphic character labeled "Small Gov" as their pride and joy.
description.panel4: The "Small Gov" character speaks, saying "Fuck the poor."

emotion: The comic criticizes the idea that advocating for smaller government automatically leads to better outcomes, highlighting that reducing the size of government can negatively impact vulnerable populations.

title: Chosen One

description.panel1: A figure with a beard is waking up Brad, telling him to wake up.
description.panel2: The bearded figure tells Brad that, according to a legend, he is the chosen one.
description.panel3: Brad, looking annoyed, asks the bearded figure if he is being serious.
description.panel4: Brad, now frustrated, exclaims that he cannot handle another problem, while the bearded figure apologizes.

emotion: The author uses humor to depict the frustration and annoyance of unexpectedly being burdened with significant responsibility, even when it's framed as something grand like being "the chosen one".

title: Who's Gonna Call

description.panel1: A pink character acknowledges the need to get rid of a ghost, and a yellow character suggests calling the Ghostbusters.
description.panel2: The pink character inquires if there is an app or website available for the Ghostbusters.
description.panel3: The yellow character responds that the Ghostbusters only take calls.
description.panel4: The pink character expresses frustration, implying they will be stuck with the ghost since they cannot make a call.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the modern preference for digital communication over traditional phone calls, presenting it as a barrier to solving a problem.

title: Sniff Test

description.panel1: The dog asks the person holding a box if it is a new thing, and the person confirms it.
description.panel2: The dog tells the person to wait.
description.panel3: The dog sniffs the box as the person continues to hold it.
description.panel4: The dog approves with an "OK!" after sniffing the box.

emotion: What the author expresses through this comic: The comic humorously illustrates the dog's instinctive need to sniff and inspect new things before accepting them.

title: Digging a Hole

description.panel1: A pink figure accuses a dog of burying bodies in the backyard. The dog denies it.
description.panel2: The pink figure suggests digging in the backyard, and the dog tells him to stop.
description.panel3: The pink figure questions if the dog wants to prevent finding bodies, and the dog emphatically denies it.
description.panel4: The dog insists it just doesn't want holes, while the pink figure sarcastically disbelieves him.

emotion: The author humorously portrays a situation where a simple act (like digging) is questioned with exaggerative assumptions, highlighting the contrast between suspicion and ordinary intentions.

title: Big Tail

description.panel1: A green character and a pink character are looking at a squirrel, with the pink character exclaiming about the size of the squirrel's behind.
description.panel2: The green character comments that the squirrel's behind is "thicc."
description.panel3: The green character and the pink character are lying in bed together; the pink character looks concerned.
description.panel4: The pink character asks the green character if they are thinking about the squirrel again, with the green character smiling sheepishly.

emotion: The comic humorously portrays the exaggerated focus and obsession of one character over an unusual detail (the squirrel's behind), eventually illustrating how this fixation extends humorously into their private moments.

title: Hands in the Air

description.panel1: A pink character points a gun at a blue character, demanding "Hands in the air!"
description.panel2: The pink character instructs the blue character to "Now wave them in the air like you just don't care!" while the blue character, now with raised hands, looks worried.
description.panel3: The blue character waves their hands slightly above their head, still looking distressed, while the pink character watches.
description.panel4: The pink character accuses the blue character, saying "YOU CARED A LITTLE BIT!!" and the blue character, now more nervous, responds "NO! I SWEAR!"

emotion: The comic humorously portrays the discrepancy between taking a commanded action literally versus the intended carefree attitude it typically implies, highlighting the misunderstanding in a high-stress situation.

title: Big Man

description.panel1: A yellow character says, "It takes a big man to admit he's wrong," while a red character asks, "How big?"
description.panel2: The yellow character points to the distance, directing the red character’s attention elsewhere.
description.panel3: The red and yellow characters look towards the horizon where a gigantic, pink character is visible behind some mountains.
description.panel4: The enormous pink character raises a hand and says, "my bad."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the phrase "it takes a big man to admit he's wrong" by depicting a literal giant admitting his mistake, playing on the dual meaning of "big man" as both metaphorical maturity and physical size.

title: Chain Breaker

description.panel1: A plant is shown absorbing sunlight, with the caption "The plant feeds from the sun."
description.panel2: A herbivorous rabbit is eating a plant, with the caption "The herbivore feeds on the plant."
description.panel3: A carnivorous fox is approaching the rabbit, looking like it will eat it, with the caption "The carnivore feeds on the."
description.panel4: The rabbit has pulled out a sword, surprising the fox, with the caption "Oh shit is that a sword?! This changes everything!"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the food chain and then subverts expectations by introducing an element (the rabbit wielding a sword) that drastically changes the typical predator-prey dynamic.

title: Free Your Mind

description.panel1: A character expresses difficulty relaxing and is advised to "free your mind" by another character.
description.panel2: The character is encouraged to let their mind be free, depicted by a small brain character.
description.panel3: The character, growing frustrated, tells the brain to leave them alone.
description.panel4: The character angrily insists they don't want the brain anymore, pushing it away.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the idea of freeing one's mind, which leads to a misunderstanding where the character literally tries to get rid of their brain in pursuit of relaxation.

title: Business End

description.panel1: A distressed, injured character asks a representative at an insurance desk about covering medical care.
description.panel2: The character emphasizes the urgency of needing medical care immediately due to their injury.
description.panel3: The insurance representative responds by suggesting an alternative solution rather than direct treatment.
description.panel4: The insurance representative proposes that the injured character consider "simply passing away."

emotion: The author uses dark humor to critique the inefficiency and absurdity of insurance companies, highlighting how they can sometimes offer unhelpful or inappropriate responses during critical situations.

title: Out of Ideas

description.panel1: The frog expresses that it has run out of ideas.
description.panel2: The turtle offers one of its many ideas to the frog.
description.panel3: The frog excitedly asks if the turtle has any good ideas.
description.panel4: The turtle admits that it does not have a single good idea.

emotion: The author highlights the irony of having many ideas but none that are valuable or useful.

title: Someone Better

description.panel1: A green character tells a pink character named Doug that there will always be someone better than him.
description.panel2: The green character confidently says that no one is better at being him than he is.
description.panel3: Both characters stand silently for a moment, contemplating the previous statements.
description.panel4: The green character proudly holds a trophy that says "Best Doug," highlighting his unique identity.

emotion: The author conveys the idea that while others may excel in various skills, each person is uniquely the best at being themselves, emphasizing the importance of self-acceptance and individuality.

title: Shadow Lord Dresses Up

description.panel1: The Shadow Lord asks if their cloak is finished, and their servant confirms that it is.
description.panel2: The Shadow Lord expresses relief that the cloak is finally done.
description.panel3: The servant reveals that they have made matching outfits for both of them.
description.panel4: The Shadow Lord and the servant are shown wearing matching red t-shirts.

emotion: The author uses humor to depict a typically ominous character, the Shadow Lord, in a light-hearted situation. The comic plays on the contrast between the Shadow Lord's dark, serious persona and the unexpected cheerful matching outfits, highlighting the theme that even the most intimidating figures can have light-hearted moments.

title: Spirited Conversation

description.panel1: A pink character is sitting under a tree, meditating.
description.panel2: A yellow character approaches the pink character and asks if they are trying to achieve enlightenment.
description.panel3: The pink character, with furrowed brows and a strained expression, responds that they are gathering energy.
description.panel4: The pink character reveals in enlightenment-like posture and glow that they are gathering energy to reply to a long text.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the idea that replying to a long text message often requires significant mental and emotional preparation, likening it to the pursuit of enlightenment.

title: Turtle Tension

description.panel1: The turtle expresses that it is constantly worried.
description.panel2: The frog advises the turtle to only worry about things it can control.
description.panel3: The turtle responds by saying its entire life is out of control.
description.panel4: The frog cheerfully concludes that the turtle then has nothing to worry about.

emotion: The author is humorously emphasizing the futility of worrying by suggesting that if one's life is entirely out of control, worrying is pointless since it won't change anything.

title: Just One More Hit

description.panel1: The character proudly announces that their work is finally finished, indicating their satisfaction with completing a task.
description.panel2: The character observes their work on a computer screen, noting that it has received 107 likes and a positive comment from a user named "funfan99."
description.panel3: The character continues to view their work on the screen, still showing the 107 likes and the comment from "funfan99."
description.panel4: The character, motivated by the positive feedback, decides to start creating again, saying "AGAIN."

emotion: The author highlights the addictive nature of social media validation and how positive feedback can drive continuous creation and a cycle of seeking more approval.

title: Poor Delivery.

description.panel1: A person expresses their dislike for corporations tracking their every move online and asks for agreement.
description.panel2: Another person, delivering a pizza, asks if the first person will leave a tip.
description.panel3: The first person, holding a phone, says that the pizza tracker shows the delivery should have been made two minutes ago, so they won't leave a tip.
description.panel4: The first person dismisses the delivery person, saying they need to check another tracking number for the next 12 hours.

emotion: The author highlights the irony of complaining about corporate tracking while heavily relying on and scrutinizing tracking technology for convenience in daily activities like pizza delivery.

title: Sunspicious

description.panel1: The Sun, appearing outside a window, invites the person inside to come outside, but the person refuses.
description.panel2: The Sun offers vitamin D as an incentive, and the person responds with indifference.
description.panel3: The Sun adds serotonin to the offer, prompting the person to reluctantly agree.
description.panel4: Once the person is outside, the Sun mockingly mentions skin cancer, and the person angrily responds with a curse.

emotion: The author uses humor and irony to comment on the mixed relationship people have with the sun, highlighting both its health benefits and potential dangers.

title: Toy Phone Who Dis?

description.panel1: A child hands a toy phone to an adult, saying, "it's fo you, daddy". The adult responds with gratitude, thinking it's a playful moment.
description.panel2: The adult humorously answers the toy phone, saying "HELLOOO?" in a playful manner.
description.panel3: The tone shifts dramatically as the adult hears a menacing message from the toy phone that says, "I HAVE YOUR WIFE. IF YOU HANG UP, SHE DIES", causing the adult to become anxious.
description.panel4: The adult is visibly distressed, sweating, and asks "HOW DID" before the voice on the toy phone interrupts with "SHUT UP AND DO EXACTLY AS I SAY."

emotion: The author humorously contrasts an innocent, playful interaction with a toy phone with a dark and unexpected twist, highlighting the absurdity of the situation and creating a comic effect through the juxtaposition.

title: Dead Tired

description.panel1: One character tells another who is working late, "You should really sleep." The working character responds, "I'll sleep when I'm dead."
description.panel2: Later, the now-dead character, depicted as a skeleton, says, "Aight, time to get some shut eye."
description.panel3: The skeleton character tries to sleep.
description.panel4: The skeleton character realizes, "Aw shit, I ain't got eyelids."

emotion: The author humorously portrays the irony of the phrase "I'll sleep when I'm dead" by illustrating that even in death, the character is unable to sleep due to the lack of eyelids. This comic emphasizes the importance of getting enough rest and not overworking oneself.

title: Friend or the End

description.panel1: The blue character asks the purple character to choose between "truth" or "dare," and the purple character chooses "dare."
description.panel2: The blue character dares the purple character to be their friend.
description.panel3: The purple character asks what will happen if they refuse, and the blue character responds with "death."
description.panel4: The blue character impatiently asks for a decision, and the purple character, now serious and contemplating, responds with, "I'm thinking!"

emotion: The author humorously highlights the awkwardness and pressure involved in forming friendships by exaggerating the stakes and responses in a playful manner.

title: Unquestionable

description.panel1: A green character gives dating advice, suggesting to ask questions as people love talking about themselves.
description.panel2: A blue character on a date asks their date, "What do you do for fun?".
description.panel3: The date responds, "I hate talking about myself. What do you do for fun?".
description.panel4: The blue character, with a face of slight distress, thinks to themselves, "this motherfucker".

emotion: The author expresses the humor in the awkwardness and unpredictability of dating advice and interactions, particularly when expectations are subverted.

title: First World Problem

description.panel1: A character excitedly looks at a blueprint for the 8th world and mentions their anticipation for someone's arrival.
description.panel2: The same character orders preparations for someone's arrival, implying a challenging encounter.
description.panel3: In a simple game-like setting, another character lightly taps a small enemy.
description.panel4: The character is humorously defeated by the small enemy, ending the game.

emotion: The author contrasts the expectation of a grand, challenging journey with the anticlimactic reality of an early, unexpected defeat, highlighting the humor and irony of "first world problems."

title: The Best

description.panel1: A frog says to a turtle, "You're the best!"
description.panel2: The turtle responds to the frog, "No, you're the best."
description.panel3: The frog acknowledges with a thought, "That makes way more sense, actually."
description.panel4: The turtle reacts with confusion, saying, "What on earth was I thinking?!"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a mutual exchange of compliments between the frog and the turtle, leading to a realization and a moment of self-doubt, showcasing the humorous nature of self-perception and humility.

title: Exchange Words

description.panel1: A person is confused and questioning why they keep forgetting words.
description.panel2: The person's brain, depicted as a separate character, says it needs to make room for a new word it just learned.
description.panel3: The brain removes the word "sauté" from the box labeled "words" while saying, "Bye, 'sauté!'"
description.panel4: The brain places the new word "bussy" into the box labeled "words."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how learning new vocabulary might sometimes cause previously known words to be forgotten, suggesting the brain has a limited capacity for retaining information.

title: Party Parting

description.panel1: A person stands up and tells his friends that he has to leave, despite it being a great party.
description.panel2: The friends, still sitting on the couch, remind him that it's his party and his house.
description.panel3: The host looks distressed and admits he doesn't want to host anymore, apologizing and expressing confusion.
description.panel4: The host leaves the room, closing the door behind him, while the friends sit on the couch looking bewildered.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the feeling of social exhaustion and the desire to escape social obligations, even if it's an event one is hosting. It highlights the unexpected and awkward moments that can arise from such social fatigue.

title: A Hole.

description.panel1: A bagel expresses feelings of emptiness and a desire to feel whole.
description.panel2: A donut hole enters the scene, responding to the term "hole."
description.panel3: The donut hole offers to complete the bagel, suggesting they fit together.
description.panel4: The bagel angrily rejects the offer, exclaiming, "I am a bagel you fuckin' freak!"

emotion: The comic plays humorously with the literal and metaphorical ideas of completeness and identity, illustrating how an attempt at solving a perceived problem can be met with strong resistance if there's a mismatch in understanding or context.

title: Better Than This

description.panel1: A green character expresses disappointment to a blue character, saying "You really let us down, man."
description.panel2: The blue character responds by saying "You're better than this" with a confident expression.
