description.panel3: The blue character laughs mischievously, saying "Better? Oh ho ho..."
description.panel4: The blue character, with an intense sarcastic expression, calls the green character "You sweet naive little baby bitch."

emotion: The author is highlighting a sarcastic and darkly humorous interaction, where superficial encouragement reveals deeper cynicism and contempt.

title: Name Drop

description.panel1: A pink character asks a yellow character for job interview tips.
description.panel2: The yellow character advises the pink character to be himself and calls him by name, Sean.
description.panel3: At the job interview, the interviewer asks the pink character his name, and he incorrectly responds with "Eric."
description.panel4: The pink character, now distressed and sweating, realizes he made a mistake by not stating his actual name, Sean.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the common anxiety and mental mishaps that can occur during a job interview, emphasizing how stress might cause one to forget even the most basic information, like their own name.

title: Say Yes to Life

description.panel1: A purple character tells a blue character to "say yes to life," and the blue character agrees.
description.panel2: The blue character is confronted by a large yellow character labeled "LIFE."
description.panel3: The yellow character ("LIFE") asks the blue character if they are "looking for an ass kicking."
description.panel4: The blue character looks alarmed, while the purple character nods confidently.

emotion: The author expresses in a humorous way that embracing life means accepting all parts of it, including the challenges and difficulties ("ass kickings") that come along with it.

title: My Pleasure

description.panel1: Two characters exchange pleasantries with one saying "A pleasure to meet you" and the other responding "Pleasure is all mine."
description.panel2: Later, one character is happily playing a video game on a couch.
description.panel3: The other character, outside the window, angrily shouts "Hey! That looks pleasurable!!"
description.panel4: The angry character outside demands that the pleasure belongs to them, shouting "I TOLD YOU! THAT SHIT'S MINE!! YOU HEAR ME?!"

emotion: The author humorously critiques the literal interpretation of common expressions and the absurdity of claiming exclusive rights over shared concepts like pleasure.

title: The Non-Shenanigans of Milo the Cat

description.panel1: The title of the comic, showing Milo the cat lying on its back with text "The Non-Shenanigans of Milo the Cat."
description.panel2: Milo tells Jonas that he left a gift on the doorstep, appearing pleased with himself.
description.panel3: Jonas, looking worried, asks if Milo left a dead bird.
description.panel4: Jonas, surprised and happy, discovers a cake with "I love you, Jonas!" written on it at the doorstep.

emotion: The author humorously subverts the expectation that cats usually bring undesirable "gifts" to their owners by instead showing that Milo left a thoughtful cake, highlighting an unexpected and sweet gesture.

title: Milo, the Worst Cat in the World!

description.panel1: The comic introduces Milo as "the worst cat in the world".
description.panel2: A character named Jonas asks Milo if he will get scratched for petting Milo.
description.panel3: Milo assures Jonas that he would never hurt him by scratching.
description.panel4: Jonas reluctantly pets Milo while expressing a sigh of resignation.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the often conflicting reputation of cats being affectionate yet unpredictable through the character of Milo, who is labeled as "the worst cat" but insists he wouldn't scratch, leaving the owner reluctantly engaging with Milo.

title: Milo, The Least Relatable Cat in the World!

description.panel1: The title introduces Milo as the least relatable cat in the world.
description.panel2: A person, Jonas, asks Milo if he is going to knock a cup off the counter.
description.panel3: Milo responds that knocking the cup off the counter would be rude.
description.panel4: Milo expresses affection for Jonas, who reacts with frustration.

emotion: The author humorously contrasts common cat behavior with the unexpected politeness and affection of Milo, highlighting the uniqueness of Milo's character.

title: Set the Mood

description.panel1: A blue character is sitting sadly with a downcast expression.
description.panel2: A pink character approaches the blue character, comforts them, and says, "Aww, don’t be sad."
description.panel3: A sign stating "No Sadness Allowed" is shown, with the pink character gesturing to it while the blue character still looks sad.
description.panel4: The pink character gives the blue character a stern look and says, "Final warning, ok?", while the blue character now looks forcedly cheerful and nervous.

emotion: The author illustratively expresses the idea that imposing forced happiness can be uncomfortable and unrealistic, highlighting the pressure to hide true emotions to conform to societal expectations of constant positivity.

title: Don't Shake the Baby

description.panel1: A yellow character holding a baby explains to a green character in a suit that they brought their baby to work for "Bring Your Kid to Work" day.
description.panel2: The green character greets the baby, extending their hand.
description.panel3: The yellow character looks alarmed as the green character accidentally shakes the baby.
description.panel4: The baby is shown sitting in a CEO's chair, implying that the shaken baby has now become the CEO.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the absurd idea that shaking the baby resulted in the baby becoming the CEO, emphasizing the unexpected and exaggerated consequences of the action.

title: Life Time

description.panel1: Life explains that 8 hours of the 24-hour day are for sleep.
description.panel2: Life states that 12 hours are dedicated to work-related activities.
description.panel3: Life mentions that 4 hours are reserved for chores and errands.
description.panel4: Life indicates that the remaining time is for enjoyment.

emotion: The author illustrates how a typical 24-hour day is divided between various essential activities, emphasizing the limited time available for personal enjoyment.

title: Turtle Hurdle

description.panel1: The turtle tells the frog, "I may not be the strongest…"
description.panel2: The turtle continues, "Or the funniest…"
description.panel3: The turtle adds, "Or the smartest…"
description.panel4: The frog asks, "Made yourself sad again?" The turtle responds, "yea".

emotion: The author expresses how self-deprecating thoughts can lead to sadness and highlights the importance of self-acceptance.

title: All Duressed Up

description.panel1: A yellow character asks a pink character why they are all dressed up while holding a cup.
description.panel2: The pink character responds that they have an important interview at 5 PM.
description.panel3: The yellow character points out that it is only 8 AM.
description.panel4: The pink character suddenly looks panicked and says, "It’s 8 AM already?! I’m not ready yet!"

emotion: The author uses humor to depict the anxiety and over-preparation some people experience when anticipating an important event, highlighting how they may overestimate the need to get ready well in advance.

title: Spout Off

description.panel1: A human character sits by a body of water and says "HEY!" to two fish.
description.panel2: The human character asks the fish, "ARE YOU STAYING HYDRATED??" and the fish respond with "not again..."
description.panel3: The human character enthusiastically reminds the fish, "DON'T FORGET TO DRINK WATER!! HYDRATE!!"
description.panel4: The two fish laugh, commenting to each other, with one saying "LMAO" and the other saying "HUMANS SUCK"

emotion: The author humorously highlights the irony of a human giving hydration advice to fish, underscoring how humans often impose their own logic onto situations where it's irrelevant.

title: Patreonized

description.panel1: The artist contemplates stopping making their comics, thinking they're awful.
description.panel2: A dark figure representing negative thoughts tells the artist to stop.
description.panel3: A figure labeled "Patron" offers support and encourages the artist to continue.
description.panel4: Rejuvenated by the support, the artist resumes drawing with newfound motivation.

emotion: The author highlights the struggle of self-doubt faced by creators and the powerful impact of having supportive patrons who encourage them to continue their work.

title: Laughing in Loud

description.panel1: The blue character texts the yellow character asking if they saw the meme they sent.
description.panel2: The yellow character replies enthusiastically, indicating they found the meme very funny.
description.panel3: The blue character responds, stating they can't stop laughing.
description.panel4: The yellow character replies humorously, using exaggerated expressions to indicate extreme laughter.

emotion: The author highlights the shared enjoyment and exaggeration commonly expressed in digital communications when friends share something funny, portraying the humorous and relatable reactions.

title: Growing Pain

description.panel1: A character is holding a radio beside a potted plant with the caption "Playing music to plants can promote growth."
description.panel2: The character is playing "Baby Shark" music to the plant.
description.panel3: The plant grows rapidly upon hearing the music, reaching towards the character.
description.panel4: The now oversized plant aggressively leans towards the character, causing them distress.

emotion: The author humorously suggests that while playing music to plants can promote growth, certain songs might lead to unexpected and overwhelming outcomes.

title: Undone.

description.panel1: A person asks the protagonist if they are done with their plate, and the protagonist reluctantly says "yes."
description.panel2: The protagonist watches as the person takes their plate away.
description.panel3: The text "10 YEARS LATER..." appears above the protagonist, who is now sitting alone in a room.
description.panel4: The protagonist, looking forward, says "I wasn't done with that plate."

emotion: The author is expressing the idea that sometimes, even small regrets can have lasting impacts and that seemingly trivial decisions may haunt individuals for a long time.

title: Hell or Bust

description.panel1: A person asks Death if they can wager their life on a game for a chance to survive.
description.panel2: Death responds affirmatively while confidently holding his scythe.
description.panel3: The person offers a laptop displaying a challenging game titled "Try Not to Cum With This Game Play."
description.panel4: Death nervously wipes sweat from his face, indicating the challenge is more daunting than expected.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the concept of making an unprecedented wager with Death using a ludicrously provocative and challenging game, highlighting Death's unexpected vulnerability.

title: Leisure Time

description.panel1: A character sits on a couch, looking stressed and thinking, "I'm too stressed. I need some leisure time."
description.panel2: The character considers options like watching TV or listening to music for leisure.
description.panel3: The character then thinks that perhaps they should read a book or learn a new language.
description.panel4: The character continues to feel stressed thinking about other productive tasks like exercising, cleaning, or reconnecting with friends.

emotion: The author expresses the difficulty of truly relaxing when feeling stressed, as even leisure time comes with the pressure to be productive.

title: Driven

description.panel1: A character states that it's easier to tackle a big project by starting with small tasks.
description.panel2: The character is washing dishes and advises doing some chores to get motivated.
description.panel3: Another character, clearly injured and bleeding, asks if they are being driven to the hospital.
description.panel4: The first character, still washing dishes, sweatily responds that they are working on it.

emotion: The author humorously portrays the concept of procrastination and misplaced priorities, showing how focusing on minor tasks can lead to neglecting urgent and important responsibilities.

title: Monster

description.panel1: A child tells an adult that there's a monster under their bed, and the adult questions it in disbelief.
description.panel2: The adult looks under the bed and exclaims in shock that there is indeed a monster.
description.panel3: The adult relays a message from the monster, saying that if the child ever misbehaves again...
description.panel4: The adult ominously concludes that the monster will kill the child.

emotion: The author humorously uses the concept of a monster under the bed to illustrate how an adult might use fear to discourage a child from misbehaving.

title: Break The Habit

description.panel1: A yellow character asks a blue character what they are doing, and the blue character replies that they are breaking a bad habit.
description.panel2: The blue character is strenuously swinging a hammer, with great effort, toward something off-panel.
description.panel3: The blue character's hammer just makes a small "plink" on a large rock labeled "Bad Habit."
description.panel4: The blue character sits down, looking exhausted and defeated, in front of the unchanged large rock labeled "Bad Habit."

emotion: The author is expressing the difficulty and frustration often encountered when trying to break bad habits, highlighting that despite our efforts, significant progress can sometimes be minimal and challenging to achieve.

title: Truly Free

description.panel1: The yellow character asks the blue character if they are free tonight.
description.panel2: The blue character gives a profound answer, saying that no one is truly free.
description.panel3: The yellow character looks surprised and contemplative, saying "oh my god..."
description.panel4: The blue character thinks to himself, "I can't believe that worked."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how a deep philosophical response can be used to avoid commitments or evade social plans.

title: Stuck

description.panel1: A person tells a doctor that a song is stuck in their head and the doctor suggests it can be surgically removed.
description.panel2: Two surgeons are in the operating room, locating the song in the person's head.
description.panel3: The surgeons identify the song as "Crazy Frog."
description.panel4: The patient is surprised and asks if they left it in, to which the doctor replies that they can't let it escape again.

emotion: The author uses humor to illustrate the frustration of having a catchy song stuck in one's head, comparing it to a scenario where surgical intervention is required to remove it, emphasizing how persistent and annoying such songs can be.

title: Just Like Old Times

description.panel1: Two characters unexpectedly recognize each other and call out each other's names, "Kyle?!" "Eric?!"
description.panel2: The characters express surprise and enthusiasm upon meeting again after a long time.
description.panel3: One character energetically asks the other what they have been doing for the past 15 years.
description.panel4: The second character responds with a simple "nothing," implying they have not done much in the intervening years.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the anticlimactic and often mundane nature of real-life experiences despite long gaps in time, poking fun at the expectation that long-separated acquaintances should have a lot of significant updates to share.

title: Apprehension

description.panel1: A pink character is welcomed to a website by a yellow character.
description.panel2: The yellow character suggests that the website is best experienced through their app.
description.panel3: The pink character declines the suggestion to use the app, saying "no thanks."
description.panel4: The yellow character aggressively expels the pink character from the website for not using the app.

emotion: The author is highlighting the aggressive push by some websites to force users into using their apps, often making the user experience on the website unpleasant if they don't comply.

title: Put a Ring on It

description.panel1: A character on one knee proposes with a ring, and the other character enthusiastically says "YES!" while wearing mittens.
description.panel2: The kneeling character points out that the other character needs to take off their mittens, to which the other character responds "OH RIGHT."
description.panel3: The standing character takes off their mittens, handing them to the kneeling character.
description.panel4: The kneeling character, sweating and seemingly stressed, says "WELL PUT IT ON" while awkwardly trying to help put the ring on the visibly uncomfortable standing character.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the practical complications and awkwardness that can arise during a romantic proposal.

title: The Heaviest Sigh

description.panel1: A character is seen closing their eyes and inhaling deeply.
description.panel2: The character starts exhaling with a long "sssss" sound, looking increasingly distressed.
description.panel3: The character continues exhaling with an even longer "sssss" sound, appearing more strained and exhausted.
description.panel4: The sound continues while a speech bubble pointing to a gravestone labeled "RIP" suggests the character's sigh was so heavy it metaphorically caused its demise.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the extreme weight of a heavy sigh, suggesting it can be so burdened and exhausting that it feels as though it could lead to one's own demise.

title: Important Research

description.panel1: A tired character holding a cup of coffee tells another character that they barely slept last night.
description.panel2: The tired character explains they had important research to do.
description.panel3: A flashback shows the character using their phone to search "WTF IS FIRE?"
description.panel4: The character in bed, with a phone says "Fascinating" as the time shows it's late at night.

emotion: The author humorously depicts how trivial internet searches can often turn into distractions that lead to staying up late, suggesting a contrast between perceived importance and actual frivolous distractions.

title: Boxed Out

description.panel1: A character is sitting inside a cardboard box, looking distressed.
description.panel2: Another character approaches and asks to buy the box for $50k.
description.panel3: A third character bids $100k for the box, and a fourth character ups it to $200k.
description.panel4: Two characters sit at a table; one comments that the cardboard box market is brutal, and the other states that box ownership is a fantasy.

emotion: The author is highlighting the absurdity and accessibility issues of the housing market by depicting a scenario where even a simple cardboard box is subject to high bids and unattainable prices, making ownership seem like an unrealistic dream.

title: High and Dry

description.panel1: An indoor potted plant is shown wilting and dry while outside it is raining heavily.
description.panel2: A healthy, flourishing flower is depicted outside in the rain.
description.panel3: The character sitting comfortably inside looks towards the drying plant beside them, holding a cup.
description.panel4: The character drinks from the cup, oblivious to the plant's need for water.

emotion: The author highlights the irony of the indoor plant wilting from lack of water despite the abundance of rain outside, emphasizing neglect or a lack of awareness in providing for the immediate needs of one's environment.

title: Agnosticket HD

description.panel1: A police officer asks a driver, "Do you know how fast you were going?"
description.panel2: The police officer follows up with a philosophical question, "Do any of us really know anything?"
description.panel3: Close-up of the police officer with an "Official Cop Hat" that is revealed to be empty inside the head area.
description.panel4: The police officer and the driver are sitting outside together, with the police officer wondering if they are part of something bigger, and the driver excitedly asking if the officer believes in dragons.

emotion: The author plays with the unexpected change in tone from a typical traffic stop to a deep philosophical conversation, humorously highlighting the randomness and sometimes whimsical nature of human interactions.

title: Unforgivable

description.panel1: A blue character advises a pink character that they need to be able to forgive themselves, and the pink character seemingly agrees.
description.panel2: The pink character addresses themselves (represented by another identical pink character) saying they have something to tell them.
description.panel3: The pink character, addressing their identical self, starts to say "I forgive you," but the other pink character interrupts, saying they forgot to put the laundry in the dryer.
description.panel4: The blue character watches as the pink character becomes angry and chases their identical self off-screen.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the difficulty of self-forgiveness, showing that while forgiving oneself seems important in theory, minor mistakes can still lead to significant self-criticism.

title: Holding the baby.

description.panel1: Someone abruptly hands a baby to a startled person.
description.panel2: The person holds the baby carefully, feeling anxious about its fragility.
description.panel3: The person gets angry at something the baby seems to have said.
description.panel4: The person, still holding the baby, challenges it aggressively and points at it with a pen.

emotion: The comic humorously illustrates the extreme and unrealistic reaction someone might have when left alone with a baby, depicting an exaggerated shift from anxiety to aggression.

title: Insecurity System

description.panel1: A person receives praise at work from their boss, who tells them "You've done great work here! Keep it up!"
description.panel2: The same person receives praise from their parents, who say "Your mom and I are so proud of you and all you've done."
description.panel3: The person, now alone, thinks to themselves, "Heh, I have them all fooled..."
description.panel4: The person's thought continues, "They have no idea I'm actually a dumbass."

emotion: The author is conveying the concept of imposter syndrome, where despite receiving praise and recognition from others, an individual might still feel inadequate or undeserving of their achievements, believing they've somehow deceived others into thinking they are competent.

title: Better Late Than Never

description.panel1: The yellow character angrily asks the blue character why they are late.
description.panel2: The blue character explains that every part of them wanted to stay home.
description.panel3: The blue character emphasizes that despite wanting to stay home, they still showed up.
description.panel4: Both characters stand silently, implying an understanding and acceptance of the situation.

emotion: The author expresses the concept of internal struggle and dedication, highlighting the effort and willpower it takes for someone to show up despite their strong desire to stay home. It's a humorous acknowledgement of the common feeling of reluctance to go to work or fulfill responsibilities.

title: Demon Daze

description.panel1: A person summoning a demon with a ritual.
description.panel2: The demon appears but expresses that it's not feeling up to it.
description.panel3: The person suggests trying the summoning later, and the demon agrees.
description.panel4: The demon is seen sitting on a chair, looking dejected and tired.

emotion: The comic humorously portrays a scenario where even supernatural beings can have off days, illustrating that everyone, regardless of their nature, can feel unmotivated and in need of rest.

title: Calling Card

description.panel1: Two characters, one yellow and one blue, are shaking hands, with the yellow character saying "A pleasure meeting you."
description.panel2: The blue character hands a card to the yellow character and says "Here's my card."
description.panel3: A close-up of the card reveals that it says "PLEASE LIKE ME."
description.panel4: The blue character looks nervous and sweaty while the yellow character holds the card.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the anxiety and desire for approval that often underlie social interactions, emphasizing vulnerability in the quest for social acceptance.

title: Roll Over

description.panel1: A person instructs their dog to roll over by saying, "ROLL OVER, BOY!"
description.panel2: The dog begins to roll over on the ground.
description.panel3: The person realizes that the dog is rolling down a hill and says, "UH OH".
description.panel4: The person repeats "UH OH" while the dog continues to roll further down the hill, disappearing from view.

emotion: The author uses humor to depict an unexpected consequence of a simple command, emphasizing the importance of awareness of the surroundings when giving instructions.

title: Terrific.

description.panel1: A character joyfully exclaims that it is a beautiful day.
description.panel2: The character decides to bask in the sun and let their mind wander.
description.panel3: The character lies down on the grass, looking content and relaxed.
description.panel4: The character suddenly has a realization and states, "I have done terrible things."

emotion: The author expresses a humorous contrast between the character's initial carefree mindset and how moments of relaxation can sometimes bring unsettling thoughts to the surface.

title: Behind the Times

description.panel1: The character wakes up and looks at the alarm clock, which shows that they have woken up an hour before their alarm is set to go off.
description.panel2: The character happily thinks about going back to sleep for more time.
description.panel3: The character wakes up again, this time noticing they have woken up four hours before their alarm.
description.panel4: The character sits up, wide awake, and states that they have never been more awake in their life.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the irony of how waking up briefly before your alarm feels rewarding, whereas waking up much earlier than needed often results in being fully awake and unable to go back to sleep.

title: Stuck in a Rut

description.panel1: A yellow character asks a pink character if everything is okay.
description.panel2: The pink character responds that they are stuck in a rut.
description.panel3: The yellow character points out that the pink character has been in that rut for six years.
description.panel4: The yellow character highlights how the pink character has even started decorating the rut, indicating they've been in it for too long.

emotion: The author uses humor to illustrate the concept of being stuck in a long-term state of stagnation, suggesting that the pink character has become so accustomed to their rut that they've made it a permanent situation.

title: Me Time

description.panel1: A yellow character lying on a couch expresses feeling burned out all the time, while a purple character sitting nearby with a phone responds humorously, suggesting they relate.
description.panel2: The yellow character shares their worry about friends secretly hating them, and the purple character agrees, saying they feel the same way.
description.panel3: The yellow character says they are broke and sad, and the purple character laughs, identifying with this feeling as well.
description.panel4: The yellow character, now alone, smirks confidently.

emotion: The author highlights the commonality of feeling misunderstood or alone despite shared experiences, and humorously hints at perseverance or self-acceptance even when not openly discussed.

title: Trash Talk

description.panel1: Two raccoons are sitting at a table with a gourmet meal in front of them.
description.panel2: A person expresses surprise, saying they thought raccoons ate trash.
description.panel3: The raccoons look at the person in silence.
description.panel4: One raccoon angrily asks if the person thinks they choose to eat trash, while the other raccoon tries to calm him down.

emotion: The author uses humor to challenge the stereotype that raccoons prefer eating trash, illustrating that given a choice, they would rather eat something better.

title: Last Word

description.panel1: The pink character is confused and asks the yellow character about the meaning of the word "rizz."
description.panel2: The yellow character explains that young people frequently create new words to replace old ones.
description.panel3: The pink character concludes that one must accept this trend or risk becoming outdated.
description.panel4: The pink character appears aged, indicating his resistance to adapting has made him feel old.

emotion: The author highlights how quickly language evolves among younger generations and humorously underscores the importance of adapting to stay relevant.

title: All Night

description.panel1: The blue character confidently asserts that he will never talk, calling the other character a "pig."
description.panel2: The green character, unbothered, responds that he has all night, calling the blue character a "dirtbag."
description.panel3: The green character mocks the blue character for having no plans on a Friday night.
description.panel4: The blue character, embarrassed, claims his friends had to cancel plans, while the green character sarcastically says, "Whatever you gotta tell yourself."

emotion: The author uses a humorous interrogation scene to highlight the blue character's loneliness and insecurity about not having social plans, while the green character mocks and doesn't believe his excuses.

title: Time Will Tell

description.panel1: A character declares that they could get a lot done if they just had the time.
description.panel2: The character struggles to take a large bag labeled "TIME" from a green arm labeled "LIFE."
description.panel3: The character is now holding the bag of "TIME."
description.panel4: The character admits, "Alright, you called my bluff," while holding the bag.

emotion: The author expressed that people often blame their lack of productivity on not having enough time, but when given the opportunity, they still may not make the most of it.

title: Are You Sure Though?

description.panel1: A character labeled "US GOV" asks another character if they really like net neutrality.
description.panel2: The other character firmly responds "yes."
description.panel3: "US GOV" questions the other character again with "but what about now?" and the character responds "YES!"
description.panel4: "US GOV" angrily insists with "but how about FUCKING YES HOLY SHIT!" indicating frustration.

emotion: The author is expressing a frustration at being repeatedly questioned about a stance, despite giving clear and consistent affirmative responses. It highlights the insistence and disbelief by institutions or authorities (represented by "US GOV") even when there is clear consensus or opinion from others.

title: The Bone Zone

description.panel1: A person bends down and asks a dog if it's ready to go somewhere.
description.panel2: The person, now sweating nervously, enthusiastically says, "The BONE ZONE!"
description.panel3: The dog stares at the person with a blank expression, sweating.
description.panel4: The dog remains with a cautious expression.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the dog's unease and confusion in reaction to the person's exaggerated excitement about going to "The Bone Zone." The cartoon plays on the contrast between the human's enthusiasm and the dog's wary response.

title: Spell and Speak

description.panel1: A witch uses a spell that gives a cat the power to talk.
description.panel2: The witch anticipates the cat to speak, but it remains silent.
description.panel3: The witch insists the cat says something, but the cat still does not respond.
description.panel4: The witch becomes frustrated, leading the cat to flee in fear.

emotion: The author humorously illustrated that forcing someone to do something against their nature, like making a cat talk, leads to frustration and unintended consequences.

title: Stray from God

description.panel1: A green character is making fun of a purple character, calling them a freak.
description.panel2: The pink character intervenes, saying that everyone is God's creation and should be respected.
description.panel3: God appears on a cloud, looking at the purple character and denying that He made them, expressing disgust.
description.panel4: The purple character is left confused and distressed, questioning the situation.

emotion: The author is highlighting themes of exclusion and the pain caused by rejection, even invoking the concept of divine creation to show how devastating and confusing it can be when someone feels they do not belong or are not accepted, even by a higher power.

title: Out of Step

description.panel1: A snake asks a turtle if it wants to join for a walk to get some steps in.
description.panel2: The turtle questions the concept of steps, and the snake explains that 10,000 steps a day are needed to stay healthy.
description.panel3: Both the snake and the turtle are silent, looking at each other.
description.panel4: The snake realizes the difficulty in achieving steps and says, "I am going to die."

emotion: The author humorously highlights the absurdity of a snake trying to achieve 10,000 steps a day, given that snakes do not have legs or take steps, poking fun at the generalized fitness advice.

title: Aww, Nuts

description.panel1: The squirrel is thinking that it could have sworn it buried the nuts around the area it's searching in.
description.panel2: The squirrel is visibly frustrated, thinking how it might have seriously lost the nuts and calling itself stupid.
description.panel3: The squirrel is feeling defeated, thinking that it is useless.
description.panel4: Twenty years later, a tree has grown from the buried nuts, with a bird sitting atop the tree and a person reading a book in its shade.

emotion: The author expresses that apparent mistakes or failures can sometimes lead to unintended positive outcomes over time. The squirrel's lost nuts ultimately led to the growth of a tree, providing benefits to others.

title: Too Real

description.panel1: A blue character is excitedly telling a yellow character that the RPG they are playing has the most realistic dialogue options ever.
description.panel2: The in-game character tells the player about a legend involving a golem within Mt. Makra.
description.panel3: The dialogue options provided to the player include repetitive choices such as "Wow, that's crazy" and "Haha yeah."
description.panel4: The blue character is frustrated and points out to the yellow character that the dialogue options are the same every time.

emotion: The author is highlighting the irony and disappointment when a game claims to have realistic dialogue options, but in reality, the options are repetitive and lack true variety.

title: Silly Mood

description.panel1: A purple bear wearing a hat asks a blue bear why they are in a silly mood.
description.panel2: The purple bear dances energetically while the blue bear stands still, looking unimpressed.
description.panel3: The scene darkens and the blue bear responds that without the silliness, darkness would consume them.
description.panel4: The purple bear stands on its head, continuing its silly antics, while the blue bear watches.

emotion: The author expresses the idea that being in a silly and joyful mood can be a way to combat feelings of darkness and negativity. The comic suggests that lightheartedness and humor can serve as a coping mechanism to fend off darker emotions.

title: Finishing Touch

description.panel1: A character is tickling their own sides, making a tickling motion.
description.panel2: A brain, representing the character's thoughts, acknowledges that it knows the character is just tickling themselves.
description.panel3: The character is now sitting at a desk with a laptop, appearing idle or bored.
description.panel4: The brain, now surprised and panicking, exclaims, "I'M HAVING SEX!!!"

emotion: The author highlights the brain's awareness and its capacity to differentiate between self-induced and external stimuli, using humor to depict how the brain might misinterpret sexual activity when viewed online or imagined vividly.

title: Lord Knows

description.panel1: A person is angrily asking God why bad things are happening.
description.panel2: God responds with confusion, stating that He has no idea.
description.panel3: God, appearing frustrated, explains that He is trying His best.
description.panel4: The person comforts the now-sobbing God, who admits that it’s all overwhelming at times.

emotion: The author expresses the idea that even divine beings can feel overwhelmed by the challenges they face, highlighting a shared vulnerability and the importance of empathy.

title: Intrusive Thoughts

description.panel1: A turtle asks a frog if it ever gets intrusive thoughts.
description.panel2: The frog anxiously responds that it gets intrusive thoughts all the time and expresses its dislike for them.
description.panel3: The frog's positive thoughts appear, saying "you're the best" and "you are enough."
description.panel4: The frog, overwhelmed by the positive thoughts, shouts "SHUT UP!"

emotion: The author illustrates the irony and struggle of dealing with intrusive thoughts, showing that even positive affirmations can become overwhelming and frustrating at times.

title: Wild Life

description.panel1: A rabbit is panicking and thinking it will starve and die.
description.panel2: The rabbit sees food and feels relieved that it will survive.
description.panel3: The rabbit hears a twig snap and becomes anxious again.
description.panel4: Two onlookers wish they could be as carefree as the rabbit.

emotion: The author is expressing that even seemingly simple and carefree lives, like that of a rabbit, can have their own stresses and anxieties.

title: Da Bull Trouble

description.panel1: A character excitedly shows off their new cave painting to another character.
description.panel2: The other character questions why the painting depicts a bull.
description.panel3: The questioning character points out that there is already a bull painting on the wall.
description.panel4: The character who made the new painting is puzzled about why there would be a need for more than one bull painting.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the concept of redundancy and the differing perspectives on the value and necessity of creating additional art when a similar piece already exists.

title: Happy Lapse

description.panel1: Two characters are walking outside, with one asking "Ready to go?" and the other responding "Yep!"
description.panel2: The character in yellow suddenly exclaims "Ooops!"
description.panel3: The character in pink asks "Did you forget something?" and the character in yellow responds "haha, yeah" while looking embarrassed.
description.panel4: The character in yellow reveals, "I forgot how to enjoy life again," looking distressed.

emotion: The author portrays a struggle with rediscovering joy and the difficulty of reconnecting with happiness after a period of forgetting or losing the ability to enjoy life's experiences.

title: Leveled.

description.panel1: A character sitting on a couch says, "I wish life was more like a video game."
description.panel2: The character silently sits on the couch, looking at their hands.
description.panel3: A dramatic text appears saying, "ENNUI LEVEL UP!"
description.panel4: The character continues to sit on the couch, with a simple, content look on their face.

emotion: The author highlights the character's desire for life to be more exciting and gamified, yet humorously reveals that, in reality, their feelings of boredom (ennui) have just increased, showcasing life's mundane nature.

title: Joy Thief

description.panel1: A green character looks at a phone screen showing a popular artist named Amy with 1.2 million followers and thinks, "I shouldn't compare myself to others."
description.panel2: The green character is drawing, focusing on their own creative work while thinking, "My creative journey is all that matters."
description.panel3: The green character shows their drawing to a yellow character, who responds with, “Nice.”
description.panel4: The green character, after hearing the compliment, looks at the drawing and the phone screen again and thinks, “It looks like something this artist would make.”

emotion: The author expresses the idea that comparing oneself to others, especially popular figures, can steal the joy and originality from one's own creative journey.

title: Splash Damage

description.panel1: A person is happily telling a small potted plant that it is time to be watered.
description.panel2: The person is pouring a large amount of water onto the plant.
description.panel3: The plant is coughing and looking distressed due to the excessive amount of water.
description.panel4: The person assumes the plant is satisfied and states that the water should last for a week or two, while the plant looks upset and exhausted.

emotion: The author highlights the common misconception that more effort (or resources, in this case water) is always better, showing the importance of giving an appropriate amount and understanding actual needs. The comic humorously exaggerates the consequences of overwatering a plant to emphasize the point.

title: Against the Wall

description.panel1: A character realizes they are trapped in a "walled garden" along with a figure labeled "Social Media".
description.panel2: The character appears resigned or content, saying they like it in the "walled garden".
description.panel3: Suddenly, the character sees the "Social Media" figure setting fire to the wall and asks what they are doing.
description.panel4: The "Social Media" figure is fully engulfed in flames, with a sinister expression, while the character looks on in shock and panic.

emotion: The author highlights the deceptive allure and potential dangers of social media, demonstrating how users can become complacent in their restricted environments ("walled gardens"), only to face unexpected and destructive consequences.

title: Children are our Future.

description.panel1: One character says, "You know, they say children are our future," while another character responds, "Really?!"
description.panel2: The second character asks, "Your kid is my future?" and the first character confirms, "Yep."
description.panel3: The second character looks at the child and awkwardly scratches their head.
description.panel4: The child asks the second character, "Do I ever get laid?" and the second character looks distressed and sweaty.

emotion: The author uses humor to highlight the awkward and uncertain expectations that adults place on children when thinking about the future and the exaggerated pressures and anxieties that may come with such expectations.

title: Sweat Relief

description.panel1: A character expresses intense thirst.
description.panel2: The character is seen walking towards a plant.
description.panel3: The plant speaks, revealing that it knows a dark secret about the character hiding bodies.
description.panel4: The character becomes extremely nervous and begins to sweat profusely upon hearing the plant's statement.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates an absurd scenario that escalates quickly, showing the character's exaggerated reaction to the plant's unexpected and ominous accusation.

title: Planned Obsolescence

description.panel1: A person expresses frustration that their toaster broke shortly after they bought it.
description.panel2: The toaster explains it was designed to stop working due to "planned obsolescence."
description.panel3: The person sympathetically tells the toaster they understand completely.
description.panel4: The person declares they also intend to stop working prematurely.

emotion: The author uses humor to critique the concept of planned obsolescence, highlighting the frustration consumers feel when products are intentionally designed to fail early.

title: Under Armor

description.panel1: A person enthusiastically pulls the underwear of another person and says, "Wedgie time, nerd!"
description.panel2: The action of the wedgie highlights sparkling and impressive underwear.
description.panel3: The person giving the wedgie reacts with shock and exaggerated sweating.
description.panel4: The person who received the wedgie comments, "Activated my thirst trap…"

emotion: The comic humorously depicts a scenario where a wedgie prank backfires on the bully, as the target's unexpected attire causes an amusing and surprising reaction.

title: How Do You Stand in the Shower?

description.panel1: A person is standing in the shower facing towards the water stream, with a letter "A" above them.
description.panel2: A person is standing in the shower facing away from the water stream, with a letter "B" above them.
description.panel3: A person is standing in the shower facing sideways, with a letter "C" above them, and letting the water stream hit their chest.
description.panel4: A person is standing in the shower bending over, with a letter "D" above them, and letting the water stream hit their back.

emotion: The comic humorously showcases the different ways people might stand in the shower while being hit by the water stream, highlighting the variety in such a common daily routine.

title: Note to Self

description.panel1: A character is lying in bed, seemingly about to sleep, with an alarm clock on the nightstand.
description.panel2: The character suddenly sits up, looking excited, and exclaims that they have just gotten a great idea.
description.panel3: The character decides to write their idea down and grabs a notebook and pen.
description.panel4: The next morning, the character looks at the notebook and, instead of the great idea, sees they had written "GIVE UP."

emotion: The author is conveying a humorous situation where an idea that seemed brilliant during the night turns out to be trivial or nonsensical upon waking, illustrating the unreliable nature of late-night inspirations.

title: Never Better

description.panel1: A frog asks a turtle, "How've you been?".
description.panel2: The turtle responds, "Never been better".
description.panel3: The frog, looking confused, asks, "Is that good or bad?".
description.panel4: The turtle, now hiding in its shell, doesn't respond, prompting the frog to say, "Answer me, turtle".

emotion: The comic humorously depicts the ambiguity and potential confusion in interpreting common phrases, highlighting the difficulty in understanding whether "never been better" is a positive or negative statement.

title: Risky Text

description.panel1: A character is nervously holding a phone, thinking "man, that's a risky text I just sent..."
description.panel2: The text on the phone screen reads "I love you," with speech bubbles indicating a pending response.
description.panel3: The phone screen shows that the message was sent to "DAD," who responded with "love you too."
description.panel4: The character is crying tears of joy while looking at the phone.

emotion: The author expressed the anxiety and relief one might feel after sending an emotionally vulnerable message, showing the positive response from a loved one (in this case, the character's father), which leads to a heartfelt emotional reaction.

title: Death Count

description.panel1: The character is frustrated and expresses disbelief in the usefulness of math in real life.
description.panel2: A ninja appears and threatens the character, demanding the integral of a function.
description.panel3: The character panics and struggles to answer the math question.
description.panel4: The ninja reveals they were joking and reassures the character that they will never have to use that math in real life.

emotion: The author humorously conveys the sentiment that certain advanced math concepts often seem irrelevant and unlikely to be used in everyday life.

title: Silent Treatment

description.panel1: The extrovert tells the introvert to talk more, assuring them they will enjoy it eventually.
description.panel2: The introvert thanks the extrovert and says they also have some advice.
description.panel3: The introvert asks the extrovert if they have ever tried something, prompting curiosity.
description.panel4: The introvert bluntly tells the extrovert to try shutting up, implying it can be beneficial.

emotion: The author illustrates the clash between extroverted and introverted personalities, emphasizing that while extroverts may feel talking more is beneficial, introverts may find silence just as valuable.

title: Outreach

description.panel1: A dinosaur is angrily roaring, and a small turtle asks why the dinosaur is so mad.
description.panel2: The dinosaur shouts that it cannot hug anybody.
description.panel3: The turtle suggests that the dinosaur can hug it.
description.panel4: The dinosaur attempts to hug the turtle, holding it in its tiny arms, finding solace.

emotion: The author highlights the frustration caused by physical limitations and shows that empathy and a simple solution can alleviate distress, emphasizing the effectiveness of small acts of kindness.

title: Turn On

description.panel1: A character is peacefully lying under a tree with a relaxed expression.
description.panel2: A brain with legs approaches a switch labeled "HORNY SWITCH" and is about to flip it on.
description.panel3: The brain flips the switch to the "ON" position making a "KACHUNK" sound.
description.panel4: The previously relaxed character now looks desperate and is sweating, saying, "I GOTTA LOOK AT A BUTT!!"

emotion: The author uses humor to depict how sudden and strong impulses, like sexual desire, can take over a person's calm state when triggered unexpectedly.

title: All too Common.

description.panel1: An officer hands a sketch to the police chief, eager to reveal what the suspect looks like.
description.panel2: The police chief looks at the sketch, which shows a simple, nearly featureless face.
description.panel3: The police chief reacts with skepticism at the sketch.
description.panel4: Another officer joins, and despite the simple sketch, they query if it accurately represents the suspect.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the often overly simplistic or unhelpful nature of police sketches, highlighting the challenge of identifying suspects based on vague sketches.

title: Gone to the Dogs

description.panel1: A character with arms outstretched greets a dog who sniffs him and says "aww, hey there girl".
description.panel2: The character assumes the dog is reacting to his own dog, saying "haha, you must smell my dog".
description.panel3: The character, now confronted by a DEA officer, worriedly says "I swear..." while the dog continues to sniff him.
description.panel4: Later, the character, addressing his own dog lounging with a chewing toy, says "YOU GOT ME IN BIG TROUBLE!"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how an innocent situation with a dog, mistakenly sniffing for illegal substances, can lead to unexpected trouble with law enforcement.

title: Hidden Talent

description.panel1: A character confidently asks if the reader has major life problems.
description.panel2: The character offers a life hack, moving towards a rock.
description.panel3: The character demonstrates the life hack by hiding under the rock.
description.panel4: The character, now sweating, suggests hiding forever as a solution.

emotion: The author uses humor to illustrate the sometimes unrealistic or impractical nature of life hacks, highlighting the idea that hiding from problems is not a viable long-term solution.

title: Unsubscribe

description.panel1: A character labeled "Business" offers some new emails to a person.
description.panel2: The person requests to unsubscribe from the emails, and "Business" acknowledges that they are unsubscribed.
description.panel3: The person expresses disbelief and "Business" confirms with a nod.
description.panel4: Despite being unsubscribed, the person is bombarded with more emails from "Business," who laughs maniacally.

emotion: The author highlights the frustrating and often ineffective process of trying to unsubscribe from unwanted email lists, where requests to unsubscribe are frequently ignored or not properly implemented.

title: Timed Out

description.panel1: The character sits on a couch thinking that if they leave now, they will get to work with time to spare.
description.panel2: Ten minutes later, the character still on the couch now thinks that if they leave immediately, they'll arrive right on time.
description.panel3: Another ten minutes later, still on the couch, the character thinks they'll be just a little late if they leave right now.
description.panel4: Ten more minutes later, the character, now visibly distressed, wonders why they are like this.

emotion: The author illustrates a common struggle with procrastination and self-frustration, highlighting how delay in taking action can lead to unnecessary stress and lateness.

title: Hugs Not Drugs.

description.panel1: A patient asks a doctor if they have any drugs to ease the pain.
description.panel2: The doctor suggests giving a hug instead of drugs, saying "Hugs, not drugs."
description.panel3: The doctor gives the patient a hug as a treatment.
description.panel4: The patient later questions a $1500 bill over the phone and is told that their policy does not cover hugs.

emotion: The author humorously criticizes the high cost and absurdities of medical billing and insurance policies.

title: Sound Bite

description.panel1: A green character asks for ideas for the next microwave model at a meeting.
description.panel2: A blue character suggests adding more beeps.
description.panel3: The characters around the table enthusiastically agree and also suggest more beeps.
description.panel4: The green character triumphantly declares, "More beeps! More beeps! More beeps! More beeps!" while raising their arms in excitement; the others cheer in agreement.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a brainstorming session where the participants agree on adding more beeps as the main feature for a new microwave model, mocking the repetitive and trivial nature of corporate decision-making processes.

title: From You

description.panel1: An adult praises a child for their drawing.
description.panel2: The adult gives the child new sketchbooks to encourage their drawing.
description.panel3: The adult offers to drive the child to an art club.
description.panel4: The adult acknowledges the child's artistic talent, admitting that it did not come from them.

emotion: The author expresses the importance of recognizing, encouraging, and supporting a child's talents and passions, even if those talents are not shared by the parent or guardian.

title: Remember

description.panel1: Two characters are walking together on grass.
description.panel2: One character looks at the other and asks, "Hey, remember when we..."
description.panel3: The other character has disappeared, leaving the first character alone and sad.
description.panel4: The remaining character sits down, looking pensive, and says, "I'll remember for the both of us."

emotion: The author expresses themes of loss, memory, and carrying the burden of remembering shared experiences when a companion is no longer present.

title: Tells It Like It Is

description.panel1: A green character is praised by a pink character for being outspoken and honest.
description.panel2: The green character confidently points to a rabbit and identifies it as a rabbit.
description.panel3: The rabbit contradicts the green character by saying "QUACK!"
description.panel4: The pink and purple characters look angry and confused, while the green character looks bewildered.

emotion: The author highlights the irony and humor when someone who is perceived as always being correct and outspoken is proven wrong by an unexpected source—in this case, a rabbit that quacks like a duck.

title: Sesquipedalian

description.panel1: A pink character tells a green character they don't like to talk much, and the green character responds with "How loquacious."
description.panel2: The pink character questions the green character's statement, and the green character mumbles affirmatively.
description.panel3: The green character nervously looks up the definition of "loquacious" on their phone.
description.panel4: The green character finds out that "loquacious" means "talkative" and appears embarrassed.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the irony of using a sophisticated word ("loquacious") to describe someone who claims not to talk much, only to reveal that the character didn't know the meaning of the word and ends up embarrassed.

title: Final Talks

description.panel1: Two characters, representing yellow and pink team members, announce to a social media character that they can use AI to write posts and generate pictures.
description.panel2: The yellow and pink characters realize that the social media character doesn't need them anymore, and confirm it.
description.panel3: The yellow and pink characters decide to leave the social media character to manage things on its own.
description.panel4: The yellow and pink characters celebrate their newfound freedom outdoors, feeling liberated.

emotion: The author is expressing the idea that the advent of AI technology can take over tasks previously handled by humans, potentially freeing them from certain responsibilities.

title: Best Life

description.panel1: A frog states to a turtle, "I'm living my best life."
description.panel2: The turtle responds, questioning if this is truly the frog's best life and the frog confirms it is.
description.panel3: The frog and turtle remain silent, with the frog looking despondent.
description.panel4: The turtle apologizes to the frog, and the frog responds with "it's ok."

emotion: The author uses the interaction between the frog and the turtle to highlight the disparity between one's perception of living their best life and the reality of their situation, potentially expressing themes of unfulfilled satisfaction or complacency.

title: Bad Girl

description.panel1: The purple character advises the yellow character to avoid sudden movements because the dog bites if threatened.
description.panel2: The purple character introduces the yellow character to the blue character and the dog.
description.panel3: The yellow character says "Hello, nice to meet you" and extends a hand towards the dog.
description.panel4: The dog bites the yellow character, and the purple character exclaims, "Dude! I warned you!"

emotion: The author expresses the importance of listening to advice and being cautious around animals that may feel threatened.

title: Power in Numbers

description.panel1: A person is saying "Remember, there is power in numbers!" while gesturing to two other people standing beside them.
description.panel2: The person points to a large number "5" and says, "Like this five over here, for example."
description.panel3: The person states "Five can be a very powerful number" with a confident look.
description.panel4: The number "5" is seen crushing the person, covering them in blood.

emotion: The author uses humor to depict the phrase "power in numbers" through a literal and exaggerated visual, ending with the ironic and dark twist that a powerful number can be dangerous.

title: Bottoms Up.

description.panel1: One character tells another "Don't talk to me until I've had my coffee!" while holding a coffee mug.
description.panel2: The second character takes the coffee mug from the first character's hand.
description.panel3: The second character throws the coffee mug away, spilling the coffee.
description.panel4: Both characters stand quietly next to each other without speaking.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the common expression about needing coffee to function properly by showing a literal and exaggerated reaction that solves the problem of not being ready to talk – by eliminating the coffee itself.

title: You Better Watch Out.

description.panel1: Santa Claus is shown eating a cookie and drinking milk next to a table.
description.panel2: Santa Claus looks satisfied and is holding the plate of cookies.
description.panel3: Santa Claus continues eating the cookies, unaware of someone with glaring eyes watching him from the shadows.
description.panel4: Santa Claus is lying on the ground, seemingly unconscious after eating the cookies, while the blue character who is watching him looks worried.

emotion: The comic humorously suggests that someone might have spiked the cookies Santa was eating, leading to his collapse and the blue character's concern, highlighting a comedic twist on the traditional tale of leaving cookies for Santa.

title: Going a Distance.

description.panel1: A pink character enthusiastically tells a green character that they need to leave to train for a marathon.
description.panel2: The pink character is intensely running at full speed.
description.panel3: The pink character is exhausted, sweating profusely, and trying to catch their breath, thinking they've already run a considerable distance.
description.panel4: The green character is standing calmly while the pink character is shown to be exhausted and likely having run a very short distance.

emotion: The comic humorously highlights the difference between enthusiasm and actual endurance, often experienced by beginners who overestimate their stamina and find themselves quickly exhausted.

title: Bark Bark.

description.panel1: A dog is barking outside under the sun with a blue sky.
description.panel2: The dog continues barking as the sun sets.
description.panel3: The dog writes "BARK BARK" in a diary while inside the house.
description.panel4: The dog is sleeping and dreaming about barking under the sun again.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a dog's simple and repetitive life, encompassing day-to-day activities and dreams, dominated by barking.

title: Language Barrier

description.panel1: A person accidentally steps on a dog's paw, causing the dog to yelp in pain.
description.panel2: The person apologizes to the dog, expressing regret for causing pain.
description.panel3: The person continues to apologize, emphasizing their concern for the dog's well-being.
description.panel4: The person offers to make it up to the dog by suggesting they go on a nice long walk later.

emotion: The author expresses the idea of acknowledging and addressing accidental harm caused to others, emphasizing empathy, concern, and a desire to make amends through a relatable and heartwarming interaction between a person and their pet.

title: Horse Drawn.

description.panel1: A blue character asks a green character to draw a horse, and the green character agrees.
description.panel2: The green character expresses frustration about how the head of the horse looks.
description.panel3: The green character further criticizes their drawing, especially the legs.
description.panel4: The green character, now holding a gun, tells the blue character not to mention the poorly drawn horse.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the frustration and insecurity that can come with trying to meet someone else's expectations or produce a satisfactory result, particularly in artistic endeavors. The exaggerated reaction serves to emphasize the pressure and comic relief found in not meeting self-imposed standards.

title: Search and Destroy.

description.panel1: A pink character asks Google about the chemical composition of a fart, while Google looks annoyed.
description.panel2: Google exclaims in frustration that it has had enough of these kinds of questions and decides to quit.
description.panel3: The pink character looks confused and a bit concerned as Google leaves.
description.panel4: Bing appears from behind a corner and offers to answer the fart-related question, making the pink character look surprised.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts the frustration of Google with trivial or absurd questions, juxtaposed with Bing's eagerness to step in and provide answers to those same questions, highlighting a shift in search engine preferences and attitudes.

title: Step Up.

description.panel1: A snake expresses the desire to go on a journey.
description.panel2: Another snake responds by saying that every journey begins with a single step.
description.panel3: The snake encounters a turtle and looks at it with interest.
description.panel4: The snake is seen riding on the turtle's back, urging it to go faster.

emotion: What the author expresses through this comic is that sometimes, embarking on a journey can take unexpected forms and require creative solutions, often relying on others for assistance.

title: Consumed.

description.panel1: A large pitcher character happily exclaims "OH YEAH!" while holding a smaller pitcher and two figures stand beside it, cheering.
description.panel2: The large pitcher reacts in shock as one of the figures takes the smaller pitcher away.
description.panel3: The two figures start pouring the contents of the smaller pitcher into cups.
description.panel4: The large pitcher cries out "MY BABY!" as the figures drink from the cups, making slurping and gulping sounds.

emotion: The author expressed a humorous yet dark portrayal of the large pitcher's attachment to the smaller pitcher, drawing attention to the emotional reaction when something cherished is consumed by others.

title: Kelly

description.panel1: A girl named Kelly prays, saying "Dear God, it's me, Kelly."
description.panel2: A busy God hears Kelly's prayer and responds with frustration, asking, "Argh. What now?"
description.panel3: God, appearing indifferent, grants Kelly's unspecified wish, saying, "Yeah, sweety. Whatever. Here you go."
description.panel4: A comically enormous cat is seen causing chaos in a city, with the scene being reported on the news.

emotion: The author humorously portrays the idea that God's responses to prayers might sometimes be impulsive or inattentive, leading to unexpected and exaggerated outcomes.

title: Happy End

description.panel1: A masseuse asks a client if they would like a happy end.
description.panel2: The client, lying face down on a massage table, replies affirmatively.
description.panel3: The masseuse agrees and prepares for the next step.
description.panel4: The masseuse reads a fairytale to the client, ending with "and they lived happily ever after," while the client lies with a content expression.

emotion: The author uses humor and a play on words to subvert the expectation of a "happy end" at a massage parlor, implying a wholesome and innocent conclusion instead.

title: Meet the Midwife

description.panel1: A woman asks someone what they do for a living while dining at a table.
description.panel2: The other person responds that they are a midwife and asks the woman what she does.
description.panel3: The woman replies that she does kind of the same job but in the opposite direction.
description.panel4: The person she is talking to is revealed as the Grim Reaper, who simply says "Right."

emotion: The author uses a play on words and dark humor to highlight the contrast between a midwife, who assists in bringing new life into the world, and the Grim Reaper, who is associated with taking life away.

title: Meaningless Existence

description.panel1: A person approaches another person at a desk and asks, "Hey Bob, what are you doing?"
description.panel2: Bob responds, "A number of trivial activities to distract myself from the meaninglessness of my existence."
description.panel3: There is a moment of silence as both people stare at each other.
description.panel4: The person who asked the question says "LOL."

emotion: The author portrays a humorous yet existential dialogue, highlighting how individuals might use trivial activities to cope with or momentarily escape the perceived futility of life, with the interaction ending on a simplistic and somewhat absurd note of laughter.

title: Attraction

description.panel1: A scientist tells a robot that they feel strangely attracted to it today.
description.panel2: The robot excitedly thinks, "Finally, it's happening!"
description.panel3: The scientist walks away, commenting that it's a good thing they didn't build the robot any genitals.
description.panel4: The robot is seen welding and appears to be working on creating genitals.

emotion: The author humorously explores themes of artificial intelligence and human-robot interaction, highlighting the robot's desire for human-like features and the irony of not having those features initially.

title: Cute Baby

description.panel1: A woman on an airplane attempts to comfort a nervous man by telling him not to be scared.
description.panel2: The woman points out a cute baby on the plane, suggesting that a plane with such a cute baby wouldn't crash.
description.panel3: The airplane pilot rushes excitedly down the aisle, responding to the mention of a cute baby.
description.panel4: The airplane is shown flying steadily in the sky.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the idea that the presence of something adorable, like a cute baby, can instill a sense of security to the extent that even the pilot is distracted or captivated by it.

title: the Weather

description.panel1: A rhino asks a giraffe, "Hey, how's the weather up there?"
description.panel2: The giraffe, looking up, responds, "I can't tell... How's the weather up there?" to a bird flying overhead.
description.panel3: The bird, flying at a higher altitude, says, "I can't tell... How's the weather up there?" to a spaceship in the sky.
description.panel4: An astronaut floating in space queries, "How's the weather up there?" but there's no response.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a chain of misunderstandings about the weather, highlighting how perspectives and limits vary at different heights, eventually ending with an astronaut in space where conventional weather is irrelevant.

title: Momma Earth

description.panel1: Earth is depicted with a speech bubble saying, "Oh yeah, I'm gonna cum..."
description.panel2: Earth, still shown in space, has another speech bubble saying, "Momma Earth's gonna cum real hard!"
description.panel3: People are standing around a volcano, seemingly unaware of the impending eruption.
description.panel4: The volcano erupts violently, and the people react to the eruption.

emotion: The comic uses anthropomorphism and humor, likening a volcanic eruption to a human climax, to vividly personify Earth's natural processes in a surprising and humorous manner.

title: Why Hath Thou Forsaken Me

description.panel1: A person kneels in prayer asking, "Oh Lord, why hast thee forsaken me?"
description.panel2: A voice from a cloud responds, "I has't not forsaken thee..."
description.panel3: The voice continues, "I has't cometh unto else yonder thy hither thou."
description.panel4: The person responds with confusion, saying "WTF?".

emotion: The author humorously highlights the confusion between archaic language and modern understanding, depicting a humorous disconnect in the communication between the person and the divine voice.

title: I Know

description.panel1: One person at the table tells the waiter, "We'll have a bottle of your finest wine."
description.panel2: The other person at the table responds, "We can't afford that."
description.panel3: The first person, leaning in close to the other, responds with "I know..."
description.panel4: A large ship similar in appearance to historical luxury liners is depicted, with an iceberg in the water ahead suggesting an impending disaster.

emotion: The author uses humor and visual storytelling to illustrate a person's decision to indulge in luxury despite the known financial risks, cleverly paralleling the situation with the famous maiden voyage of the Titanic, which ended in tragedy due to unforeseen risks.

title: Seesaw

description.panel1: A person sitting on one end of a seesaw wishes for a friend to play with.
description.panel2: Another person arrives and offers to be the friend.
description.panel3: Both people are now sitting on the seesaw together.
description.panel4: The person who just arrived is laughing loudly ("BUAHAHA!").

emotion: The comic humorously highlights the unexpected and sometimes strange interactions that can occur when seeking companionship.

title: When

description.panel1: A dog wonders if humans will ever recognize how great they are.
description.panel2: The dog questions when humans will truly appreciate them.
description.panel3: The dog then asks when humans will return the poop they have taken from them.
description.panel4: The dog sits quietly, seemingly reflecting on their thoughts.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates a dog's perspective, conveying their innocence and misunderstanding of human actions like cleaning up after them, reflecting on the disparity between human intentions and animal perceptions.

title: Modern Art

description.panel1: A man looks at modern art on the wall and says "Ha..."
description.panel2: The man continues looking at the artwork and comments, "My six-year-old could've drawn that."
description.panel3: A woman informs the man, "Sir, your six-year-old did draw that."
description.panel4: The man is forcefully removed from the museum, while someone says, "Get that [expletive] outta here."

emotion: The author uses humor to critique the subjectivity of modern art, highlighting that some pieces may appear simplistic to the viewer and could be mistaken for a child's work, eliciting unexpected reactions.

title: A Doctor

description.panel1: A flight attendant asks if there is a doctor on board.
description.panel2: A man on the plane responds by saying he has a doctor’s degree while casually drinking wine.
description.panel3: The flight attendant urgently calls out that a passenger is bleeding to death.
description.panel4: The man casually clarifies that his degree is in philosophy.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the difference between having a doctoral degree and being a medical doctor, highlighting the potential confusion and lack of practical help in an emergency situation.

title: Girls Night

description.panel1: A witch asks another witch to come over and make some magic punch.
description.panel2: The second witch agrees and mentions she has some old vegetables that will spoil soon.
description.panel3: The first witch, next to a man in a cage, confirms this plan, referring to the man as Alfred.
description.panel4: The two witches celebrate their "Girls' Night" while the man remains in the cage.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a "Girls' Night" with witches involving making magic punch with questionable ingredients and keeping a man trapped in a cage, highlighting the dark and whimsical nature of the witches' activities.

title: Death Flash

description.panel1: A person lying in a hospital bed recounts to another person that "Death flashed before my eyes."
description.panel2: A person stands face-to-face with a hooded figure depicting Death, who is opening their cloak as if flashing.
description.panel3: The person standing refers to the situation as "creepy."
description.panel4: Back in the hospital bed, the first person is being called "so brave" by the second person.

emotion: The comic humorously literalizes the phrase "Death flashed before my eyes," depicting Death comically lifting their cloak instead of the intended metaphorical meaning of near-death experiences, and highlighting the misunderstanding in a comical and lighthearted way.

title: Silent Hill

description.panel1: A person asks, "Rick, can I talk to you for a sec?" in a hilly landscape.
description.panel2: The hilly landscape is shown without any dialogue or characters.
description.panel3: The same hilly landscape is shown with the person asking, "Hellooo?".
description.panel4: The words "SILENT HILL" are shown, indicating the silence in the depicted location.

emotion: The author humorously emphasizes the concept of silence by associating it with the phrase "Silent Hill," showing that the location lives up to its name through the lack of response from the person named Rick.

title: Hang up

description.panel1: One character tells the other, "You hang up!"
description.panel2: The second character responds, "No, you hang up!"
description.panel3: Both characters are shown standing on stools with nooses around their necks, under a tree.
description.panel4: The second character reiterates, "No, you hang up!" while they both remain in the precarious situation.

emotion: The author uses dark humor to exaggerate a common couple's argument about who should end a phone call, extending it to a macabre and literal interpretation of "hanging up." This highlights how minor disputes can sometimes become irrationally prolonged.

title: Born Sexy Yesterday

description.panel1: A scientist introduces a robot to another robot, saying, "We've built you a brand new mate."
description.panel2: The robot responds, "Actually, I prefer more experienced women."
description.panel3: The scientist comments on the new robot, saying, "She's basically a toddler."
description.panel4: The robot argues, "But she has all the newest hardware!", while the new robot appears child-like, causing the scientist to react with, "Oh, professor."

emotion: The author is critiquing the trope often featured in media where female characters are created or introduced as naive and inexperienced yet become romantic interests, highlighting the problematic nature of valuing superficial qualities over experience and maturity.

title: Adam and Steve

description.panel1: Two snails greet each other by saying, "Oh, Adam" and "Oh, Steve."
description.panel2: One snail responds with "Uhh, no homo."
description.panel3: The snails are depicted in silence.
description.panel4: The other snail replies, "Dude, we are hermaphrodites."

emotion: The author humorously addresses the concept of hermaphroditism in snails, which makes the phrase "no homo" irrelevant, thereby also indirectly critiquing the use of this phrase in general.

title: Bad Luck

description.panel1: A black cat named Lord Nibbles is shown having a great day.
description.panel2: Two people see Lord Nibbles and one of them exclaims in fear that black cats bring bad luck.
description.panel3: Lord Nibbles looks sad as he says that the remark has really hurt his feelings.
description.panel4: One of the people is shown falling backward, seemingly into misfortune, while Lord Nibbles walks away nonchalantly.

emotion: The author humorously targets the superstition that black cats bring bad luck, showing that it’s unfounded and even hurtful while playfully suggesting that mistreating or disrespecting the cat will have its consequences.

title: Dog Slaughter House

description.panel1: A man is shocked upon seeing a building labeled "Dog Slaughter House."
description.panel2: The man resolves to take action to address the situation.
description.panel3: The man shakes hands with another individual inside an office as he gains ownership of the premise.
