description.panel4: The man is pleased with the newly renovated and brightly painted building, still labeled "Dog Slaughter House."

emotion: The author might be highlighting the irony that, while external appearances or conditions can change, the core issue (in this case, the troubling function of the "Dog Slaughter House") remains unaddressed.

title: Dinner for Two

description.panel1: A man stands on a rocky shore, looking over the sea at sunset.
description.panel2: The man spots a mermaid sitting on a rock in the sea, and he appears to draw closer to her.
description.panel3: The mermaid gestures invitingly toward the man, as he leans in closer from the shore.
description.panel4: Underwater, two mermaids are smiling and happily sharing a meal with another mermaid who is holding the man's head.

emotion: The author seems to express a darkly humorous warning about the danger of being lured by appearances, showing how the man's approach toward the mermaid resulted in a deadly, ironic twist.

title: My Father Shirt

description.panel1: A man angrily reacts to a waiter spilling something on his shirt.
description.panel2: The man explains that the shirt he is wearing was inherited from his father’s father.
description.panel3: The man emphasizes that the shirt has been passed down for many generations.
description.panel4: The man decides that the shirt is ugly and states that he will gift it to his son.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the irony of keeping an inherited item for generations only to pass it on dismissively due to its unattractive appearance.

title: Dark Secret

description.panel1: A mysterious figure in a top hat and cloak is shown walking at night.
description.panel2: The figure thinks to themselves that no one shall know their dark secret.
description.panel3: The figure appears to approach a black cat.
description.panel4: The figure bends down to pet the cat, which begins to purr.

emotion: The author humorously reveals that the mysterious figure's "dark secret" is simply a love for cats, subverting the reader's expectation of something sinister.

title: Consciousness

description.panel1: The glass is labeled as "half empty."
description.panel2: The glass is labeled as "half full."
description.panel3: The glass is depicted as having gained consciousness.
description.panel4: The glass does not want assumptions to be made about it and is shown walking away.

emotion: The author highlights the subjective nature of perception and encourages avoiding making assumptions based on predefined perspectives.

title: Hell is Just a Sauna

description.panel1: An angel informs God that it's time for His appointment.
description.panel2: The angel hesitantly clarifies that it's with "you know who," implying Satan.
description.panel3: God nonchalantly retrieves His flip-flops, preparing for the visit.
description.panel4: Later, God and Satan are talking in Hell, with Satan complaining about the year and God deflecting responsibility, noting that Satan was created by Himself.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts the annual meeting between God and Satan, showcasing a casual, almost mundane interaction between the two, with an underlying message about responsibility for the creation of evil.

title: Regret Nothing

description.panel1: A person asks Dr. Carter if she regrets not having kids.
description.panel2: Dr. Carter responds by saying she regrets not kicking the person in the shins and then kicks them.
description.panel3: The person, now in pain, points out that Dr. Carter did kick them in the shins.
description.panel4: Dr. Carter replies, "Then I regret nothing," while riding away on a skateboard.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the idea that fulfilling one's desires, even in trivial or impulsive actions, can lead to a life without regrets.

title: I Robot

description.panel1: Two individuals call out to a robot passing by in a park.
description.panel2: The robot looks in their direction, saying "Huh?" while carrying bags of groceries.
description.panel3: The two individuals imitate robotic sounds, saying "BEEP... BOOOP... BEEEP."
description.panel4: The robot sighs and continues walking, carrying the groceries.

emotion: The author demonstrates a scenario where humans mockingly stereotype robots, highlighting a form of insensitivity and the robot's disheartened reaction, subtly urging readers to consider the feelings of others—even those of an artificial nature.

title: Weak Password

description.panel1: The person at the computer receives a message stating "YOUR PASSWORD IS TOO WEAK."
description.panel2: The person becomes visibly frustrated and starts lifting up the keyboard and computer monitor.
description.panel3: The person throws the computer out of a window.
description.panel4: The person leans out the window and says "WHO'S WEAK NOW?"

emotion: The author humorously conveys frustration over being told their password is weak, leading to an exaggerated response of physically throwing the computer out the window to assert dominance over the situation.

title: Insult Machine

description.panel1: One scientist proudly announces the invention of an insult robot to another.
description.panel2: The robot is shown complimenting a woman named Janet, saying she looks stunning today.
description.panel3: The woman and the second scientist look at the robot quietly, with no expression.
description.panel4: The first scientist explains that they are still working on the punchlines for the robot's insults.

emotion: The author humorously depicts a robot designed to insult, ironically delivering compliments instead, highlighting the gap between the robot's intended function and its literal execution, and the ongoing efforts to improve its programming.

title: Satanic Spanking

description.panel1: A person approaches a devilish figure at a podium and says they are there for the satanic spanking.
description.panel2: The devilish figure responds that they don't see the person's name on the list.
description.panel3: The person tries to clarify by mentioning "Beelzebub's booty buffin’," causing confusion.
description.panel4: The devilish figure calls for security and refers to the situation as "The Demonic Ass-Destruction."

emotion: The author humorously depicts a bizarre and exaggerated scenario involving a character misunderstanding a dark, infernal bureaucracy, highlighting the absurdity of the interaction.

title: Hide and Seek

description.panel1: A dog enjoys playing hide and seek.
description.panel2: The dog hides behind a rock, thinking, "Haha..."
description.panel3: The dog confidently believes, "She’ll never find me here."
description.panel4: The dog is easily found by a person.

emotion: The comic humorously portrays the dog's naïve confidence and the simplicity of its hiding spot, leading to it being easily found.

title: Your Side

description.panel1: One spider instructs another spider to stay on their side.
description.panel2: The second spider questions what will happen if they don't comply.
description.panel3: The first spider threatens to release one hundred babies that will feast on the second spider's rotting carcass.
description.panel4: The second spider, now alone, responds with "Geez."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the importance of respecting boundaries and the possible extreme reactions to not doing so.

title: On the Edge Ghost

description.panel1: A ghost named Gary decided to live on the edge.
description.panel2: Gary, being a ghost, was easily able to float near the edge.
description.panel3: Someone tells Gary that they can see his underpants, surprising him.
description.panel4: Gary blushes, showing that ghosts can get embarrassed too.

emotion: The author expresses that even entities like ghosts, typically perceived as emotionless or scary, can experience human-like emotions such as embarrassment, adding a humorous twist to the character of Gary the ghost.

title: Respect

description.panel1: Two people are sitting at a table facing each other.
description.panel2: One person says, "Dear, I've been meaning to tell you something..."
description.panel3: The first person continues, "I respect you." The other person starts to respond, "Oh, thank y—".
description.panel4: The first person interrupts aggressively, saying, "I wasn't finished, you stupid twat!"

emotion: The author uses irony to convey a message about the contradiction between words and actions; despite verbally expressing respect, the aggressive behavior suggests otherwise.

title: Spooning

description.panel1: A fork expresses the desire to be spooned.
description.panel2: A knife offers to fulfill the fork's desire.
description.panel3: The fork and the knife are lying next to each other.
description.panel4: The fork conveys that the experience is not as satisfying.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates that certain emotional experiences and physical affections might feel incomplete or inadequate when not expressed in the desired or expected manner.

title: Lucky

description.panel1: A character excitedly tells Timmy to look at something.
description.panel2: A grasshopper is seen with its head missing, implying an encounter.
description.panel3: The character comments that it looks like someone got lucky last night.
description.panel4: Timmy awkwardly agrees while forcing a laugh.

emotion: The author humorously uses a double entendre about "getting lucky" to refer to the grasshopper's headless state, playing on the fact that female grasshoppers sometimes eat the males during mating.

title: Pretty Sure

description.panel1: A central figure in the group announces that one of them will betray him.
description.panel2: The central figure specifies that the betrayal will involve a sexual act.
description.panel3: A person to the left questions if the betrayal is supposed to be through a kiss.
description.panel4: The central figure confidently reaffirms that the betrayal will be via the sexual act he previously mentioned.

emotion: The author uses unexpected and absurd humor to parody a well-known historical and religious event, creating a surprising and comedic reinterpretation.

title: How to Make Friends

description.panel1: A girl prays by her bedside, asking God to send her a friend.
description.panel2: The girl sits on the floor with a hole in her stomach and a creature emerging from it.
description.panel3: The girl lies on the floor with the creature continuing to emerge from her stomach.
description.panel4: The girl lies on the floor with the creature fully emerged and looking at her.

emotion: The author conveys a dark, humorous twist on the idea of finding friendship, suggesting that the pursuit of companionship can sometimes lead to unexpected and bizarre outcomes.

title: Falling in Love

description.panel1: Two snowflakes with happy expressions are floating in the air.
description.panel2: The two snowflakes appear to be drifting closer to each other, one looking happy and the other looking slightly apprehensive.
description.panel3: One snowflake lands on a person's finger while the other snowflake looks alarmed and upset.
description.panel4: A person is seen standing in the snow looking upward, seemingly unaware of the snowflake situation.

emotion: The author illustrates the fleeting and uncontrollable nature of love, represented by the snowflakes' brief interaction before being separated by external circumstances.

title: Meditation

description.panel1: One person asks Bob a question, interrupting him.
description.panel2: Bob tells the person to be quiet because he is trying to meditate.
description.panel3: Bob reflects on his recent behavior, admitting he has been somewhat aggressive.
description.panel4: The other person, now knocked to the ground, agrees with Bob's assessment.

emotion: The author uses humor to highlight the irony of trying to find peace and calm through meditation while recognizing and dealing with one's aggressive tendencies.

title: True Killer

description.panel1: A character in a sewer grate whispers, "PSST, HEY KID" to a child standing above.
description.panel2: The character in the grate says, "DON'T WORRY, I'M NOT GONNA KILL YA."
description.panel3: The character in the grate continues, "BUT YA KNOW WHAT IS GONNA KILL YA?"
description.panel4: The comic reveals the punchline as the character in the grate says, "THOSE UNTIED SHOELACES," while the child runs away with untied shoelaces.

emotion: The author uses a humorous juxtaposition of a supposedly threatening situation with an unexpected and mundane warning to highlight the absurdity of everyday dangers compared to imagined fears.

title: Tree Hugger

description.panel1: A person carrying two bags walks past a tree and says, "Haha, nice hemp bags!"
description.panel2: The person hears a sound and wonders, "What was that sound?"
description.panel3: The person stands in front of the tree and says, "I feel strangely connected to that tree."
description.panel4: The person hugs the tree, shoes falling off, and says, "Ewww."

emotion: The author humorously expresses the irony of someone having an instant, overwhelming connection to a tree due to their use of hemp products, leading to an awkward moment of hugging the tree and feeling discomfort.

title: Conspiracy

description.panel1: A standing person asks a seated person, "Hey, how are you?"
description.panel2: The seated person responds, "Not good. I just fell off a horse."
description.panel3: The standing person replies, "I fell off this chair earlier."
description.panel4: A horse is seen stealthily putting the chair back in place, implying the horse moved the chair to cause the fall.

emotion: The author highlights the irony and humor in seemingly minor misfortunes by linking a dramatic fall from a horse to an equally dramatic fall from a simple chair, suggesting a playful conspiracy by the horse.

title: Robbery Funeral Birth

description.panel1: Someone is pointing a gun at another person, declaring it as a robbery.
description.panel2: A third person challenges the situation, asserting that it is a funeral.
description.panel3: A fourth individual disagrees, claiming that it is actually a birth.
description.panel4: The fourth individual pulls out a doll-like figure from under their clothes, emphasizing the birth assertion.

emotion: The author seems to be playing with the absurdity and surrealism of unexpected, incongruent events happening simultaneously, highlighting the diversity of perspectives and questioning the certainty of any given situation.

title: Taking Orders

description.panel1: A dog tied to a leash expresses frustration about always taking orders.
description.panel2: The dog decides to become a human from now on in order to stop taking orders.
description.panel3: The dog is shown as a human, wearing a tie and sitting at a desk with a computer.
description.panel4: The dog, now human and working at an office desk, sarcastically comments that the new situation is "way better."

emotion: The author humorously highlights the irony of a dog wanting to stop taking orders by becoming a human, only to end up in an office job where taking orders is still a daily reality, thereby suggesting that both humans and dogs face similar constraints.

title: Hi Earth

description.panel1: A character offscreen asks Earth how it is doing.
description.panel2: Earth looks pensive and hesitates to respond.
description.panel3: Earth reassures that it will be fine.
description.panel4: Earth expresses uncertainty about the wellbeing of others.

emotion: The author conveys a sense of environmental awareness, suggesting that while the planet itself will endure, human activities might severely impact the future of humanity and other species.

title: New Years Angst

description.panel1: A rocket is flying through the air with a speech bubble saying, "I’m not ready to die."
description.panel2: The rocket, still in the air, seems confused and says, "Wait, what?"
description.panel3: The rocket has landed on the ground and exclaims, "I’m alive!"
description.panel4: A dog walks up to the rocket, and it seems the rocket’s initial relief may be short-lived.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the rocket's fear of its anticipated demise, its relief at unexpectedly surviving, and the immediate realization that new dangers can arise even after overcoming initial fears. This evokes a broader theme of uncertainty and the irony of unexpected challenges.

title: Trees with Attitude

description.panel1: A tree tells Janet that she is the uncoolest among them.
description.panel2: Janet explains that it is because Greg threw away her cool jeggings.
description.panel3: The tree concurs with Janet and tells Greg that he has to leave the gang.
description.panel4: Greg, portrayed as a bush with sunglasses, is seen walking away sadly.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates themes of social dynamics and the arbitrary nature of "coolness" within a group, using anthropomorphic plants to convey the story.

title: Mad Cherry

description.panel1: One cherry asks the other if they are still mad at them.
description.panel2: The other cherry asks for forgiveness, saying that life is short.
description.panel3: The second cherry reflects on the uncertainty of how much time they have left on Earth.
description.panel4: The cherries are seen garnishing a sundae, humorously implying their fate as toppings.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the theme of mortality and the triviality of grudges by showing cherries discussing life and forgiveness before being used as sundae toppings.

title: Going Out

description.panel1: A person sitting on a chair asks, "Bob, are you ready?"
description.panel2: Bob, who is wearing a dress and a false mustache, answers, "I'm ready."
description.panel3: The person on the chair tells Bob, "You can't go out like that."
description.panel4: The person approaches Bob and states, "Your moustache is unkempt," while adjusting the mustache.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the prioritization of minor details (like an unkempt mustache) over more apparent, unconventional issues (such as the man dressed in a dress) when getting ready to go out.

title: If Jesus was a Mean Girl

description.panel1: The title of the comic "If Jesus Was a Mean Girl" is displayed on a pink background with a banner.
description.panel2: Jesus is seen in a boat saying, "Get in loser. We're going to go turn water to wine."
description.panel3: Jesus is reclining and says, "I don't think my father, the inventor of everything would be happy to hear about this."
description.panel4: Jesus on the cross asks, "Ew, why are you so obsessed with me?" while people are seen lamenting below.

emotion: The author uses humor and irony to reimagine Jesus as a character from the movie "Mean Girls," blending biblical references with modern mean-spirited dialogue to create a satirical and humorous take on both the movie and religious narratives.

title: Z Day

description.panel1: A person expresses a desire to be cremated after death.
description.panel2: The same person elaborates, saying they want their ashes to be buried so a beautiful tree can grow from them.
description.panel3: The other person rejects the idea, preferring to be part of a zombie apocalypse instead of becoming a tree.
description.panel4: In a "later" scene, the person who wanted to be a zombie is shown as a zombie, encountering the tree that grew from the ashes.

emotion: The author humorously contrasts differing post-mortem wishes, culminating in an ironic twist where the zombie encounters the tree, suggesting unexpected outcomes to our post-life desires.

title: Lives Many

description.panel1: A witch, using a crystal ball, foresees doom and suffering ahead while speaking to a black cat.
description.panel2: The witch rests her hand on her face, looking annoyed or disheartened as the black cat sits across from her.
description.panel3: The witch loudly calls for the next crystal ball reading.
description.panel4: The witch sits in front of a table lined with multiple crystal balls, sighing in frustration or exhaustion.

emotion: The author illustrates the monotonous and seemingly endless task of predicting a grim future repetitively, highlighting the witch's exasperation with her tedious job.

title: Sit Down

description.panel1: A doctor asks Mr. Johnson to have a seat as she needs to talk to him.
description.panel2: Mr. Johnson refuses to sit, stating he knows the doctor's trick.
description.panel3: Mr. Johnson explains that if he sits down, the doctor will tell him he is going to die, so he prefers to stand.
description.panel4: The doctor thinks to herself that Mr. Johnson is clever.

emotion: The author expresses a humorous scenario where a patient outsmarts the doctor by refusing to sit down in anticipation of bad news, highlighting the patient's suspicion and wit.

title: Whoa

description.panel1: A spaceship flying in space with Earth visible in the background and a speech bubble saying "Whoa."
description.panel2: A UFO piloted by an alien flying next to the spaceship with both saying "Whoa."
description.panel3: A collection of objects, including a UFO, a triangular eye symbol, Earth, and planets, all saying "Whoa."
description.panel4: A person sitting at a desk looking at a comic strip and saying "Whoa."

emotion: The author expresses a sense of amazement and wonder, highlighting how various beings and objects in the universe, as well as the person reading the comic, all react with the same sense of awe.

title: Ancient Egyptian Age

description.panel1: A girl dressed as a witch is asking her cat, Lord Nibbles, to do something funny for their YouTube channel.
description.panel2: The cat, Lord Nibbles, expresses his disdain for amusing the YouTube audience.
description.panel3: The girl tries to bribe the cat with a promise of tuna from the fridge.
description.panel4: The cat, thinking about ancient times, obliges by playing a keyboard while the girl records, missing the reverence he received in Ancient Egypt.

emotion: The author illustrates the humorous dynamic between modern social media culture and the perceived dignity of cats, suggesting that cats might feel they were more respected in ancient times compared to their entertainment role today.

title: CEO

description.panel1: A worker on a factory floor thinks, "I wish this job paid better."
description.panel2: An office employee working at a desk thinks, "I wish this job paid better."
description.panel3: A person looking outside a high-rise building’s window thinks, "I wish this job paid better."
description.panel4: A CEO sitting in their office confidently smiles.

emotion: The comic conveys the idea that regardless of one's position within a company, people commonly wish for better pay, indicating a universal desire for financial improvement across all job levels.

title: Life of the Party

description.panel1: A clown enthusiastically tells kids they can achieve anything they set their minds to.
description.panel2: The clown reveals his personal struggles, mentioning that his ex-wife calls him an embarrassment and won’t let him see his children.
description.panel3: The clown appears dejected and exhausted, sitting on a large cake, with the kids looking bored.
description.panel4: Two adults, away from the scene, comment about how hiring the clown was worth it because of the quiet it brought.

emotion: The author uses humor and irony to depict the gap between inspirational messages and the harsh realities of life, highlighting how desperate situations can lead to unexpected forms of "success."

title: Boa Constrictor

description.panel1: A zookeeper introduces the boa constrictor to a group of children, saying, "Now, class. This is a boa constrictor."
description.panel2: The zookeeper reassures a frightened child that the boa constrictor will not bite, stating, "Don't worry, son. It's not going to bite."
description.panel3: The zookeeper reiterates to the children that the constrictor isn’t biting, showing the snake wrapped harmlessly around his arm and stating, "As you can see, the constrictor isn't biting."
description.panel4: The zookeeper clarifies, somewhat ominously, that the boa constrictor kills its prey by choking, saying, "It kills its prey by choking!"

emotion: The author uses humor to highlight the unexpected twist in the zookeeper's reassurance, pointing out the unsettling way a boa constrictor actually subdues its prey as opposed to the benign scenario the children might have imagined.

title: Merry Spankmas

description.panel1: The image portrays a festive house decorated with lights, depicting a peaceful and cozy Christmas time.
description.panel2: The scene shifts inside the house, showing people enjoying a serene and reflective holiday moment by the fireplace.
description.panel3: Suddenly, the comic introduces a humorous twist with a muscular Santa Claus character preparing to go down a chimney, explicitly depicted in a silly manner.
description.panel4: The final panel returns to the tranquil outdoor scene, emphasizing the serene and quiet nature of the season.

emotion: The author juxtaposes the traditional serene and cozy imagery of Christmas with an unexpected and humorous take on Santa Claus, highlighting the contrast between the idealized calm of the holiday season and the surprising, playful elements that can emerge.

title: Red Flags

description.panel1: Illustration of soldiers attacking a castle.
description.panel2: A panicked soldier informs a seated lady about the approaching enemy and asks for instructions.
description.panel3: The lady commands her sister to show the red flags.
description.panel4: The castle displays red flags, prompting the attacking soldiers to retreat.

emotion: The comic humorously shows how the symbolic display of "red flags" (typically indicating danger or warning) is used in a medieval context to repel the attackers, analogous to how red flags serve as warnings in modern contexts.

title: Thanksz

description.panel1: One pigeon asks another pigeon if it ever wonders where all the crumbs come from.
description.panel2: The other pigeon responds that humans drop crumbs all the time.
description.panel3: The first pigeon proposes that they show their thanks.
description.panel4: The pigeons are seen dropping feces all over parked cars as their way of showing thanks.

emotion: The author humorously depicts pigeons' means of "thanking" humans in an unexpected and ironic manner.

title: Abduction

description.panel1: A character named Jerry is being abducted by a UFO.
description.panel2: Two people observe the abduction and comment on it.
description.panel3: The observers express relief and mutual dislike for Jerry.
description.panel4: Upon arriving inside the UFO, Jerry is welcomed warmly, suggesting he is valued there.

emotion: The author humorously expresses how someone who is unappreciated or disliked in one context can be highly valued in another, emphasizing the relativity of worth and acceptance.

title: Fucking Cars

description.panel1: A person reacts negatively to something on their computer, insisting on "no fucking in cars".
description.panel2: The person clarifies their desire for material about "fucking cars".
description.panel3: The scene changes to show two cars in an exaggerated depiction of intercourse, complete with "Honk Honk" sounds.
description.panel4: The person is satisfied with the new content, saying "That's the stuff".

emotion: The comic uses wordplay and visual humor to highlight the difference between two similar phrases that have very different meanings, ultimately illustrating the person's specific interest in cars in an anthropomorphic context.

title: The Voice

description.panel1: The child tells their parents there is a monster under their bed.
description.panel2: The child describes the monster's screaming as being like "the voice of a thousand haunted souls."
description.panel3: The monster appears and explains that the child’s loud behavior is disturbing its sleep.
description.panel4: The monster advises the child to get their act together.

emotion: The author comically portrays the concept of a child’s disruptive nighttime behavior, humorously depicting how it affects not only the parents but also the mythical monster under the bed, turning the usual fear trope on its head by making the monster more annoyed than frightening.

title: Still Dead

description.panel1: The Grim Reaper approaches a house, and the person at the door asks, "Oh, hi Jim, is that you?"
description.panel2: The Grim Reaper replies, "Um, no. You're dead."
description.panel3: The person laughs and says, "Haha, good one. I see you, Larry. No wait, Janet?"
description.panel4: The Grim Reaper, looking puzzled and tired, says, "Hmm, Michael?" and then remarks, "This always takes forever on Halloween."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the confusion caused by costumes on Halloween, even to the Grim Reaper, who has a hard time identifying people correctly on this holiday.

title: Tree Hugger

description.panel1: A person is seen walking toward a tree in an open field.
description.panel2: The person starts hugging the tree.
description.panel3: The tree has a thought bubble saying, "This isn't helping anything."
description.panel4: The tree has another thought bubble following with, "But it feels good."

emotion: The author expresses the idea that while certain actions may not have a practical impact, they can still provide emotional comfort or a sense of well-being.

title: One of Yours

description.panel1: A witch is sitting on a park bench, watching children play.
description.panel2: A person approaches the witch and asks if any of the kids are hers.
description.panel3: The witch responds by saying "Nope."
description.panel4: The witch adds, "But it will be soon," implying she intends to take one of the children by using her ability, hinting at a mischievous or sinister plan.

emotion: The author uses humor and an unexpected twist to play on the common trope of witches being child-snatchers, combining it with a casual park setting to create a darkly comedic situation.

title: The Lorax

description.panel1: The Lorax introduces himself, indicating he speaks for the trees and threatening to break the person's knees if they continue their harmful actions.
description.panel2: The Lorax aggressively warns the person he will attack them physically to make them stop.
description.panel3: The Lorax emphasizes the consequences of ignoring his warning, demanding that nature be given a chance.
description.panel4: The Lorax warns that the person's future is bleak if they don't change their ways, stating that Mother Nature will prevail.

emotion: The author uses the character of the Lorax to convey a strong and urgent message about the importance of protecting the environment, highlighting the severe consequences of continuing harmful practices towards nature.

title: Vibration Mode

description.panel1: A witch and a man are standing outside a building, and the witch says she had a great time tonight.
description.panel2: The man asks if he can walk the witch home, and she declines, saying she has her broom with her.
description.panel3: The witch turns on the "Vibration" mode on her broom.
description.panel4: The witch flies away on her broom joyfully, saying "WEEEEE."

emotion: The author humorously expresses the independence of the witch character by showing how she prefers to use her magical broom—a unique, convenient mode of transportation—over accepting a traditional offer of being walked home.

title: Spank Me

description.panel1: A robot in a maid's outfit watches as a professor dismissively walks away after dropping something on the floor.
description.panel2: The robot enthusiastically announces to the professor its ability to clean, while holding a feather duster.
description.panel3: The robot accidentally knocks over and breaks a vase while attempting to demonstrate its cleaning skills.
description.panel4: The robot sheepishly asks the professor if it should be spanked as it points to the broken vase.

emotion: The author illustrates a humorous scenario in which a well-meaning but clumsy robot tries to show off its abilities, only to cause an unintended mess, and then innocently asks for punishment, highlighting both the robot's childlike innocence and the unexpected consequences of its actions.

title: Kids in the Streets

description.panel1: An older person looks out of a window and starts reminiscing with the phrase "Back in my day..."
description.panel2: The older person continues, stating that there were more kids playing out in the streets.
description.panel3: The older person notes that now, kids are all at home playing video games.
description.panel4: The punchline reveals the speaker's inappropriate conclusion, "Makes it hard for a pedophile."

emotion: The comic uses dark humor to comment on how the increased indoor activities of children (playing video games) create fewer opportunities for harmful individuals lurking outside, contrasting nostalgic memories with a disturbing reality.

title: Comment Section

description.panel1: The witch is flying daringly on her broomstick during the night.
description.panel2: The witch looks at her phone and notices that her latest YouTube video has received a lot of views.
description.panel3: The witch realizes that her video has also received a lot of comments.
description.panel4: The witch throws her phone away, indicating that she finds dealing with the comments too challenging.

emotion: The author is expressing that, while chasing views and attention online may seem daring and exciting, the potentially overwhelming and negative nature of the comment section can drive people away, even those who seem brave.

title: Where the Corpses Go

description.panel1: The character is lying on the ground next to a gravestone, remarking, "So this is what being a corpse feels like."
description.panel2: The same character continues lying there, saying, "Not as fun as I thought it’d be."
description.panel3: The character remains in the same position, quietly observing their surroundings.
description.panel4: The character exclaims, "Yay, free WiFi."

emotion: The author uses humor to illustrate a situation where a normally somber experience (being a corpse) is made amusing by contrasting it with a modern, mundane delight (finding free WiFi). This comic plays with the unexpected and juxtaposes death with contemporary trivial pleasures, emphasizing the all-pervasiveness of technology in our lives.

title: Sponsored

description.panel1: A man is confessing to a priest, saying "Forgive me, Father, for I have sinned."
description.panel2: The man continues his confession, expressing sorrow for hurting his friends and family.
description.panel3: The priest suggests to the man, "Have you tried eating a Snickers?"
description.panel4: The outside of the church is shown with a large billboard on its roof that reads "Sponsored by Snickers."

emotion: The comic humorously critiques the commercialization and sponsorship infiltrating various aspects of life, even serious and sacred spaces like a church confession.

title: WWJD

description.panel1: A person with a halo is walking outdoors.
description.panel2: The person with a halo sees another person holding a sign that says "GOD HATES FAGS."
description.panel3: The person with a halo continues to walk and sees another person holding signs that say "SLUTTY WOMEN BURN IN HELL" and "NO CONDOMS!"
description.panel4: The person with a halo approaches a table with a sign that says "LEAVING THE CHURCH" and asks, "WHERE DO I SIGN?"

emotion: The author is expressing criticism of the hateful and judgmental attitudes sometimes associated with religious institutions, suggesting that such attitudes can drive people away from the church.

title: Officer McSexy

description.panel1: Officer McSexy is shown on patrol, walking down the street.
description.panel2: A woman runs up to Officer McSexy, frantically explaining that her baby is trapped under something burning.
description.panel3: Officer McSexy dismisses the woman's concern about her baby, making a comment about it being too early to talk about babies.
description.panel4: The woman is attempting to rescue the baby from a burning scene while Officer McSexy walks away, showing no concern.

emotion: The author uses dark humor to critique the character's inattentiveness and lack of responsibility in a dire situation where help is critically needed. The comic contrasts societal expectations of a policeman's role with the character's inappropriate priorities and nonchalance.

title: Last Word

description.panel1: The Grim Reaper asks a man if he has any last words.
description.panel2: The man questions if it should be "words" in plural form, but the Grim Reaper insists on just one word due to a tight schedule.
description.panel3: The man begins to think about what his final word will be.
description.panel4: The man decides and says, "BOOBS."

emotion: The author uses humor to highlight the absurdity and triviality of the man's choice for his last word in the face of death.

title: A Few Drinks

description.panel1: A seated woman asks the man at the kitchen counter about his plans for Halloween.
description.panel2: The man responds that he plans to have a few drinks and party a little.
description.panel3: The woman clarifies, questioning if he means drinking people's blood and playing puppet with their lifeless bodies.
description.panel4: The man confirms her clarification casually, and the woman reacts nonchalantly.

emotion: The author humorously juxtaposes the normalcy of casual Halloween plans with the macabre actions of the characters, highlighting their nonchalant attitude towards gruesome activities.

title: Ghost Problems

description.panel1: A person complains about getting stains on their costume.
description.panel2: The person continues to complain about everyday annoyances, mentioning sheets getting stuck in underpants after peeing.
description.panel3: A ghost responds by lamenting the eternal solitude and the suffering of watching loved ones continue their lives without them.
description.panel4: The person looks confused and responds with "Wait... what?"

emotion: The comic contrasts the trivial annoyances of everyday life with the profound and existential suffering of a ghost, highlighting the disparity in their concerns in a humorous and absurd manner.

title: Allergies

description.panel1: An alien boasts that their species is superior to humans in every way.
description.panel2: The alien explains that their species has no concept of gender and reproduce by sneezing.
description.panel3: The alien adds that life is difficult for those with allergies.
description.panel4: The final panel shows multiple small aliens, presumably reproductions caused by sneezing.

emotion: The author humorously depicts an alien species with superior qualities facing an unexpected issue, highlighting that even advanced beings can have simple and relatable problems like allergies.

title: Comic Strip

description.panel1: A majestic mountain stands tall under a clear blue sky with the caption, "What a magnificent mountain."
description.panel2: A bear stands proudly on top of the mountain with the caption, "What a magnificent bear on top of the mountain."
description.panel3: A spider is seen standing on the bear's head with the caption, "What a magnificent spider on top of the bear."
description.panel4: The spider has a thought bubble saying, "I am the shit."

emotion: The author is humorously illustrating the theme of relative perspective and self-importance, where each character considers themselves more magnificent based on their position in the hierarchy, culminating in the spider's exaggerated sense of superiority.

title: Unrelatable

description.panel1: A person explains to what appears to be an angel at the gates of heaven that the movie they just saw was very bad.
description.panel2: The person elaborates that the protagonist of the movie was annoying and ruined everything in his life.
description.panel3: The person continues to critique the movie, citing the protagonist's failed marriage, career, and a ridiculous car accident at the end.
description.panel4: The angel pronounces the movie "totally unrelatable" as the person walks away.

emotion: The author expresses humor through the irony of a person in the afterlife, presumably reflecting on their own troubled life, criticizing a movie character for having similar struggles, while an angel humorously finds it unrelatable, highlighting a humorous disconnect between fictional and real-life troubles.

title: Psycho Therapy

description.panel1: A patient expresses to the therapist that they have been feeling very stressed at work lately.
description.panel2: The therapist suggests that the stress might be related to a subconscious desire to sleep with the patient's mother.
description.panel3: The patient reacts with shock, exclaiming "What the hell!"
description.panel4: The patient questions the therapist, asking if they have been reading their diary.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the sometimes absurd and far-fetched interpretations therapists can make about a person's issues, poking fun at the idea of overanalyzing subconscious desires.

title: Check Please

description.panel1: A person admits to a robot that they thought the robot was a human when they agreed to the date.
description.panel2: The robot replies that it is a good listener.
description.panel3: The robot mentions that it has multiple body parts that can vibrate.
description.panel4: The person raises their hand and asks for the check.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the misunderstanding and awkwardness in a date between a human and a robot, illustrating the unexpected and comical differences in expectations and communication.

title: Wedding Day

description.panel1: The couple is at the altar getting married as the officiant asks if anyone objects to the marriage.
description.panel2: The groom notices a man in the distance and asks about him.
description.panel3: The bride dismissively mentions that the man is her ex-boyfriend, Tod.
description.panel4: Despite Tod's dramatic gesture in the background, the officiant states there are no objections and pronounces the couple husband and wife.

emotion: What the author expresses through this comic: The author humorously portrays a typical wedding scenario, highlighting the irony and discomfort of the ex-boyfriend's presence and exaggerated reaction contrasted with the casual dismissal by the bride, ultimately leading to the uninterrupted wedding ceremony.

title: Bat Cave

description.panel1: A doctor or dentist is about to examine a female patient who is wearing a witch’s hat.
description.panel2: The doctor is startled by a bat flying out from the patient's clothing.
description.panel3: The doctor comments that it must have been a long time.
description.panel4: The woman explains that the bat was actually her boyfriend.

emotion: The comic uses a humorous and unexpected situation to play on the witch stereotype, with a twist that involves the misunderstanding of the doctor's reaction to an unusual occurrence.

title: My Prince

description.panel1: A knight exclaims that he has come to free the princess, standing near a tall tower.
description.panel2: The princess responds from the tower, saying that she now identifies as a prince.
description.panel3: The knight, undeterred, expresses that he still loves the person regardless of their identity.
description.panel4: The knight carries the newly identified prince, climbing down the tower, and compliments their strength; the prince thanks the knight.

emotion: The author expresses themes of acceptance and love transcending traditional gender roles, showcasing an unwavering affection regardless of gender identity.

title: Efficiency Expert

description.panel1: A man asks another man sitting at a desk what he is doing, and the man replies that he is writing a report on how to be more efficient.
description.panel2: The standing man reads a regulation that states all employees must write efficiency reports.
description.panel3: The standing man declares that the report-writing task is stupid.
description.panel4: The seated man looks disappointed and says that he spent two weeks writing the report.

emotion: The author is highlighting the irony and inefficiency of workplace regulations that require extensive effort to complete tasks intended to improve efficiency, potentially illustrating the counterproductive nature of bureaucratic processes.

title: The Highway

description.panel1: A man tells a woman, "My way or the highway," in a confrontational manner.
description.panel2: The woman decides to walk away and chooses the highway.
description.panel3: The highway speaks to her kindly, saying, "You relax, honey," showing respect and care.
description.panel4: The man from the first panel, standing a distance away, admits, "I'll do the dishes."

emotion: The author humorously conveys that choosing self-respect and autonomy can lead to better treatment and outcomes, even in difficult situations.

title: Is This Seat Taken

description.panel1: A person approaches another who is seated at a table and asks, "Excuse me, is this seat taken?"
description.panel2: The seated individual responds with a simple "No."
description.panel3: The first person laughs and calls the seated individual a "loser."
description.panel4: The person who called the other a loser sits down next to them and casually asks, "So, how was your weekend?"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the paradox of social interactions where people might mock or insult each other casually but still maintain a normal relationship, suggesting a deeper commentary on the complexities and absurdities of human relationships and social norms.
